title: The Locksmith's Afternoon
text_zh: 小区门口有一个修锁的老人，无论刮风还是下雨，他都坐在那里。他把工具整整齐齐地摆在一块布上，像在准备一场小小的展览。大部分时间没有客人，他就安静地坐着，看来来去去的人。有时候一个下午只有一两个人来配钥匙，但他从来不着急。他说：「只要有人需要我，我就不会离开。」邻居们已经习惯了他的存在，就像习惯了门口那棵老树一样。偶尔有小孩跑过来看他工作，他就把钥匙的形状一个一个地讲给他们听。那些安静的下午，他和这个小区一起慢慢变老。
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū ménkǒu yǒu yí gè xiū suǒ de lǎorén, wúlùn guā fēng háishi xià yǔ, tā dōu zuò zài nàlǐ. Tā bǎ gōngjù zhěngzhěngqíqí de bǎi zài yí kuài bù shàng, xiàng zài zhǔnbèi yì chǎng xiǎoxiǎo de zhǎnlǎn. Dà bùfen shíjiān méiyǒu kèrén, tā jiù ānjìng de zuòzhe, kàn láilái-qùqù de rén. Yǒu shíhou yí gè xiàwǔ zhǐ yǒu yī liǎng gè rén lái pèi yàoshi, dàn tā cónglái bù zhāojí. Tā shuō: 「Zhǐyào yǒu rén xūyào wǒ, wǒ jiù bú huì líkāi.」 Línjūmen yǐjīng xíguàn le tā de cúnzài, jiù xiàng xíguàn le ménkǒu nà kē lǎo shù yíyàng. Ǒu'ěr yǒu xiǎohái pǎo guòlái kàn tā gōngzuò, tā jiù bǎ yàoshi de xíngzhuàng yí gè yí gè de jiǎng gěi tāmen tīng. Nàxiē ānjìng de xiàwǔ, tā hé zhège xiǎoqū yìqǐ mànmàn biàn lǎo.
text_en: At the entrance of the residential compound, there's an old locksmith who sits there no matter whether the wind blows or the rain falls. He lays out his tools neatly on a piece of cloth, as if preparing a small exhibition. Most of the time there are no customers, so he sits quietly watching people come and go. Sometimes only one or two people come to copy keys in an entire afternoon, but he's never in a hurry. He says: 'As long as someone needs me, I won't leave.' The neighbors have gotten used to his presence, just like they've gotten used to the old tree by the gate. Occasionally children run over to watch him work, and he explains the shape of each key to them one by one. On those quiet afternoons, he grows old together with this compound.

questions.q_zh: 老人为什么喜欢这个地方？
questions.q_en: Why might the old man like this place?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhèlǐ yǒu hěn duō rén
options.text_en: because there are many people here

questions.explanation: The reason hides in plain sight: "On those quiet afternoons, he grows old together."

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以推断作者的什么立场？
questions.q_en: What stance of the author can be inferred from this passage?

options.text: 欣赏生活中的简单之美
options.pinyin: xīnshǎng shēnghuó zhōng de jiǎndān zhī měi

questions.explanation: Details like "At the entrance of the residential compound, there's an old" accumulate into this larger meaning.

questions.q_zh: 邻居们觉得老人像什么？
questions.q_en: What do the neighbors compare the old man to?

options.text_en: the old tree by the gate

questions.explanation: The comparison is spelled out: "As long as someone needs me, I won't leave."

title: The Last Bus
text_zh: 晚上十点半，最后一班公交车到站了。车上只剩下三四个人，每个人都靠着窗户，看着外面的夜景。司机把车开得很慢，好像不着急到终点站。一个穿校服的女生在看手机，屏幕的光照在她疲倦的脸上。一个中年男人抱着一束花，花已经有一点蔫了。不但车里很安静，而且车外的街道也几乎没有人了。这辆车就像一条在城市里游泳的鱼，慢慢地把每个人送回自己的生活里。我喜欢坐最后一班车，因为那时候，整个城市好像只属于我一个人。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shí diǎn bàn, zuìhòu yì bān gōngjiāo chē dào zhàn le. Chē shàng zhǐ shèng xià sān sì gè rén, měi gè rén dōu kàozhe chuānghu, kànzhe wàimiàn de yèjǐng. Sījī bǎ chē kāi de hěn màn, hǎoxiàng bù zhāojí dào zhōngdiǎn zhàn. Yí gè chuān xiàofú de nǚshēng zài kàn shǒujī, píngmù de guāng zhào zài tā píjuàn de liǎn shàng. Yí gè zhōngnián nánrén bàozhe yí shù huā, huā yǐjīng yǒu yìdiǎn niān le. Búdàn chē lǐ hěn ānjìng, érqiě chē wài de jiēdào yě jīhū méiyǒu rén le. Zhè liàng chē jiù xiàng yì tiáo zài chéngshì lǐ yóuyǒng de yú, mànmàn de bǎ měi gè rén sòng huí zìjǐ de shēnghuó lǐ. Wǒ xǐhuan zuò zuìhòu yì bān chē, yīnwèi nà shíhou, zhěnggè chéngshì hǎoxiàng zhǐ shǔyú wǒ yí gè rén.
text_en: At ten-thirty at night, the last bus arrives at the stop. Only three or four people remain on the bus, each leaning against a window, watching the night scenery outside. The driver drives slowly, as if in no hurry to reach the final stop. A girl in a school uniform looks at her phone, the screen light shining on her tired face. A middle-aged man holds a bouquet of flowers — the flowers have already wilted a little. Not only is the bus quiet, but the streets outside are nearly empty too. This bus is like a fish swimming through the city, slowly delivering each person back to their own life. I like taking the last bus, because at that hour, the whole city seems to belong to me alone.

questions.q_en: Why does the driver drive slowly?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi lù shàng chē hěn duō
options.text_en: because there's a lot of traffic

options.text_en: he seems in no hurry to reach the final stop

options.text_en: because someone wants to get off

questions.q_en: What role does the 'white space' (what's unsaid) play in this passage?

options.text_en: gives the reader room to imagine

options.text: 说明作者写作能力不足
options.text_en: shows the author's lack of skill

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么喜欢坐最后一班车？

options.text: 因为车上人少比较舒服
options.pinyin: yīnwèi chē shàng rén shǎo bǐjiào shūfu
options.text_en: because fewer people is more comfortable

options.text: 因为那时候整个城市好像只属于自己
options.pinyin: yīnwèi nà shíhou zhěnggè chéngshì hǎoxiàng zhǐ shǔyú zìjǐ
options.text_en: the whole city seems to belong to oneself

options.pinyin: yīnwèi sījī kāi de hěn màn

questions.explanation: Look at "I like taking the last bus, because at that hour" — the reason is right there.

title: Rain Through the Café Window
text_zh: 下午三点，外面突然下起了大雨。咖啡馆里的人都把目光转向窗外，好像雨是一场免费的电影。有人把伞忘在了外面，被雨淋湿了。一个服务员跑出去，把那把伞拿了进来。角落里有一对年轻人，他们一直在小声说话，完全没注意到下雨了。吧台旁边坐着一个女人，她一个人喝着咖啡，用手指在玻璃上画着什么。雨越下越大，但是没有人想离开。只要外面还在下雨，这里就是全城最温暖的地方。我点了第二杯咖啡，决定等雨停了再走。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ sān diǎn, wàimiàn tūrán xià qǐ le dà yǔ. Kāfēiguǎn lǐ de rén dōu bǎ mùguāng zhuǎn xiàng chuāng wài, hǎoxiàng yǔ shì yì chǎng miǎnfèi de diànyǐng. Yǒu rén bǎ sǎn wàng zài le wàimiàn, bèi yǔ lín shī le. Yí gè fúwùyuán pǎo chūqù, bǎ nà bǎ sǎn ná le jìnlái. Jiǎoluò lǐ yǒu yí duì niánqīngrén, tāmen yìzhí zài xiǎo shēng shuō huà, wánquán méi zhùyì dào xià yǔ le. Bātái pángbiān zuòzhe yí gè nǚrén, tā yí gè rén hēzhe kāfēi, yòng shǒuzhǐ zài bōli shàng huàzhe shénme. Yǔ yuè xià yuè dà, dànshì méiyǒu rén xiǎng líkāi. Zhǐyào wàimiàn hái zài xià yǔ, zhèlǐ jiù shì quán chéng zuì wēnnuǎn de dìfang. Wǒ diǎn le dì èr bēi kāfēi, juédìng děng yǔ tíng le zài zǒu.
text_en: At three in the afternoon, it suddenly starts raining heavily. Everyone in the café turns their gaze toward the window, as if the rain were a free movie. Someone left their umbrella outside, and it got soaked by the rain. A waiter ran out and brought the umbrella inside. In the corner sit a young couple, whispering to each other the whole time, completely unaware of the rain. Next to the bar counter sits a woman, drinking coffee alone, tracing something on the glass with her finger. The rain falls harder and harder, but nobody wants to leave. As long as it's still raining outside, this is the warmest place in the whole city. I ordered a second cup of coffee and decided to wait until the rain stops.

questions.q_zh: 被雨淋湿的是什么？
questions.q_en: What got soaked by the rain?

options.pinyin: yǒu rén wàng zài wàimiàn de sǎn

questions.explanation: "Someone left their umbrella outside, and it got soaked" gives you what you need.

questions.q_en: Where does the tension or contradiction in this passage come from?

options.text: 理想和现实之间的差距
options.pinyin: lǐxiǎng hé xiànshí zhījiān de chājù
options.text_en: the gap between ideal and reality

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么不走？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't 'I' leave?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi kāfēi hái méi hē wán

options.text: 因为决定等雨停了再走
options.pinyin: yīnwèi juédìng děng yǔ tíng le zài zǒu
options.text_en: decided to wait until the rain stops

questions.explanation: Look at "I ordered a second cup of coffee and decided" — the reason is right there.

title: The Sleeping Cat on the Bookshelf
text_zh: 二手书店的老板养了一只橘色的猫。这只猫最喜欢的地方不是地上的垫子，而是最高那层书架。它每天都跳上去，把自己缩成一团，睡在两本厚书中间。客人进来看到书架上有一只猫，常常被吓一跳。老板说：「你们别担心，它从来不会把书弄掉。它比很多客人都小心。」有时候猫醒了，就趴在书架上看下面的人，眼睛半开半闭的。无论店里多热闹，它都不会下来。只有到了关门的时间，它才慢慢地跳下来，走到老板脚边，等着一起回家。
text_pinyin: Èrshǒu shūdiàn de lǎobǎn yǎng le yì zhī jú sè de māo. Zhè zhī māo zuì xǐhuan de dìfang búshì dì shàng de diànzi, érshì zuì gāo nà céng shūjià. Tā měi tiān dōu tiào shàngqu, bǎ zìjǐ suō chéng yì tuán, shuì zài liǎng běn hòu shū zhōngjiān. Kèrén jìnlái kàn dào shūjià shàng yǒu yì zhī māo, chángcháng bèi xià yí tiào. Lǎobǎn shuō: 「Nǐmen bié dānxīn, tā cónglái bú huì bǎ shū nòng diào. Tā bǐ hěn duō kèrén dōu xiǎoxīn.」 Yǒu shíhou māo xǐng le, jiù pā zài shūjià shàng kàn xiàmiàn de rén, yǎnjing bàn kāi bàn bì de. Wúlùn diàn lǐ duō rènào, tā dōu bú huì xiàlái. Zhǐ yǒu dào le guān mén de shíjiān, tā cái mànmàn de tiào xiàlái, zǒu dào lǎobǎn jiǎo biān, děngzhe yìqǐ huí jiā.
text_en: The owner of a secondhand bookshop keeps an orange cat. The cat's favorite spot isn't the cushion on the floor, but the highest shelf. Every day it jumps up, curls itself into a ball, and sleeps between two thick books. When customers come in and see a cat on the bookshelf, they're often startled. The owner says: 'Don't worry, it never knocks books down. It's more careful than many customers.' Sometimes when the cat wakes up, it lies on the shelf watching the people below, eyes half-open, half-closed. No matter how busy the shop gets, it never comes down. Only when closing time arrives does it slowly jump down, walk to the owner's feet, and wait to go home together.

questions.q_zh: 这段话对'时间'的处理方式说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the way this passage handles 'time' suggest?

options.text: 时间不只是客观的流逝

options.text_en: time is more than objective passing

options.pinyin: xūyào gèng hǎo de guǎnlǐ shíjiān

questions.explanation: The key is "Only when closing time arrives does it slowly jump down" — read it with the answer in mind.

questions.q_zh: 作者通过叙述想引发读者什么思考？
questions.q_en: What reflection does the author want to provoke in the reader?

options.text: 对日常生活更深的理解
options.pinyin: duì rìcháng shēnghuó gèng shēn de lǐjiě

questions.explanation: "The owner of a secondhand bookshop keeps an orange cat" stays with you — that's the intended effect.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的写作手法有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What is distinctive about the writing technique in this passage?

options.text: 以小见大，用细节传达深意
options.pinyin: yǐ xiǎo jiàn dà, yòng xìjié chuándá shēnyì
options.text_en: uses small details to convey big meaning

questions.explanation: The author earns the effect through moments like "The owner of a secondhand bookshop keeps an orange cat."

title: The Subway Musician
text_zh: 每天早上八点，地铁站的通道里都会传来吉他声。弹吉他的是一个三十多岁的男人，他把琴盒打开放在地上，但好像并不在意有没有人往里面放钱。他的音乐不是那种很吵的，而是安安静静的，像一条小河流过人群。赶着上班的人大部分都走得很快，只有少数人会停下来听一两分钟。有一次我被他弹的一首歌吸引住了，不知不觉站在那里听了很久。后来我才发现，他每天弹的歌都不一样，好像在用音乐记录每一天的心情。这个城市虽然很忙，但是因为有他，地铁站变得不那么冷了。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang bā diǎn, dìtiě zhàn de tōngdào lǐ dōu huì chuán lái jítā shēng. Tán jítā de shì yí gè sānshí duō suì de nánrén, tā bǎ qínhé dǎkāi fàng zài dì shàng, dàn hǎoxiàng bìng bú zàiyì yǒu méiyǒu rén wǎng lǐmiàn fàng qián. Tā de yīnyuè búshì nà zhǒng hěn chǎo de, érshì ān'ānjìngjìng de, xiàng yì tiáo xiǎo hé liú guò rénqún. Gǎnzhe shàngbān de rén dà bùfen dōu zǒu de hěn kuài, zhǐ yǒu shǎoshù rén huì tíng xiàlái tīng yī liǎng fēnzhōng. Yǒu yí cì wǒ bèi tā tán de yì shǒu gē xīyǐn zhù le, bùzhī-bùjué zhàn zài nàlǐ tīng le hěn jiǔ. Hòulái wǒ cái fāxiàn, tā měi tiān tán de gē dōu bù yíyàng, hǎoxiàng zài yòng yīnyuè jìlù měi yì tiān de xīnqíng. Zhège chéngshì suīrán hěn máng, dànshì yīnwèi yǒu tā, dìtiě zhàn biàn de bú nàme lěng le.
text_en: Every morning at eight, guitar music drifts through the subway station corridor. The guitarist is a man in his thirties who opens his guitar case on the ground, though he doesn't seem to care whether anyone puts money in it. His music isn't the loud kind — it's quiet and gentle, like a small river flowing through the crowd. Most people rushing to work walk quickly past; only a few stop to listen for a minute or two. Once I was captivated by a song he played and stood there listening for a long time without realizing it. Later I discovered that he plays different songs every day, as if recording each day's mood with music. Although this city is busy, because of him, the subway station feels a little less cold.

questions.q_zh: 弹吉他的男人在意别人给他钱吗？
questions.q_en: Does the guitar player care about people giving him money?

options.pinyin: tā bú ràng biéren gěi qián
options.text_en: he doesn't let people give money

questions.explanation: "The guitarist is a man in his thirties who opens" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 「我」有一次在地铁站怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened to 'I' once at the subway station?

options.text: 被一首歌吸引住了，听了很久
options.pinyin: bèi yì shǒu gē xīyǐn zhù le, tīng le hěn jiǔ
options.text_en: was captivated by a song and listened a long time

options.pinyin: gěi le tā hěn duō qián
options.text_en: gave him a lot of money

questions.explanation: "Once I was captivated by a song he played" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 他每天弹的歌有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What's special about the songs he plays each day?

options.pinyin: dōu shì hěn chǎo de gē
měi tiān tán de dōu bù yíyàng

questions.explanation: Go back to "Later I discovered that he plays different songs every day." That's where the answer lives.

title: Rooftop Laundry
text_zh: 从我家窗户往外看，能看到对面楼的天台。每天早上，住在那里的人把洗好的衣服挂出去。白色的床单被风吹得像一面旗子，红色的毛衣在阳光下特别好看。我不认识那些邻居，但是通过他们晾的衣服，我好像了解了一点他们的生活。有小孩的校服，说明那家有孩子在上学。有一件很大的工作服，可能是一个在工厂上班的人穿的。不但能看到衣服的颜色，而且有时候还能闻到洗衣液的香味。这些普通的衣服被风和阳光照顾着，就像这个城市里的人被生活慢慢照顾着一样。
text_pinyin: Cóng wǒ jiā chuānghu wǎng wài kàn, néng kàn dào duìmiàn lóu de tiāntái. Měi tiān zǎoshang, zhù zài nàlǐ de rén bǎ xǐ hǎo de yīfu guà chūqù. Bái sè de chuángdān bèi fēng chuī de xiàng yí miàn qízi, hóng sè de máoyī zài yángguāng xià tèbié hǎokàn. Wǒ bú rènshi nàxiē línjū, dànshì tōngguò tāmen liàng de yīfu, wǒ hǎoxiàng liǎojiě le yìdiǎn tāmen de shēnghuó. Yǒu xiǎohái de xiàofú, shuōmíng nà jiā yǒu háizi zài shàng xué. Yǒu yí jiàn hěn dà de gōngzuòfú, kěnéng shì yí gè zài gōngchǎng shàngbān de rén chuān de. Búdàn néng kàn dào yīfu de yánsè, érqiě yǒu shíhou hái néng wén dào xǐyījī de xiāngwèi. Zhèxiē pǔtōng de yīfu bèi fēng hé yángguāng zhàogùzhe, jiù xiàng zhège chéngshì lǐ de rén bèi shēnghuó mànmàn zhàogùzhe yíyàng.
text_en: Looking out from my window, I can see the rooftop of the building across the street. Every morning, the people living there hang out their freshly washed clothes. White bedsheets billow in the wind like flags; a red sweater looks especially beautiful in the sunlight. I don't know those neighbors, but through the clothes they hang out to dry, I feel I understand a little about their lives. There's a child's school uniform, meaning that family has a kid in school. There's an oversized work uniform, probably worn by someone who works in a factory. Not only can you see the colors of the clothes, but sometimes you can even smell the fragrance of detergent. These ordinary clothes are tended by wind and sun, just as the people in this city are slowly tended by life itself.

questions.q_zh: 「我」怎么了解对面邻居的生活？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' learn about the neighbors' lives?

options.pinyin: tōngguò kàn tāmen liàng de yīfu
options.text_en: by looking at the clothes they hang out
by looking at their social media

questions.explanation: Look at "I don't know those neighbors, but through the clothes" — it shows the mechanism.

questions.q_zh: 白色的床单被风吹得像什么？
questions.q_en: What do the white bedsheets look like in the wind?
questions.explanation: The comparison is spelled out: "White bedsheets billow in the wind like flags; a red."

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以推断作者的什么立场？
questions.q_en: What stance of the author can be inferred from this passage?

options.text: 欣赏生活中的简单之美
options.pinyin: xīnshǎng shēnghuó zhōng de jiǎndān zhī měi

questions.explanation: "These ordinary clothes are tended by wind and sun" is the clearest signal of the passage's point.

title: The Library's Quiet Rule
text_zh: 我们学校的图书馆有一个不成文的规则：三楼是绝对安静的区域，连翻书的声音都要尽量小一些。大家自觉地遵守这个规则，不需要任何人提醒。新来的学生有时候不知道，会在三楼小声打电话，马上就会被周围的人用目光提醒。管理员是一个五十多岁的阿姨，她从来不大声说话，只要有人太吵了，她就走过去，轻轻地把一根手指放在嘴唇前面。这个动作比任何话都有用。无论是考试前还是平时，三楼永远是最安静的。有人说这里安静得像一座寺庙。我觉得，这种安静不是被规定出来的，而是大家一起创造出来的。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xuéxiào de túshūguǎn yǒu yí gè bù chéng wén de guīzé: sān lóu shì juéduì ānjìng de qūyù, lián fān shū de shēngyīn dōu yào jǐnliàng xiǎo yìxiē. Dàjiā zìjué de zūnshǒu zhège guīzé, bù xūyào rènhé rén tíxǐng. Xīn lái de xuéshēng yǒu shíhou bù zhīdào, huì zài sān lóu xiǎo shēng dǎ diànhuà, mǎshàng jiù huì bèi zhōuwéi de rén yòng mùguāng tíxǐng. Guǎnlǐyuán shì yí gè wǔshí duō suì de āyí, tā cónglái bú dà shēng shuō huà, zhǐyào yǒu rén tài chǎo le, tā jiù zǒu guòqù, qīngqīng de bǎ yì gēn shǒuzhǐ fàng zài zuǐchún qiánmiàn. Zhège dòngzuò bǐ rènhé huà dōu yǒuyòng. Wúlùn shì kǎoshì qián háishi píngshí, sān lóu yǒngyuǎn shì zuì ānjìng de. Yǒu rén shuō zhèlǐ ānjìng de xiàng yí zuò sìmiào. Wǒ juéde, zhè zhǒng ānjìng búshì bèi guīdìng chūlái de, érshì dàjiā yìqǐ chuàngzào chūlái de.
text_en: Our school library has an unwritten rule: the third floor is an absolutely quiet zone — even the sound of turning pages should be kept as soft as possible. Everyone follows this rule voluntarily, without anyone needing to remind them. New students sometimes don't know and make quiet phone calls on the third floor, but are immediately reminded by the gazes of those around them. The librarian is a woman in her fifties who never speaks loudly. Whenever someone is too noisy, she walks over and gently puts a finger to her lips. This gesture is more effective than any words. Whether before exams or on ordinary days, the third floor is always the quietest. Some say it's as quiet as a temple. I think this kind of quiet isn't imposed by rules — it's something everyone creates together.

questions.q_zh: 新来的学生在三楼做什么会被提醒？
questions.q_en: What do new students do on the third floor that gets them reminded?
questions.explanation: "New students sometimes don't know and make quiet phone calls" gives you what you need.

questions.q_zh: 管理员怎么提醒太吵的人？
questions.q_en: How does the librarian remind noisy people?

options.pinyin: bǎ shǒuzhǐ fàng zài zuǐchún qiánmiàn
options.text_en: puts a finger to her lips
asks them to go to another floor

questions.explanation: "Whenever someone is too noisy, she walks over and gently" walks you through it.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得三楼的安静是怎么来的？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' think the third floor's quiet came about?

options.text_en: it was set by the school

options.text: 是大家一起创造出来的
options.pinyin: shì dàjiā yìqǐ chuàngzào chūlái de
shì yīnwèi sān lóu méiyǒu rén
options.text_en: because nobody is on the third floor

questions.explanation: Look at "It's something everyone creates together" — it shows the mechanism.

title: The Missing Teacup
text_zh: 办公室里有一个奇怪的事情：每到星期五下午，茶水间的一个蓝色茶杯就会不见。到了星期一早上，它又会出现在原来的地方，而且被洗得干干净净。大家都很好奇是谁拿走的，但是从来没有人承认。有人猜是打扫卫生的阿姨拿去用了，但她说她从来不喝茶。也有人说可能是被新来的同事不小心拿错了。直到有一天，我加班到很晚，看到保安叔叔走进茶水间，把那个蓝色的杯子拿走了。原来他每个周末值班的时候，都会用那个杯子泡茶。他不好意思地说：「那是我女儿送给我的，跟这个一模一样，但是被我不小心打碎了。」
text_pinyin: Bàngōngshì lǐ yǒu yí gè qíguài de shìqíng: měi dào xīngqīwǔ xiàwǔ, cháshuǐ jiān de yí gè lán sè chábēi jiù huì bújiàn. Dào le xīngqīyī zǎoshang, tā yòu huì chūxiàn zài yuánlái de dìfang, érqiě bèi xǐ de gāngān-jìngjìng. Dàjiā dōu hěn hàoqí shì shéi ná zǒu de, dànshì cónglái méiyǒu rén chéngrèn. Yǒu rén cāi shì dǎsǎo wèishēng de āyí ná qù yòng le, dàn tā shuō tā cónglái bù hē chá. Yě yǒu rén shuō kěnéng shì bèi xīn lái de tóngshì bù xiǎoxīn ná cuò le. Zhídào yǒu yì tiān, wǒ jiābān dào hěn wǎn, kàn dào bǎo'ān shūshu zǒu jìn cháshuǐ jiān, bǎ nàge lán sè de bēizi ná zǒu le. Yuánlái tā měi gè zhōumò zhíbān de shíhou, dōu huì yòng nàge bēizi pào chá. Tā bù hǎoyìsi de shuō: 「Nà shì wǒ nǚ'ér sòng gěi wǒ de, gēn zhège yìmó-yíyàng, dànshì bèi wǒ bù xiǎoxīn dǎ suì le.」
text_en: Something strange happens in the office: every Friday afternoon, a blue teacup in the break room disappears. By Monday morning, it reappears in its original spot, washed perfectly clean. Everyone is curious about who takes it, but nobody has ever admitted it. Some guessed it was the cleaning lady, but she said she never drinks tea. Others said maybe a new colleague accidentally took the wrong cup. Until one day, when I was working overtime late, I saw the security guard walk into the break room and take the blue cup away. It turns out that every weekend when he's on duty, he uses that cup to brew tea. He said embarrassedly: 'My daughter gave me one exactly like this, but I accidentally broke it.'

questions.q_en: What is the symbolic meaning in this text?

options.text: 外在事物反映内心世界
options.text_en: external things reflect the inner world

questions.explanation: The text draws the comparison directly: "Something strange happens in the office: every Friday afternoon."

questions.q_zh: 是谁拿走了茶杯？
questions.q_en: Who took the teacup?
questions.explanation: The key is "I saw the security guard walk into the break room" — read it with the answer in mind.

questions.q_zh: 保安为什么用那个杯子？
questions.q_en: Why does the security guard use that cup?

options.text: 因为那个杯子跟女儿送他的一模一样

options.text_en: it's identical to the one his daughter gave him

options.text: 因为他觉得那个杯子好看
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā juéde nàge bēizi hǎokàn
options.text_en: because he thinks the cup is nice

options.pinyin: yīnwèi biéren ràng tā yòng de
options.text_en: because someone told him to use it

questions.explanation: Look at "My daughter gave me one exactly like" — the reason is right there.

title: Two Names
text_zh: 我有两个名字。在家里，爸妈叫我「小鱼」，因为我小时候特别喜欢游泳。在学校和公司，大家都叫我的大名「李雨桐」。有时候我觉得自己像两个不同的人。「小鱼」是那个喜欢赤脚在地上跑的小孩，而「李雨桐」是一个每天穿着正式的衣服去上班的大人。只要妈妈一叫「小鱼」，我就会突然变回小时候的样子。有一次，同事不小心听到我妈打电话叫我「小鱼」，大家都笑了。从那以后，办公室里的人也开始叫我「小鱼」。奇怪的是，被同事这样叫的时候，我觉得工作变得不那么累了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng gè míngzi. Zài jiā lǐ, bàmā jiào wǒ「Xiǎo Yú」, yīnwèi wǒ xiǎo shíhou tèbié xǐhuan yóuyǒng. Zài xuéxiào hé gōngsī, dàjiā dōu jiào wǒ de dàmíng「Lǐ Yǔtóng」. Yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde zìjǐ xiàng liǎng gè bùtóng de rén. 「Xiǎo Yú」shì nàge xǐhuan chì jiǎo zài dì shàng pǎo de xiǎohái, ér「Lǐ Yǔtóng」shì yí gè měi tiān chuānzhe zhèngshì de yīfu qù shàngbān de dàrén. Zhǐyào māma yī jiào「Xiǎo Yú」, wǒ jiù huì tūrán biàn huí xiǎo shíhou de yàngzi. Yǒu yí cì, tóngshì bù xiǎoxīn tīng dào wǒ mā dǎ diànhuà jiào wǒ「Xiǎo Yú」, dàjiā dōu xiào le. Cóng nà yǐhòu, bàngōngshì lǐ de rén yě kāishǐ jiào wǒ「Xiǎo Yú」. Qíguài de shì, bèi tóngshì zhèyàng jiào de shíhou, wǒ juéde gōngzuò biàn de bú nàme lèi le. Yěxǔ měi gè dàrén xīnlǐ dōu zhùzhe yí gè xūyào bèi jiào xiǎomíng de háizi.
text_en: I have two names. At home, my parents call me 'Little Fish' because I loved swimming as a child. At school and at work, everyone calls me by my formal name, Li Yutong. Sometimes I feel like two different people. 'Little Fish' is the kid who loved running barefoot on the ground, while 'Li Yutong' is an adult who wears formal clothes to work every day. The moment my mom calls 'Little Fish,' I suddenly turn back into my childhood self. Once, a colleague accidentally overheard my mom call me 'Little Fish' on the phone, and After that, people in the office started calling me 'Little Fish' too. Strangely, when my colleagues call me that, work feels less tiring.

questions.q_en: Why do the parents call her 'Little Fish'?

options.text_en: because her home is by a river

questions.explanation: The reason hides in plain sight: "At home, my parents call me 'Little Fish' because."

options.text_en: her dad called her that at the office

questions.q_en: How does she feel after colleagues call her 'Little Fish'?

options.pinyin: juéde gōngzuò biàn de bú nàme lèi le

questions.explanation: Read "Strangely, when my colleagues call me that, work feels less" slowly — the feeling is in the phrasing.

title: Someone Else's Birthday
text_zh: 昨天晚上我点了一份炒饭。外卖员来了，递给我一个很大的袋子。我打开一看——是一个生日蛋糕，上面写着「亲爱的，生日快乐」。我吓了一跳。我跟外卖员解释，他看了看手机说：「糟糕，两份单搞混了。」然后他满头大汗地去找我的炒饭。半个小时以后他回来了，端着我的炒饭，不好意思地说：「那个人收到了你的炒饭，还以为是男朋友送的惊喜。」我一边吃炒饭一边想：当那个蛋糕变成炒饭的时候，她是什么表情？
text_pinyin: Zuótiān wǎnshang wǒ diǎn le yí fèn chǎofàn. Wàimài yuán lái le, dì gěi wǒ yí gè hěn dà de dàizi. Wǒ dǎkāi yí kàn — shì yí gè shēngrì dàngāo, shàngmiàn xiě zhe 'qīn'ài de, shēngrì kuàilè'. Wǒ xià le yí tiào. Wǒ gēn wàimài yuán jiěshì, tā kàn le kàn shǒujī shuō: 'zāogāo, liǎng fèn dān gǎo hùn le.' Ránhòu tā mǎntóu dàhàn de qù zhǎo wǒ de chǎofàn. Bàn ge xiǎoshí yǐhòu tā huílái le, duān zhe wǒ de chǎofàn, bù hǎoyìsi de shuō: 'nà ge rén shōu dào le nǐ de chǎofàn, hái yǐwéi shì nán péngyou sòng de jīngxǐ.' Wǒ yìbiān chī chǎofàn yìbiān xiǎng: dāng nà ge dàngāo biànchéng chǎofàn de shíhou, tā shì shénme biǎoqíng?
text_en: Last night I ordered fried rice. The delivery guy handed me a huge bag. I opened it — a birthday cake, iced with 'Happy Birthday, darling.' I nearly jumped. I explained the mix-up; he checked his phone: 'Oh no, I swapped the two orders.' Then he ran off, sweating, to track down my fried rice. Half an hour later he came back with it, embarrassed: 'The other person got your fried rice and thought it was a surprise from her boyfriend.' I ate my fried rice and wondered: what was her face when the birthday cake turned into fried rice?

questions.q_zh: 外卖员送错了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the delivery person bring by mistake?
questions.explanation: Go back to "I opened." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 对方收到炒饭以后以为是什么？
questions.q_en: What did the other person think the fried rice was?

options.text: 以为是男朋友送的惊喜
options.pinyin: yǐwéi shì nán péngyou sòng de jīngxǐ
options.text_en: thought it was a surprise from her boyfriend
thought it was from a neighbor

questions.explanation: Focus on "Half an hour later he came back with it, embarrassed" — the rest follows.

questions.q_zh: 作者选择这个视角有什么效果？
questions.q_en: What effect does the author's choice of perspective have?
questions.explanation: Without "Last night I ordered fried rice," the passage loses its anchor.

title: The Shoe Repair Stand
text_zh: 菜市场旁边有一个修鞋的老太太。她把一把小凳子放在路边，旁边摆着她的工具箱。她的手很粗糙，但是做起活来又快又好。不管是皮鞋还是运动鞋，她都能修。有一次我把一双很旧的鞋拿去让她修，她看了看说：「这鞋跟了你很久了吧？穿旧的鞋子比新的舒服。」她只要十分钟就把鞋修好了，收了我十块钱。我问她为什么收得这么便宜，她笑着说：「我不是为了赚钱，只是闲不住。坐在家里不如坐在外面，还能看看人。」她每天从早上八点坐到下午四点，中间只休息一次，吃一个自己带来的饭盒。
text_pinyin: Càishìchǎng pángbiān yǒu yí gè xiū xié de lǎo tàitai. Tā bǎ yì bǎ xiǎo dèngzi fàng zài lùbiān, pángbiān bǎizhe tā de gōngjùxiāng. Tā de shǒu hěn cūcāo, dànshì zuò qǐ huó lái yòu kuài yòu hǎo. Bùguǎn shì píxié háishi yùndòngxié, tā dōu néng xiū. Yǒu yí cì wǒ bǎ yì shuāng hěn jiù de xié ná qù ràng tā xiū, tā kàn le kàn shuō: 「Zhè xié gēn le nǐ hěn jiǔ le ba? Chuān jiù de xiézi bǐ xīn de shūfu.」 Tā zhǐyào shí fēnzhōng jiù bǎ xié xiū hǎo le, shōu le wǒ shí kuài qián. Wǒ wèn tā wèishénme shōu de zhème piányi, tā xiàozhe shuō: 「Wǒ búshì wèile zhuàn qián, zhǐ shì xián bu zhù. Zuò zài jiā lǐ bùrú zuò zài wàimiàn, hái néng kànkan rén.」 Tā měi tiān cóng zǎoshang bā diǎn zuò dào xiàwǔ sì diǎn, zhōngjiān zhǐ xiūxi yí cì, chī yí gè zìjǐ dài lái de fànhé.
text_en: Next to the wet market there's an elderly woman who repairs shoes. She sets a small stool by the roadside with her toolbox beside it. Her hands are rough, but her work is fast and good. Whether it's leather shoes or sneakers, she can fix them. Once I brought her a old pair of shoes to repair. She looked at them and said: 'These shoes have been with you a long time, haven't they? Worn-in shoes are more comfortable than new ones.' She fixed the shoes in just ten minutes and charged me ten yuan. I asked why so cheap. She smiled and said: 'I'm not in it for the money — I just can't sit still. Better to sit outside than at home. I can still watch people.' She sits there every day from eight in the morning to four in the afternoon, resting only once to eat a lunch box she brings herself.

questions.q_zh: 老太太为什么修鞋收费很便宜？
questions.q_en: Why does the old woman charge so little for shoe repair?

options.text: 因为她不是为了赚钱，只是闲不住
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā búshì wèile zhuàn qián, zhǐ shì xián bu zhù
options.text_en: she's not in it for money, just can't sit still
because the shoes are too old

questions.q_en: What stance of the author can be inferred from this passage?

options.text: 欣赏生活中的简单之美
options.pinyin: xīnshǎng shēnghuó zhōng de jiǎndān zhī měi

questions.explanation: Details like "Next to the wet market there's an elderly woman" accumulate into this larger meaning.

questions.q_zh: 文中的象征意义是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the symbolic meaning in this text?

options.text: 外在事物反映内心世界
options.text_en: external things reflect the inner world

questions.explanation: "Next to the wet market there's an elderly woman" — the parallel is stated, not implied.

title: The Night Jogger
text_zh: 每天晚上十点左右，我在阳台上都能看到一个人在小区里跑步。他跑得很慢，不像是在锻炼，更像是在散步。他总是戴着耳机，一个人安静地跑。无论天气多冷，他都穿着同一件灰色的运动衫。有时候下着小雨他也跑，路灯把他的影子拉得很长。我从来不知道他住在哪栋楼，也不知道他是做什么工作的。但是每天看到他跑过去，我就觉得今天的一天正式结束了。有一天晚上他没有出现，我竟然有一点不习惯。后来才知道他出差去了。等他回来继续跑的那天晚上，我在阳台上默默地松了一口气。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān wǎnshang shí diǎn zuǒyòu, wǒ zài yángtái shàng dōu néng kàn dào yí gè rén zài xiǎoqū lǐ pǎobù. Tā pǎo de hěn màn, bú xiàng shì zài duànliàn, gèng xiàng shì zài sànbù. Tā zǒng shì dàizhe ěrjī, yí gè rén ānjìng de pǎo. Wúlùn tiānqì duō lěng, tā dōu chuānzhe tóng yí jiàn huī sè de yùndòngshān. Yǒu shíhou xiàzhe xiǎo yǔ tā yě pǎo, lùdēng bǎ tā de yǐngzi lā de hěn cháng. Wǒ cónglái bù zhīdào tā zhù zài nǎ dòng lóu, yě bù zhīdào tā shì zuò shénme gōngzuò de. Dànshì měi tiān kàn dào tā pǎo guòqù, wǒ jiù juéde jīntiān de yì tiān zhèngshì jiéshù le. Yǒu yì tiān wǎnshang tā méiyǒu chūxiàn, wǒ jìngrán yǒu yìdiǎn bù xíguàn. Hòulái cái zhīdào tā chūchāi qù le. Děng tā huílái jìxù pǎo de nà tiān wǎnshang, wǒ zài yángtái shàng mòmò de sōng le yì kǒu qì.
text_en: Every night around ten, I can see someone jogging in the compound from my balcony. He runs slowly — not like exercising, more like taking a walk. He always wears earphones, running quietly alone. No matter how cold the weather, he wears the same gray sweatshirt. Sometimes he runs even in light rain, the streetlights stretching his shadow long. I never know which building he lives in or what he does for work. But every time I see him run past, I feel the day has officially ended. One night he didn't appear, and I was surprisingly unsettled. Later I learned he'd gone on a business trip. The night he came back and started running again, I quietly breathed a sigh of relief on my balcony.

questions.q_zh: 作者通过叙述想引发读者什么思考？
questions.q_en: What reflection does the author want to provoke in the reader?

options.text: 对日常生活更深的理解
options.pinyin: duì rìcháng shēnghuó gèng shēn de lǐjiě

questions.explanation: The author uses "Every night around ten" to open a door in your thinking.

questions.q_zh: 那个人没出现的那天晚上，「我」怎么了？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel the night the jogger didn't appear?
questions.explanation: Read "One night he didn't appear, and I was surprisingly unsettled" slowly — the feeling is in the phrasing.

questions.q_zh: 那个人为什么有一天没跑步？
questions.q_en: Why didn't the person jog one day?

options.text_en: because he went on a business trip

questions.explanation: Look at "Later I learned he'd gone on a business trip" — the reason is right there.

title: The Flower Seller's Bucket
text_zh: 地铁站出口有一个卖花的老太太。她不用花店那种漂亮的包装，只是把花一束一束地插在一个旧铁桶里。她卖的花都是最普通的——百合、玫瑰、还有一些叫不出名字的小花。买花的人不多，大部分路过的人看一眼就走了。但是偶尔会有人停下来，把鼻子凑到花前面闻一闻。老太太从来不主动招呼客人，只是安静地坐在那里，手里拿着一把剪刀，慢慢地把花的叶子修整齐。不管今天卖出去几束，到了晚上她都会把剩下的花送给地铁站的保洁阿姨。保洁阿姨把花插在拖把旁边的水桶里，那个角落一下子就亮了。
text_pinyin: Dìtiě zhàn chūkǒu yǒu yí gè mài huā de lǎo tàitai. Tā bú yòng huādiàn nà zhǒng piàoliang de bāozhuāng, zhǐ shì bǎ huā yí shù yí shù de chā zài yí gè jiù tiě tǒng lǐ. Tā mài de huā dōu shì zuì pǔtōng de——bǎihé, méigui, hái yǒu yìxiē jiào bu chū míngzi de xiǎo huā. Mǎi huā de rén bù duō, dà bùfen lùguò de rén kàn yì yǎn jiù zǒu le. Dànshì ǒu'ěr huì yǒu rén tíng xiàlái, bǎ bízi còu dào huā qiánmiàn wén yì wén. Lǎo tàitai cónglái bù zhǔdòng zhāohu kèrén, zhǐ shì ānjìng de zuò zài nàlǐ, shǒu lǐ názhe yì bǎ jiǎndāo, mànmàn de bǎ huā de yèzi xiū zhěngqí. Bùguǎn jīntiān mài chūqù jǐ shù, dào le wǎnshang tā dōu huì bǎ shèng xià de huā sòng gěi dìtiě zhàn de bǎojié āyí. Bǎojié āyí bǎ huā chā zài tuōbǎ pángbiān de shuǐtǒng lǐ, nàge jiǎoluò yíxiàzi jiù liàng le.
text_en: At the subway station exit there's an old woman who sells flowers. She doesn't use the pretty wrapping of flower shops — she just puts bunches of flowers into an old iron bucket. The flowers she sells are the most ordinary kind: lilies, roses, and some small flowers whose names I can't say. Not many people buy flowers; most passersby glance and walk on. But occasionally someone stops and leans their nose toward the flowers for a sniff. The old woman never actively greets customers. She just sits quietly, holding a pair of scissors, slowly trimming the leaves neat. No matter how many bunches she sells that day, in the evening she gives the remaining flowers to the subway station's cleaning lady. The cleaning lady puts the flowers in the water bucket next to her mop, and that corner instantly brightens.

questions.q_zh: 食物在这段话中代表什么？
questions.q_en: What does food represent in this passage?
questions.explanation: "At the subway station exit there's an old woman" — the parallel is stated, not implied.

questions.q_zh: 卖不完的花最后怎么处理？
questions.q_en: What happens to the unsold flowers?

options.text: 送给地铁站的保洁阿姨
options.pinyin: sòng gěi dìtiě zhàn de bǎojié āyí
options.text_en: given to the station's cleaning lady

questions.explanation: "No matter how many bunches she sells that day" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 做饭/吃饭的场景暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the eating/cooking scene imply?
questions.explanation: Go back to "At the subway station exit there's an old woman." That's where the answer lives.

title: The Bench Between Two Trees
text_zh: 公园里有一条长椅，刚好在两棵大树中间。夏天的时候，树叶把阳光挡住了，坐在那里特别凉快。这条长椅是很多人的「秘密地方」。早上是老人们的——他们在那里下棋、聊天。中午是上班族的——他们买了午饭坐在那里吃。下午是妈妈们的——她们一边看孩子玩，一边说话。到了傍晚，长椅就属于那些散步的情侣了。我观察了很久，发现大家好像有一种默契：每个人都只在自己的时间段来，从来不跟别的时间段的人抢位子。这条长椅不属于任何人，但是它被每个人温柔地使用着，就像一个被轮流照顾的朋友。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yì tiáo chángyǐ, gānghǎo zài liǎng kē dà shù zhōngjiān. Xiàtiān de shíhou, shùyè bǎ yángguāng dǎng zhù le, zuò zài nàlǐ tèbié liángkuai. Zhè tiáo chángyǐ shì hěn duō rén de「mìmì dìfang」. Zǎoshang shì lǎorénmen de——tāmen zài nàlǐ xià qí, liáotiān. Zhōngwǔ shì shàngbānzú de——tāmen mǎi le wǔfàn zuò zài nàlǐ chī. Xiàwǔ shì māmamen de——tāmen yìbiān kàn háizi wán, yìbiān shuō huà. Dào le bàngwǎn, chángyǐ jiù shǔyú nàxiē sànbù de qínglǚ le. Wǒ guānchá le hěn jiǔ, fāxiàn dàjiā hǎoxiàng yǒu yì zhǒng mòqì: měi gè rén dōu zhǐ zài zìjǐ de shíjiān duàn lái, cónglái bù gēn biéde shíjiān duàn de rén qiǎng wèizi. Zhè tiáo chángyǐ bù shǔyú rènhé rén, dànshì tā bèi měi gè rén wēnróu de shǐyòngzhe, jiù xiàng yí gè bèi lúnliú zhàogù de péngyou.
text_en: In the park there's a bench right between two big trees. In summer, the leaves block the sunlight, making it especially cool to sit there. This bench is many people's 'secret spot.' In the morning it belongs to the elderly — they play chess and chat there. At noon it's the office workers' — they buy lunch and eat sitting there. In the afternoon it's the mothers' — they watch their children play while talking. By evening, the bench belongs to the couples out for a stroll. After observing for a long time, I noticed everyone seems to share an unspoken understanding: each person comes only during their own time slot and never competes for the seat with people from other time slots. This bench doesn't belong to anyone, yet it's used gently by everyone, like a friend being taken care of in turns.

questions.q_zh: 中午谁坐在长椅上？
questions.q_en: Who sits on the bench at noon?
questions.explanation: Go back to "At noon it's the office workers'." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 这段话给读者留下了什么思考空间？
questions.q_en: What space for thought does this passage leave the reader?

options.text: 关于个人价值和意义的反思
options.pinyin: guānyú gèrén jiàzhí hé yìyì de fǎnsī
options.text_en: reflection on personal value and meaning

options.pinyin: rúhé zhuàn gèng duō de qián

questions.explanation: The author uses "In the park there's a bench right between two big" to open a door in your thinking.

questions.q_zh: 作者选择这个视角有什么效果？
questions.q_en: What effect does the author's choice of perspective have?
questions.explanation: Without "In the park there's a bench right between two big," the passage loses its anchor.

title: The Alley Barber
text_zh: 我家附近有一条老巷子，巷子里有一家只有两把椅子的理发店。理发师是一个六十多岁的大叔，他只会两种发型：短的和更短的。他不会染头发，也不会做那些年轻人喜欢的时髦发型。但是每天来的客人都不少，因为他剪头发只要十五块钱，而且他特别会聊天。无论你跟他说什么话题，他都能接得上。有人说他是整条巷子里消息最灵通的人。他把每个客人都当作老朋友，记得他们的名字、工作、甚至孩子在哪里上学。虽然外面新开了很多漂亮的理发店，但是老客人们还是喜欢来这里。他们说：「在别的地方剪头发，只是剪头发。在这里，是回家。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā fùjìn yǒu yì tiáo lǎo xiàngzi, xiàngzi lǐ yǒu yì jiā zhǐ yǒu liǎng bǎ yǐzi de lǐfàdiàn. Lǐfàshī shì yí gè liùshí duō suì de dàshū, tā zhǐ huì liǎng zhǒng fàxíng: duǎn de hé gèng duǎn de. Tā bú huì rǎn tóufa, yě bú huì zuò nàxiē niánqīngrén xǐhuan de shímáo fàxíng. Dànshì měi tiān lái de kèrén dōu bù shǎo, yīnwèi tā jiǎn tóufa zhǐ yào shíwǔ kuài qián, érqiě tā tèbié huì liáotiān. Wúlùn nǐ gēn tā shuō shénme huàtí, tā dōu néng jiē de shàng. Yǒu rén shuō tā shì zhěng tiáo xiàngzi lǐ xiāoxi zuì língtōng de rén. Tā bǎ měi gè kèrén dōu dāng zuò lǎo péngyou, jìde tāmen de míngzi, gōngzuò, shènzhì háizi zài nǎlǐ shàng xué. Suīrán wàimiàn xīn kāi le hěn duō piàoliang de lǐfàdiàn, dànshì lǎo kèrénmen háishi xǐhuan lái zhèlǐ. Tāmen shuō: 「Zài biéde dìfang jiǎn tóufa, zhǐ shì jiǎn tóufa. Zài zhèlǐ, shì huí jiā.」
text_en: Near my home there's an old alley with a barbershop that has only two chairs. The barber is a man in his sixties who only knows two hairstyles: short and shorter. He can't dye hair or do the trendy styles young people like. But he has plenty of customers every day, because a haircut costs only fifteen yuan, and he's an exceptional conversationalist. No matter what topic you bring up, he can keep the conversation going. Some say he's the best-informed person in the whole alley. He treats every customer like an old friend, remembering their names, jobs, even where their children go to school. Although many new, fancy barbershops have opened nearby, the old customers still prefer to come here. They say: 'Getting a haircut somewhere else is just getting a haircut. Coming here is coming home.'

questions.q_zh: 这篇文章的结构安排说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the structural arrangement of this passage suggest?

options.text: 作者有意引导读者的情感
options.pinyin: zuòzhě yǒuyì yǐndǎo dúzhě de qínggǎn
options.text_en: the author deliberately guides the reader's emotions

questions.explanation: The key is "Near my home there's an old alley with a barbershop" — read it with the answer in mind.

questions.q_zh: 为什么还是有很多客人来？
questions.q_en: Why do many customers still come?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā de jìshù zuì hǎo
options.text_en: because his skills are the best

options.text: 因为便宜而且他特别会聊天
options.pinyin: yīnwèi piányi érqiě tā tèbié huì liáotiān
options.text_en: cheap and he's great at chatting

options.text: 因为巷子里没有别的理发店
options.pinyin: yīnwèi xiàngzi lǐ méiyǒu biéde lǐfàdiàn
options.text_en: no other barbershops in the alley

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā de diàn hěn piàoliang

questions.explanation: The why is in the details: "He has plenty of customers every day, because a haircut."

questions.q_zh: 老客人说在这里剪头发是什么感觉？
questions.q_en: What do old customers say getting a haircut here feels like?
questions.explanation: The emotion comes through in choices like "Coming here is coming home.'."

title: The Hospital Garden
text_zh: 这家医院的后面有一个小花园，是专门给病人散步用的。花园不大，但是被照顾得很好。护士长说，这个花园是二十年前一个老医生提议建的，因为他觉得只靠药是治不好所有病的，病人也需要阳光和新鲜空气。花园里种了不同季节开花的植物，所以无论什么时候来，都能看到花。每天下午两点到四点，病人们被允许到花园里走一走。有的病人坐在轮椅上，被家人推着慢慢转。有的病人自己拄着拐杖，沿着小路一步一步地走。这个花园虽然很小，但是对很多病人来说，它是整个医院里最像「外面世界」的地方。
text_pinyin: Zhè jiā yīyuàn de hòumiàn yǒu yí gè xiǎo huāyuán, shì zhuānmén gěi bìngrén sànbù yòng de. Huāyuán bú dà, dànshì bèi zhàogù de hěn hǎo. Hùshizhǎng shuō, zhège huāyuán shì èrshí nián qián yí gè lǎo yīshēng tíyì jiàn de, yīnwèi tā juéde zhǐ kào yào shì zhì bù hǎo suǒyǒu bìng de, bìngrén yě xūyào yángguāng hé xīnxiān kōngqì. Huāyuán lǐ zhǒng le bùtóng jìjié kāi huā de zhíwù, suǒyǐ wúlùn shénme shíhou lái, dōu néng kàn dào huā. Měi tiān xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn dào sì diǎn, bìngrénmen bèi yǔnxǔ dào huāyuán lǐ zǒu yì zǒu. Yǒude bìngrén zuò zài lúnyǐ shàng, bèi jiārén tuīzhe mànmàn zhuǎn. Yǒude bìngrén zìjǐ zhǔzhe guǎizhàng, yánzhe xiǎo lù yí bù yí bù de zǒu. Zhège huāyuán suīrán hěn xiǎo, dànshì duì hěn duō bìngrén lái shuō, tā shì zhěnggè yīyuàn lǐ zuì xiàng「wàimiàn shìjiè」de dìfang.
text_en: Behind this hospital there's a small garden made specifically for patients to take walks. The garden isn't large, but it's well cared for. The head nurse says it was proposed twenty years ago by an old doctor who believed medicine alone can't cure every illness — patients also need sunlight and fresh air. The garden has plants that bloom in different seasons, so no matter when you visit, you can see flowers. Every afternoon from two to four, patients are allowed to walk in the garden. Some sit in wheelchairs, slowly pushed around by family members. Others walk on their own with canes, step by step along the path. Although this garden is small, for many patients it's the place in the whole hospital that most resembles the 'outside world.'

questions.q_zh: 花园是谁提议建的？
questions.q_en: Who proposed building the garden?
questions.explanation: The key is "The head nurse says it was proposed twenty years ago" — read it with the answer in mind.

questions.q_zh: 那个老医生觉得病人除了药还需要什么？
questions.q_en: What did the old doctor think patients need besides medicine?
questions.explanation: Go back to "Patients also need sunlight and fresh air." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中最关键的转折是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most important turning point in this passage?
questions.explanation: "Behind this hospital there's a small garden made specifically" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

title: The Note in the Book
text_zh: 我在图书馆借了一本旧小说，打开的时候发现里面夹着一张纸条。纸条上用蓝色的笔写着：「如果你也喜欢这本书，请在第83页留下你的名字。」我很好奇地翻到第83页，发现页边上已经有三个名字了，分别写着不同颜色的笔。第一个名字旁边写着2019年，最后一个写着2024年。我想了想，也把自己的名字写了上去。然后我把那张纸条放回原来的位置，把书还给了图书馆。我不知道下一个借这本书的人什么时候会来，也不知道他们会不会翻到第83页。但是一想到有一天，一个陌生人会看到我的名字，我就觉得这件事比这本书本身还有意思。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài túshūguǎn jiè le yì běn jiù xiǎoshuō, dǎkāi de shíhou fāxiàn lǐmiàn jiāzhe yì zhāng zhǐtiáo. Zhǐtiáo shàng yòng lán sè de bǐ xiězhe: 「Rúguǒ nǐ yě xǐhuan zhè běn shū, qǐng zài dì bāshísān yè liú xià nǐ de míngzi.」 Wǒ hěn hàoqí de fān dào dì bāshísān yè, fāxiàn yè biān shàng yǐjīng yǒu sān gè míngzi le, fēnbié xiězhe bùtóng yánsè de bǐ. Dì yí gè míngzi pángbiān xiězhe èr líng yī jiǔ nián, zuìhòu yí gè xiězhe èr líng èr sì nián. Wǒ xiǎng le xiǎng, yě bǎ zìjǐ de míngzi xiě le shàngqu. Ránhòu wǒ bǎ nà zhāng zhǐtiáo fàng huí yuánlái de wèizhì, bǎ shū huán gěi le túshūguǎn. Wǒ bù zhīdào xià yí gè jiè zhè běn shū de rén shénme shíhou huì lái, yě bù zhīdào tāmen huì bu huì fān dào dì bāshísān yè. Dànshì yì xiǎng dào yǒu yì tiān, yí gè mòshēngrén huì kàn dào wǒ de míngzi, wǒ jiù juéde zhè jiàn shì bǐ zhè běn shū běnshēn hái yǒu yìsi.
text_en: I borrowed an old novel from the library and found a note tucked inside when I opened it. Written in blue pen, the note said: 'If you also like this book, please leave your name on page 83.' Curious, I turned to page 83 and found three names already in the margin, each written in a different colored pen. The first name had '2019' written beside it; the last one, '2024.' After a moment's thought, I wrote my name there too. Then I put the note back in its place and returned the book to the library. I don't know when the next person will borrow this book, or whether they'll turn to page 83. But just thinking that someday a stranger will see my name — I find that more interesting than the book itself.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: "I borrowed an old novel from the library and found" — two things pulling against each other.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么样的变化？
questions.q_en: What kind of change does this passage suggest?
questions.explanation: "I borrowed an old novel from the library and found" gives you what you need.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: "I borrowed an old novel from the library and found" — two things pulling against each other.

title: The Dialect
text_zh: 我在北京工作了五年，普通话说得已经很标准了。但是每次跟老家的妈妈打电话，我还是会自动切换到方言。这两种语言像是两个不同的频道：说普通话的时候，我是一个在大城市工作的职业女性；说方言的时候，我又变成了那个在小镇长大的女孩。有时候在办公室接到妈妈的电话，我会不好意思地走到走廊里去说。不是因为觉得方言丢人，而是因为方言里的我太真实了——会撒娇、会抱怨、会像小孩一样说话。同事们都说我打电话的时候声音变了，变得又软又甜。我想，也许方言不只是一种语言，它是一条回家的路。只要我还会说方言，家就不远。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài Běijīng gōngzuò le wǔ nián, pǔtōnghuà shuō de yǐjīng hěn biāozhǔn le. Dànshì měi cì gēn lǎojiā de māma dǎ diànhuà, wǒ háishi huì zìdòng qiēhuàn dào fāngyán. Zhè liǎng zhǒng yǔyán xiàng shì liǎng gè bùtóng de píndào: shuō pǔtōnghuà de shíhou, wǒ shì yí gè zài dà chéngshì gōngzuò de zhíyè nǚxìng; shuō fāngyán de shíhou, wǒ yòu biàn chéng le nàge zài xiǎo zhèn zhǎng dà de nǚhái. Yǒu shíhou zài bàngōngshì jiē dào māma de diànhuà, wǒ huì bù hǎoyìsi de zǒu dào zǒuláng lǐ qù shuō. Búshì yīnwèi juéde fāngyán diūrén, érshì yīnwèi fāngyán lǐ de wǒ tài zhēnshí le——huì sājiāo, huì bàoyuàn, huì xiàng xiǎohái yíyàng shuō huà. Tóngshìmen dōu shuō wǒ dǎ diànhuà de shíhou shēngyīn biàn le, biàn de yòu ruǎn yòu tián. Wǒ xiǎng, yěxǔ fāngyán bù zhǐ shì yì zhǒng yǔyán, tā shì yì tiáo huí jiā de lù. Zhǐyào wǒ hái huì shuō fāngyán, jiā jiù bù yuǎn.
text_en: I've worked in Beijing for five years and my Mandarin is quite standard now. But every time I call my mom back home, I automatically switch to dialect. These two languages are like two different channels: when speaking Mandarin, I'm a professional woman working in a big city; when speaking dialect, I become that girl who grew up in a small town again. Sometimes when I get a call from my mom at the office, I sheepishly walk to the hallway to talk. Not because I think dialect is embarrassing, but because the me in dialect is too real — I act cute, I complain, I talk like a child. My colleagues all say my voice changes when I'm on the phone — it becomes soft and sweet. I think maybe dialect isn't just a language. It's a road home. As long as I can still speak my dialect, home isn't far away.

questions.q_en: What language does she use when calling her mom?

options.pinyin: yǒu shí pǔtōnghuà yǒu shí fāngyán

questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "I automatically switch to dialect."

title: Grandmother's Dumplings
text_zh: 每年冬至，全家人都会回奶奶家包饺子。奶奶把馅儿调好以后，大家围着桌子坐下来，一边包一边聊天。奶奶包的饺子又快又好看，像一个个小元宝。我包的就不太好看了，总是被大家笑。但是奶奶说：「不好看没关系，只要是自己包的，就比外面买的好吃。」每次奶奶都会在几个饺子里偷偷放一颗花生米。谁吃到了花生米饺子，新的一年就会特别幸运。虽然大家都知道这只是一个小游戏，但是每个人咬饺子的时候都会特别认真，希望自己就是那个幸运的人。今年的花生米饺子被我弟弟吃到了，他高兴得把筷子都掉了。
text_pinyin: Měi nián dōngzhì, quán jiā rén dōu huì huí nǎinai jiā bāo jiǎozi. Nǎinai bǎ xiànr tiáo hǎo yǐhòu, dàjiā wéizhe zhuōzi zuò xiàlái, yìbiān bāo yìbiān liáotiān. Nǎinai bāo de jiǎozi yòu kuài yòu hǎokàn, xiàng yí gè gè xiǎo yuánbǎo. Wǒ bāo de jiù bú tài hǎokàn le, zǒng shì bèi dàjiā xiào. Dànshì nǎinai shuō: 「Bù hǎokàn méi guānxi, zhǐyào shì zìjǐ bāo de, jiù bǐ wàimiàn mǎi de hǎochī.」 Měi cì nǎinai dōu huì zài jǐ gè jiǎozi lǐ tōutōu fàng yì kē huāshēngmǐ. Shéi chī dào le huāshēngmǐ jiǎozi, xīn de yì nián jiù huì tèbié xìngyùn. Suīrán dàjiā dōu zhīdào zhè zhǐ shì yí gè xiǎo yóuxì, dànshì měi gè rén yǎo jiǎozi de shíhou dōu huì tèbié rènzhēn, xīwàng zìjǐ jiù shì nàge xìngyùn de rén. Jīnnián de huāshēngmǐ jiǎozi bèi wǒ dìdi chī dào le, tā gāoxìng de bǎ kuàizi dōu diào le.
text_en: Every winter solstice, the whole family goes back to grandmother's house to make dumplings. After grandmother seasons the filling, everyone sits around the table, wrapping and chatting. Grandmother's dumplings are fast and beautiful, like little gold ingots. Mine don't look so great — everyone always laughs at them. But grandmother says: 'It doesn't matter if they're ugly. As long as you made them yourself, they taste better than store-bought.' Every time, grandmother secretly puts a peanut in a few dumplings. Whoever bites into a peanut dumpling will have especially good luck in the new year. Although everyone knows it's just a little game, each person bites into their dumplings with great seriousness, hoping to be the lucky one. This year my little brother got the peanut dumpling — he was so happy he dropped his chopsticks.

questions.q_zh: 奶奶包的饺子像什么？
questions.q_en: What do grandmother's dumplings look like?
questions.explanation: "Grandmother's dumplings are fast and beautiful, like little gold ingots" — the parallel is stated, not implied.

questions.q_zh: 奶奶在饺子里放了什么？
questions.q_en: What did grandmother put in some dumplings?
questions.explanation: Focus on "As long as you made them yourself, they taste better" — the rest follows.

questions.q_zh: 弟弟吃到花生米饺子以后怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened when the younger brother got the peanut dumpling?

options.text: 他高兴得把筷子都掉了
options.pinyin: tā gāoxìng de bǎ kuàizi dōu diào le
options.text_en: he was so happy he dropped his chopsticks

questions.explanation: "He was so happy he dropped his chopsticks" gives you what you need.

title: The Slow Train
text_zh: 现在大家都坐高铁，但是在中国的很多山区，还有一种特别慢的绿皮火车。这种火车不但速度慢，而且站站都停。我第一次坐这种火车是去年夏天，从一个小城市到另一个小城市，坐了整整八个小时。车厢里没有空调，窗户都开着，风吹进来带着山里的味道。坐在旁边的大爷把自己带的花生分给了我，一边嗑一边跟我聊他年轻时候的故事。火车经过一个个小村庄的时候，我看到田里有人在干活，小路上有孩子在跑。这些风景是高铁窗外看不到的。虽然坐了很久，但我一点都不觉得无聊。有些路就是应该慢慢走的，因为走得太快，你会错过路上最好的风景。
text_pinyin: Xiànzài dàjiā dōu zuò gāotiě, dànshì zài Zhōngguó de hěn duō shānqū, hái yǒu yì zhǒng tèbié màn de lǜpí huǒchē. Zhè zhǒng huǒchē búdàn sùdù màn, érqiě zhàn zhàn dōu tíng. Wǒ dì yī cì zuò zhè zhǒng huǒchē shì qùnián xiàtiān, cóng yí gè xiǎo chéngshì dào lìng yí gè xiǎo chéngshì, zuò le zhěngzhěng bā gè xiǎoshí. Chēxiāng lǐ méiyǒu kōngtiáo, chuānghu dōu kāizhe, fēng chuī jìnlái dàizhe shān lǐ de wèidao. Zuò zài pángbiān de dàyé bǎ zìjǐ dài de huāshēng fēn gěi le wǒ, yìbiān kē yìbiān gēn wǒ liáo tā niánqīng shíhou de gùshi. Huǒchē jīngguò yí gè gè xiǎo cūnzhuāng de shíhou, wǒ kàn dào tián lǐ yǒu rén zài gàn huó, xiǎo lù shàng yǒu háizi zài pǎo. Zhèxiē fēngjǐng shì gāotiě chuāng wài kàn bu dào de. Suīrán zuò le hěn jiǔ, dàn wǒ yìdiǎn dōu bù juéde wúliáo. Yǒuxiē lù jiù shì yīnggāi mànmàn zǒu de, yīnwèi zǒu de tài kuài, nǐ huì cuòguò lù shàng zuì hǎo de fēngjǐng.
text_en: Nowadays everyone takes the high-speed rail, but in many mountainous areas of China, there's still a kind of especially slow green-skinned train. These trains are not only slow but stop at every station. I took this kind of train for the first time last summer, traveling from one small city to another — a full eight hours. The carriage had no air conditioning, all windows open, with wind blowing in carrying the scent of the mountains. The old man sitting next to me shared his peanuts, cracking them while telling me stories from his youth. As the train passed through small villages one by one, I saw people working in the fields and children running on the paths. These are scenes you can't see from a high-speed train window. Although the ride was long, I wasn't bored at all. Some roads are meant to be traveled slowly, because if you go too fast, you'll miss the best scenery along the way.

questions.q_en: What space for thought does this passage leave the reader?

options.text_en: reflection on personal value and meaning

options.pinyin: rúhé zhuàn gèng duō de qián

questions.explanation: Through "Nowadays everyone takes the high-speed rail, but in many mountainous," the author nudges you toward reflection.

questions.q_zh: 旁边的大爷做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the old man sitting next to 'I' do?

options.pinyin: fēn huāshēng gěi wǒ, gēn wǒ liáotiān

questions.explanation: The key is "The old man sitting next to me shared his peanuts" — read it with the answer in mind.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得有些路应该怎么走？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' think some roads should be traveled?
questions.explanation: Follow the action in "Some roads are meant to be traveled slowly, because if" — that's how.

title: The Window Cleaner
text_zh: 我办公室在十二楼，每个月都有人来擦外面的窗户。擦窗户的工人被一根绳子吊在半空中，从上往下慢慢地擦。每次他出现在我的窗户外面，都会把我吓一跳。他看起来一点都不害怕，有时候还会朝里面的人微笑。他把每一块玻璃都擦得特别干净，连角落里的灰尘都不放过。擦完以后，阳光照进来变得更亮了，整个办公室的心情好像都变好了。有一次我打开窗户跟他说话，他说他已经做这个工作十年了。我问他怕不怕高，他说：「刚开始怕，后来就不怕了。往下看的时候，城市其实很美。你们天天在里面，反而看不到。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ bàngōngshì zài shí'èr lóu, měi gè yuè dōu yǒu rén lái cā wàimiàn de chuānghu. Cā chuānghu de gōngrén bèi yì gēn shéngzi diào zài bàn kōng zhōng, cóng shàng wǎng xià mànmàn de cā. Měi cì tā chūxiàn zài wǒ de chuānghu wàimiàn, dōu huì bǎ wǒ xià yí tiào. Tā kàn qǐlái yìdiǎn dōu bú hàipà, yǒu shíhou hái huì cháo lǐmiàn de rén wēixiào. Tā bǎ měi yí kuài bōli dōu cā de tèbié gānjìng, lián jiǎoluò lǐ de huīchén dōu bú fàngguò. Cā wán yǐhòu, yángguāng zhào jìnlái biàn de gèng liàng le, zhěnggè bàngōngshì de xīnqíng hǎoxiàng dōu biàn hǎo le. Yǒu yí cì wǒ dǎkāi chuānghu gēn tā shuō huà, tā shuō tā yǐjīng zuò zhège gōngzuò shí nián le. Wǒ wèn tā pà bu pà gāo, tā shuō: 「Gāng kāishǐ pà, hòulái jiù bú pà le. Wǎng xià kàn de shíhou, chéngshì qíshí hěn měi. Nǐmen tiāntiān zài lǐmiàn, fǎn'ér kàn bu dào.」
text_en: My office is on the twelfth floor, and every month someone comes to clean the outside of the windows. The window cleaner hangs in midair from a rope, slowly wiping from top to bottom. Every time he appears outside my window, I get a fright. He doesn't look scared at all — sometimes he even smiles at the people inside. He cleans every piece of glass spotlessly, not missing even the dust in the corners. After he's done, the sunlight that comes in is brighter, and the whole office's mood seems to lift. Once I opened the window to talk to him. He said he's been doing this job for ten years. I asked if he's afraid of heights. He said: 'Scared at first, then not anymore. When you look down, the city is actually beautiful. You're inside it every day, so you can't see it.'

questions.q_zh: 擦窗户的工人怎么工作？
questions.q_en: How does the window cleaner work?

options.pinyin: bèi shéngzi diào zài bàn kōng zhōng
options.text_en: hung in midair by a rope

questions.explanation: "The window cleaner hangs in midair from a rope, slowly" walks you through it.

questions.q_en: What does the way this passage handles 'time' suggest?

options.pinyin: shíjiān bù zhǐ shì kèguān de liúshì
options.text_en: time is more than objective passing

questions.explanation: "Every time he appears outside my window" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 擦完窗户以后，办公室有什么变化？
questions.q_en: What changes in the office after the windows are cleaned?

options.text: 阳光更亮了，心情也变好了
options.pinyin: yángguāng gèng liàng le, xīnqíng yě biàn hǎo le

questions.explanation: Go back to "After he's done, the sunlight that comes in is brighter." That's where the answer lives.

title: The Old Man's Radio
text_zh: 隔壁的张爷爷每天早上都听收音机。声音不大，但是因为墙壁比较薄，我总是能听到。六点是新闻，七点是京剧，八点是天气预报。他的生活好像完全跟着收音机的节目表在走。我搬来这里三年了，从来没有听到他换过台。有时候我出门上班的时候，会在楼道里碰到他。他穿着一件旧毛衣，手里拿着一个搪瓷杯子，里面泡着浓浓的茶。他总是朝我点点头，不太说话。有一天收音机突然没有声音了，我有一点担心，就去敲了他的门。原来是收音机坏了。我帮他修好以后，他很高兴地说：「没有它的声音，屋子里太安静了，我不习惯。」那一刻我才明白，对他来说，收音机不只是收音机，是陪伴。
text_pinyin: Gébì de Zhāng yéye měi tiān zǎoshang dōu tīng shōuyīnjī. Shēngyīn bú dà, dànshì yīnwèi qiángbì bǐjiào báo, wǒ zǒng shì néng tīng dào. Liù diǎn shì xīnwén, qī diǎn shì jīngjù, bā diǎn shì tiānqì yùbào. Tā de shēnghuó hǎoxiàng wánquán gēnzhe shōuyīnjī de jiémùbiǎo zài zǒu. Wǒ bān lái zhèlǐ sān nián le, cónglái méiyǒu tīng dào tā huàn guò tái. Yǒu shíhou wǒ chūmén shàngbān de shíhou, huì zài lóudào lǐ pèng dào tā. Tā chuānzhe yí jiàn jiù máoyī, shǒu lǐ názhe yí gè tángcí bēizi, lǐmiàn pàozhe nóngnóng de chá. Tā zǒng shì cháo wǒ diǎndiǎn tóu, bú tài shuō huà. Yǒu yì tiān shōuyīnjī tūrán méiyǒu shēngyīn le, wǒ yǒu yìdiǎn dānxīn, jiù qù qiāo le tā de mén. Yuánlái shì shōuyīnjī huài le. Wǒ bāng tā xiū hǎo yǐhòu, tā hěn gāoxìng de shuō: 「Méiyǒu tā de shēngyīn, wūzi lǐ tài ānjìng le, wǒ bù xíguàn.」 Nà yí kè wǒ cái míngbai, duì tā lái shuō, shōuyīnjī bù zhǐ shì shōuyīnjī, shì péibàn.
text_en: Old Mr. Zhang next door listens to the radio every morning. The volume isn't loud, but because the walls are thin, I can always hear it. Six o'clock is news, seven is Beijing opera, eight is the weather forecast. His life seems to follow the radio schedule entirely. I've lived here three years and never heard him change the station. Sometimes when I leave for work, I run into him in the hallway. He wears an old sweater, holding an enamel cup filled with strong tea. He always nods at me, doesn't say much. One day the radio suddenly went silent. I was a bit worried, so I knocked on his door. Turns out the radio had broken. After I helped fix it, he said happily: 'Without its sound, the room is too quiet — I'm not used to it.' Then I understood: for him, the radio isn't just a radio. It's companionship.

questions.q_zh: 作者通过叙述想引发读者什么思考？
questions.q_en: What reflection does the author want to provoke in the reader?

options.text: 对日常生活更深的理解
options.pinyin: duì rìcháng shēnghuó gèng shēn de lǐjiě

questions.explanation: Moments like "His life seems to follow the radio schedule entirely" reach beyond the page.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么去敲张爷爷的门？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' knock on Grandpa Zhang's door?

options.text: 收音机突然没声音了，有点担心
options.pinyin: shōuyīnjī tūrán méi shēngyīn le, yǒudiǎn dānxīn
options.text_en: radio went silent, was a bit worried

questions.explanation: The why is in the details: "I was a bit worried, so I knocked."

questions.q_zh: 这段话中最关键的转折是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most important turning point in this passage?
questions.explanation: Focus on "Zhang next door listens to the radio every morning" — the rest follows.

title: The Stray Cat Colony
text_zh: 我们小区的垃圾房旁边住着一群流浪猫。它们白天躲在各种角落里睡觉，到了晚上才出来活动。小区里有几个人会定时去喂它们，其中一个是三楼的王阿姨。她每天晚上七点准时下楼，把猫粮倒在几个纸盘子里。猫们已经认识她了，只要听到她的脚步声，就会从各个方向跑过来。王阿姨给每只猫都起了名字，还会跟它们说话。邻居们对流浪猫的态度不太一样：有的人觉得它们很可爱，有的人觉得它们把小区弄脏了。但是王阿姨说：「它们也是住在这里的，只是没有人给它们发门卡而已。」这句话被邻居们传来传去，后来反对的声音就越来越少了。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū de lājī fáng pángbiān zhùzhe yì qún liúlàng māo. Tāmen báitiān duǒ zài gè zhǒng jiǎoluò lǐ shuìjiào, dào le wǎnshang cái chūlái huódòng. Xiǎoqū lǐ yǒu jǐ gè rén huì dìngshí qù wèi tāmen, qízhōng yí gè shì sān lóu de Wáng āyí. Tā měi tiān wǎnshang qī diǎn zhǔnshí xià lóu, bǎ māoliáng dào zài jǐ gè zhǐ pánzi lǐ. Māomen yǐjīng rènshi tā le, zhǐyào tīng dào tā de jiǎobù shēng, jiù huì cóng gè gè fāngxiàng pǎo guòlái. Wáng āyí gěi měi zhī māo dōu qǐ le míngzi, hái huì gēn tāmen shuō huà. Línjūmen duì liúlàng māo de tàidù bú tài yíyàng: yǒude rén juéde tāmen hěn kě'ài, yǒude rén juéde tāmen bǎ xiǎoqū nòng zāng le. Dànshì Wáng āyí shuō: 「Tāmen yě shì zhù zài zhèlǐ de, zhǐ shì méiyǒu rén gěi tāmen fā ménkǎ éryǐ.」 Zhè jù huà bèi línjūmen chuán lái chuán qù, hòulái fǎnduì de shēngyīn jiù yuè lái yuè shǎo le.
text_en: A group of stray cats lives near the garbage room in our compound. During the day they hide and sleep in various corners; they only come out to move around at night. A few people in the compound feed them on a regular schedule. One of them is Auntie Wang from the third floor. Every evening at seven sharp, she goes downstairs and pours cat food onto paper plates. The cats already know her — as soon as they hear her footsteps, they come running from all directions. Auntie Wang has named every cat and even talks to them. Neighbors have different attitudes toward the strays: some think they're cute, others think they make the compound dirty. But Auntie Wang says: 'They live here too — it's just that nobody gave them an access card.' That line got passed around among the neighbors, and after that, the complaints grew fewer and fewer.

title: The Night Market Closes
text_zh: 晚上十一点，夜市开始收摊了。卖烧烤的老板把火熄灭了，把烤架搬上小货车。卖衣服的阿姨把没卖完的衣服一件一件叠好，放进大袋子里。地上到处都是一次性筷子和塑料袋，但是不用担心——一会儿清洁工就会来。这个时候的夜市跟三个小时前完全不一样：从热热闹闹变成了空空荡荡。最后离开的总是那个卖豆腐脑的大爷，他要把大锅里剩下的豆腐脑倒掉，然后把锅洗干净。他做完这些的时候，其他摊位都已经走了。他把三轮车慢慢骑走的时候，整条街就只剩下路灯的光了。我有时候加班到很晚回来，刚好能看到收摊的最后几分钟。这也是城市的一种表情，只有少数人看得到。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shíyī diǎn, yèshì kāishǐ shōu tān le. Mài shāokǎo de lǎobǎn bǎ huǒ xīmiè le, bǎ kǎojià bān shàng xiǎo huòchē. Mài yīfu de āyí bǎ méi mài wán de yīfu yí jiàn yí jiàn dié hǎo, fàng jìn dà dàizi lǐ. Dì shàng dàochù dōu shì yícìxìng kuàizi hé sùliào dài, dànshì búyòng dānxīn——yíhuìr qīngjié gōng jiù huì lái. Zhège shíhou de yèshì gēn sān gè xiǎoshí qián wánquán bù yíyàng: cóng rèrè-nàonào biàn chéng le kōngkōng-dàngdàng. Zuìhòu líkāi de zǒng shì nàge mài dòufunǎo de dàyé, tā yào bǎ dà guō lǐ shèng xià de dòufunǎo dào diào, ránhòu bǎ guō xǐ gānjìng. Tā zuò wán zhèxiē de shíhou, qítā tānwèi dōu yǐjīng zǒu le. Tā bǎ sānlúnchē mànmàn qí zǒu de shíhou, zhěng tiáo jiē jiù zhǐ shèng xià lùdēng de guāng le. Wǒ yǒu shíhou jiābān dào hěn wǎn huílái, gānghǎo néng kàn dào shōu tān de zuìhòu jǐ fēnzhōng. Zhè yě shì chéngshì de yì zhǒng biǎoqíng, zhǐ yǒu shǎoshù rén kàn de dào.
text_en: At eleven at night, the night market begins packing up. The barbecue boss extinguishes the fire and loads the grill onto his small truck. The clothing vendor folds each unsold garment one by one and puts them into big bags. The ground is littered with disposable chopsticks and plastic bags, but no need to worry — the cleaners will come soon. The night market at this hour is completely different from three hours ago: from bustling to utterly empty. The last to leave is always the old man who sells tofu pudding. He has to pour out what's left in his big pot and then wash it clean. By the time he finishes, all the other stalls have already gone. When he slowly pedals his tricycle away, the whole street is left with nothing but the light of the streetlamps. Sometimes when I work overtime and come home late, I catch the last few minutes of the closing.

title: The Umbrella by the Door
text_zh: 我家门口的伞架上放着七把伞，但是只有三把是我的。另外四把是被邻居们忘在这里的。有一把是上个月隔壁的李先生来借盐的时候忘的。有一把是快递员送包裹的时候放在门口的，大概被雨淋怕了。还有两把不知道是谁的，已经在那里放了好几个月了。我想把它们还给主人，但是不知道应该还给谁。我试过在电梯里贴了一张纸条：「门口有几把伞，不知道是不是你的？」结果没有人来拿。也许那些伞的主人已经把它们忘了，也许他们已经买了新的。不管怎样，那些伞就这样留在了我家门口，像几个安安静静的客人，不打扰我，也不离开。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā ménkǒu de sǎnjià shàng fàngzhe qī bǎ sǎn, dànshì zhǐ yǒu sān bǎ shì wǒ de. Lìngwài sì bǎ shì bèi línjūmen wàng zài zhèlǐ de. Yǒu yì bǎ shì shàng gè yuè gébì de Lǐ xiānsheng lái jiè yán de shíhou wàng de. Yǒu yì bǎ shì kuàidìyuán sòng bāoguǒ de shíhou fàng zài ménkǒu de, dàgài bèi yǔ lín pà le. Hái yǒu liǎng bǎ bù zhīdào shì shéi de, yǐjīng zài nàlǐ fàng le hǎo jǐ gè yuè le. Wǒ xiǎng bǎ tāmen huán gěi zhǔrén, dànshì bù zhīdào yīnggāi huán gěi shéi. Wǒ shì guò zài diàntī lǐ tiē le yì zhāng zhǐtiáo: 「Ménkǒu yǒu jǐ bǎ sǎn, bù zhīdào shì bu shì nǐ de?」 Jiéguǒ méiyǒu rén lái ná. Yěxǔ nàxiē sǎn de zhǔrén yǐjīng bǎ tāmen wàng le, yěxǔ tāmen yǐjīng mǎi le xīn de. Bùguǎn zěnyàng, nàxiē sǎn jiù zhèyàng liú zài le wǒ jiā ménkǒu, xiàng jǐ gè ān'ānjìngjìng de kèrén, bù dǎrǎo wǒ, yě bù líkāi.
text_en: On the umbrella stand by my door there are seven umbrellas, but only three are mine. The other four were left here by neighbors. One was forgotten by Mr. Li next door when he came to borrow salt last month. One was left at my door by a delivery person while dropping off a package — probably scared of the rain. Two more belong to who-knows-whom and have been there for several months. I wanted to return them to their owners but didn't know whom to give them to. I tried posting a note in the elevator: 'There are some umbrellas by my door — are any of them yours?' Nobody came to claim them. Perhaps those umbrellas' owners have already forgotten them, or perhaps they've already bought new ones. Either way, the umbrellas have stayed by my door, like a few quiet guests who neither disturb me nor leave.

title: The Post Office Counter
text_zh: 我家附近有一个很老的邮局，里面只有两个窗口。左边的窗口负责寄包裹，右边的负责汇款和其他业务。现在大家都用快递和手机转账了，来邮局的人很少。但是每个星期三下午，总有几个老人来寄信。他们把信封上的地址写得很认真，一笔一画的。有一个老人每次都寄两封信，一封寄给在美国的儿子，一封寄给在上海的女儿。柜台后面的工作人员说，其实那个老人的儿女都跟他说了很多次「用微信就好了」，但是老人觉得只有收到手写的信，才能感受到他的心意。邮局虽然安静得有些冷清，但是它被这些老人的坚持温暖着。只要还有人寄信，邮局就不会关门。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā fùjìn yǒu yí gè hěn lǎo de yóujú, lǐmiàn zhǐ yǒu liǎng gè chuāngkǒu. Zuǒbiān de chuāngkǒu fùzé jì bāoguǒ, yòubiān de fùzé huìkuǎn hé qítā yèwù. Xiànzài dàjiā dōu yòng kuàidì hé shǒujī zhuǎnzhàng le, lái yóujú de rén hěn shǎo. Dànshì měi gè xīngqīsān xiàwǔ, zǒng yǒu jǐ gè lǎorén lái jì xìn. Tāmen bǎ xìnfēng shàng de dìzhǐ xiě de hěn rènzhēn, yì bǐ yí huà de. Yǒu yí gè lǎorén měi cì dōu jì liǎng fēng xìn, yì fēng jì gěi zài Měiguó de érzi, yì fēng jì gěi zài Shànghǎi de nǚ'ér. Guìtái hòumiàn de gōngzuò rényuán shuō, qíshí nàge lǎorén de érnǚ dōu gēn tā shuō le hěn duō cì「yòng Wēixìn jiù hǎo le」, dànshì lǎorén juéde zhǐ yǒu shōu dào shǒuxiě de xìn, cái néng gǎnshòu dào tā de xīnyì. Yóujú suīrán ānjìng de yǒuxiē lěngqīng, dànshì tā bèi zhèxiē lǎorén de jiānchí wēnnuǎnzhe. Zhǐyào hái yǒu rén jì xìn, yóujú jiù bú huì guān mén.
text_en: Near my home there's a old post office with only two counters. The left one handles packages; the right one handles money transfers and other services. Now that everyone uses delivery services and mobile payments, few people come to the post office. But every Wednesday afternoon, a few elderly people come to mail letters. They write the addresses on their envelopes very carefully, stroke by stroke. One old man always mails two letters: one to his son in America, one to his daughter in Shanghai. The staff behind the counter says his children have told him many times 'just use WeChat,' but the old man feels that only a handwritten letter can convey his feelings. Although the post office is quiet to the point of desolation, it's warmed by these old people's persistence. As long as someone still mails letters, the post office won't close.

title: The Piano at Midnight
text_zh: 最近一个月，每到半夜十二点，我都能听到从楼上传来的钢琴声。弹的是同一首曲子，弹得不太好，有几个地方总是弹错。这让我很好奇——谁会在半夜练琴呢？我问了物业，他们说五楼住着一个年轻人，刚搬来不久。我本来想去跟他说半夜弹琴太吵了，但是有一天晚上我仔细听了那首曲子，发现好像是一首婚礼上常用的歌。从那以后，我开始注意到他弹得越来越好了，错的地方越来越少。又过了两个星期，钢琴声突然停了。我再也没有听到过。一个月后的一个周末，我在电梯里碰到了五楼的年轻人，他穿着西装，手里捧着一束花。他旁边站着一个穿白裙子的女生，笑得很开心。
text_pinyin: Zuìjìn yí gè yuè, měi dào bànyè shí'èr diǎn, wǒ dōu néng tīng dào cóng lóu shàng chuán lái de gāngqín shēng. Tán de shì tóng yì shǒu qǔzi, tán de bú tài hǎo, yǒu jǐ gè dìfang zǒng shì tán cuò. Zhè ràng wǒ hěn hàoqí——shéi huì zài bànyè liàn qín ne? Wǒ wèn le wùyè, tāmen shuō wǔ lóu zhùzhe yí gè niánqīngrén, gāng bān lái bùjiǔ. Wǒ běnlái xiǎng qù gēn tā shuō bànyè tán qín tài chǎo le, dànshì yǒu yì tiān wǎnshang wǒ zǐxì tīng le nà shǒu qǔzi, fāxiàn hǎoxiàng shì yì shǒu hūnlǐ shàng cháng yòng de gē. Cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ kāishǐ zhùyì dào tā tán de yuè lái yuè hǎo le, cuò de dìfang yuè lái yuè shǎo. Yòu guò le liǎng gè xīngqī, gāngqín shēng tūrán tíng le. Wǒ zài yě méiyǒu tīng dào guò. Yí gè yuè hòu de yí gè zhōumò, wǒ zài diàntī lǐ pèng dào le wǔ lóu de niánqīngrén, tā chuānzhe xīzhuāng, shǒu lǐ pěngzhe yí shù huā. Tā pángbiān zhànzhe yí gè chuān bái qúnzi de nǚshēng, xiào de hěn kāixīn.
text_en: For the past month, every night at midnight I could hear piano music coming from upstairs. It was always the same piece, played not very well, with certain passages always going wrong. This made me curious — who would practice piano at midnight? I asked property management, and they said a young man had recently moved into the fifth floor. I originally wanted to go tell him playing piano at midnight was too noisy, but one night I listened carefully to the piece and realized it seemed to be a song commonly played at weddings. After that, I noticed he was getting better and better, with fewer and fewer mistakes. Two more weeks passed, and the piano suddenly stopped. I never heard it again. A month later, on a weekend, I ran into the young man from the fifth floor in the elevator. He was wearing a suit, holding a bouquet of flowers. Beside him stood a girl in a white dress, smiling radiantly.
