title: The Cloud That Looked Like a Fish
text_zh: 昨天下午我坐在阳台上看云。有一朵云的形状特别像一条鱼，它慢慢地从东边飘到西边，像在天空里游泳。我叫我女儿出来看，她说：「那不是鱼，是一条龙！」我仔细看了看，好像也有点像龙。过了几分钟，云的形状变了，我女儿说：「现在它变成了一只大鸟！」我们就这样坐在阳台上，看着云一直在变。有的时候它像山，有的时候像花，有的时候什么都不像。我女儿问我：「云为什么一直在变呢？」我说：「因为风在吹它。」她想了一下说：「那如果没有风，云就会一直是鱼了吗？」我不知道怎么回答她。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān xiàwǔ wǒ zuò zài yángtái shàng kàn yún. Yǒu yì duǒ yún de xíngzhuàng tèbié xiàng yì tiáo yú, tā mànmàn de cóng dōngbiān piāo dào xībiān, xiàng zài tiānkōng lǐ yóuyǒng. Wǒ jiào wǒ nǚ'ér chūlái kàn, tā shuō:「Nà búshì yú, shì yì tiáo lóng!」Wǒ zǐxì kàn le kàn, hǎoxiàng yě yǒudiǎn xiàng lóng. Guò le jǐ fēnzhōng, yún de xíngzhuàng biàn le, wǒ nǚ'ér shuō:「Xiànzài tā biàn chéng le yì zhī dà niǎo!」Wǒmen jiù zhèyàng zuò zài yángtái shàng, kàn zhe yún yìzhí zài biàn. Yǒu de shíhou tā xiàng shān, yǒu de shíhou xiàng huā, yǒu de shíhou shénme dōu bú xiàng. Wǒ nǚ'ér wèn wǒ:「Yún wèishénme yìzhí zài biàn ne?」Wǒ shuō:「Yīnwèi fēng zài chuī tā.」Tā xiǎng le yíxià shuō:「Nà rúguǒ méi yǒu fēng, yún jiù huì yìzhí shì yú le ma?」Wǒ bù zhīdào zěnme huídá tā. Yěxǔ zuì hǎo de shìqing jiù shì zhèyàng——bù xūyào dá'àn, zhǐ xūyào yìqǐ kàn zhe.
text_en: Yesterday afternoon I sat on the balcony watching clouds. One cloud was shaped just like a fish, slowly drifting from east to west, as if swimming in the sky. I called my daughter out to look. She said: 'That's not a fish, it's a dragon!' I looked carefully — it did kind of look like a dragon too. A few minutes later the shape changed, and my daughter said: 'Now it's become a big bird!' We sat on the balcony like that, watching the clouds keep changing. Sometimes they looked like mountains, sometimes like flowers, sometimes like nothing at all. My daughter asked: 'Why do clouds keep changing?' I said: 'Because the wind is blowing them.' She thought for a moment: 'Then if there were no wind, would the cloud always be a fish?' I didn't know how to answer.

questions.q_zh: 作者一开始觉得云像什么？
questions.q_en: What did the author first think the cloud looked like?
questions.explanation: Go back to "One cloud was shaped just like a fish, slowly drifting." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 女儿问云为什么一直在变，爸爸怎么回答？
questions.q_en: How did the father answer why clouds keep changing?
questions.explanation: Once you read "Why do clouds keep changing?' I said: 'Because the wind," the cause clicks.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: "Yesterday afternoon I sat on the balcony watching clouds" reveals the how.

title: The Shoes by the Door
text_zh: 我注意到一件有趣的事情：从一个人门口的鞋子，你可以猜出很多关于这个人的信息。我的邻居是一对老夫妻，他们门口总是整整齐齐地放着两双布鞋。楼上住着一个年轻的妈妈，她门口永远有一双大人的鞋和一双小孩的鞋，有时候小鞋是反着放的。走廊尽头的那户人家门口放着好几双运动鞋，看起来都很新。我自己的门口呢？一双旧的跑步鞋，一双拖鞋，还有我太太的凉鞋。每次有客人来，门口就会多出一两双不认识的鞋子。有时候我站在走廊里看着这些鞋子，觉得它们像是每家的名片——不用敲门，你就已经知道了一点关于门后面的人的故事。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhùyì dào yí jiàn yǒuqù de shìqing: cóng yí gè rén ménkǒu de xiézi, nǐ kěyǐ cāi chū hěn duō guānyú zhège rén de xìnxī. Wǒ de línjū shì yí duì lǎo fūqī, tāmen ménkǒu zǒng shì zhěngzhěng qíqí de fàng zhe liǎng shuāng bùxié. Lóu shàng zhù zhe yí gè niánqīng de māma, tā ménkǒu yǒngyuǎn yǒu yì shuāng dàrén de xié hé yì shuāng xiǎohái de xié, yǒu shíhou xiǎo xié shì fǎn zhe fàng de. Zǒuláng jìntóu de nà hù rénjiā ménkǒu fàng zhe hǎo jǐ shuāng yùndòng xié, kàn qǐlái dōu hěn xīn. Wǒ zìjǐ de ménkǒu ne? Yì shuāng jiù de pǎobù xié, yì shuāng tuōxié, háiyǒu wǒ tàitai de liángxié. Měi cì yǒu kèrén lái, ménkǒu jiù huì duō chū yì liǎng shuāng bú rènshi de xiézi. Yǒu shíhou wǒ zhàn zài zǒuláng lǐ kàn zhe zhèxiē xiézi, juéde tāmen xiàng shì měi jiā de míngpiàn——búyòng qiāo mén, nǐ jiù yǐjīng zhīdào le yìdiǎn guānyú mén hòumiàn de rén de gùshi.
text_en: I noticed something interesting: from the shoes at someone's door, you can guess a lot about that person. My neighbors are an elderly couple — their doorstep always has two pairs of cloth shoes neatly placed. Upstairs lives a young mother — her door always has one pair of adult shoes and one pair of children's shoes, sometimes with the small ones placed backwards. The household at the end of the hallway has several pairs of sports shoes, all looking new. And my own doorstep? An old pair of running shoes, slippers, and my wife's sandals. Whenever guests come, one or two unfamiliar pairs of shoes appear at the door. Sometimes I stand in the hallway looking at these shoes, feeling they're like each family's business card — without knocking, you already know a bit of the story behind the door.

questions.q_zh: 老夫妻门口放着什么鞋子？
questions.q_en: What shoes are at the elderly couple's door?
questions.explanation: "Their doorstep always has two pairs of cloth shoes neatly" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 年轻妈妈门口的小鞋子有时候怎么放的？
questions.q_en: How are the young mother's children's shoes sometimes placed?
questions.explanation: "Her door always has one pair of adult shoes" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 重复的行为暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

options.text_en: this matters a lot to them

options.pinyin: tā méiyǒu bié de shì zuò
options.text_en: they have nothing else to do
others require them to do it

questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "I noticed something interesting: from the shoes at someone's door."

title: The Subway Musician
text_zh: 每天早上在地铁站的换乘通道里，有一个拉小提琴的男人。他大概四十多岁，穿着普通的衣服，面前放着一个打开的琴盒。大部分人匆匆忙忙地走过，连看都不看他一眼。但是他拉得真的很好，每次经过他那里，我都会放慢脚步。有一次我停下来听了一整首曲子。拉完以后他看着我笑了笑，我也笑了笑。我往琴盒里放了十块钱。后来我跟一个同事说起这件事，同事说她也注意到他了：「他拉的是巴赫的曲子，水平很高。」我很好奇他为什么在地铁站拉琴，是因为喜欢，还是因为需要？但是我从来没有问过他。有些问题不问也好，留一点想象的空间，反而更美。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang zài dìtiě zhàn de huànchéng tōngdào lǐ, yǒu yí gè lā xiǎotíqín de nánrén. Tā dàgài sìshí duō suì, chuān zhe pǔtōng de yīfu, miànqián fàng zhe yí gè dǎkāi de qínhé. Dà bùfen rén cōngcōng mángmáng de zǒu guò, lián kàn dōu bú kàn tā yì yǎn. Dànshì tā lā de zhēn de hěn hǎo, měi cì jīngguò tā nàlǐ, wǒ dōu huì fàng màn jiǎobù. Yǒu yí cì wǒ tíng xiàlái tīng le yì zhěng shǒu qǔzi. Lā wán yǐhòu tā kàn zhe wǒ xiào le xiào, wǒ yě xiào le xiào. Wǒ wǎng qínhé lǐ fàng le shí kuài qián. Hòulái wǒ gēn yí gè tóngshì shuō qǐ zhè jiàn shì, tóngshì shuō tā yě zhùyì dào tā le:「Tā lā de shì Bāhè de qǔzi, shuǐpíng hěn gāo.」Wǒ hěn hàoqí tā wèishénme zài dìtiě zhàn lā qín, shì yīnwèi xǐhuan, háishi yīnwèi xūyào? Dànshì wǒ cónglái méi yǒu wèn guò tā. Yǒuxiē wèntí bú wèn yě hǎo, liú yìdiǎn xiǎngxiàng de kōngjiān, fǎn'ér gèng měi.
text_en: Every morning in the subway transfer corridor, there's a man playing violin. He's about forty-something, wearing ordinary clothes, with an open violin case in front of him. Most people hurry past without even glancing at him. But he plays really well — every time I pass, I slow down. Once I stopped and listened to an entire piece. After he finished, he looked at me and smiled; I smiled back. I put ten yuan in his case. Later I mentioned this to a colleague, who said she'd noticed him too: 'He plays Bach — his level is very high.' I'm curious why he plays in the subway station — because he loves it, or because he needs to? But I've never asked him. Some questions are better left unasked — leaving a bit of space for imagination is actually more beautiful.

questions.q_en: How old is the violin player approximately?
questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "Every morning in the subway transfer corridor, there's a man."

questions.q_en: Whose music does the colleague say he plays?
questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "He plays Bach — his level is very high.'."

questions.q_en: Why didn't the author ask why he plays in the subway?

options.text_en: felt it's better not to ask

options.pinyin: tīng bu dǒng tā shuō huà

questions.explanation: Once you read "Some questions are better left unasked," the cause clicks.

title: The Rooftop Garden
text_zh: 我们公寓楼的天台上本来什么都没有，只有灰色的水泥地和几根管子。但是六楼的王阿姨把它变成了一个小花园。她用旧轮胎和木箱子做了花盆，种了西红柿、黄瓜、还有一些花。她说她从小在农村长大，搬到城市以后一直想念种东西的感觉。一开始物业管理不同意，但是看到天台变得越来越漂亮，也就不说什么了。现在好几个邻居都会上去帮忙浇水。夏天的时候，西红柿红了，王阿姨就分给大家吃。有一次她给我一个刚摘下来的黄瓜，我咬了一口，味道跟超市里买的完全不一样，特别新鲜。一个小小的天台花园，不但让这栋楼变得更好看，还让邻居之间的关系变得更近了。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen gōngyù lóu de tiāntái shàng běnlái shénme dōu méi yǒu, zhǐ yǒu huī sè de shuǐní dì hé jǐ gēn guǎnzi. Dànshì liù lóu de Wáng āyí bǎ tā biàn chéng le yí gè xiǎo huāyuán. Tā yòng jiù lúntāi hé mù xiāngzi zuò le huāpén, zhòng le xīhóngshì, huángguā, háiyǒu yìxiē huā. Tā shuō tā cóng xiǎo zài nóngcūn zhǎng dà, bān dào chéngshì yǐhòu yìzhí xiǎngniàn zhòng dōngxi de gǎnjué. Yì kāishǐ wùyè guǎnlǐ bù tóngyì, dànshì kàn dào tiāntái biàn de yuè lái yuè piàoliang, yě jiù bù shuō shénme le. Xiànzài hǎo jǐ gè línjū dōu huì shàngqù bāngmáng jiāo shuǐ. Xiàtiān de shíhou, xīhóngshì hóng le, Wáng āyí jiù fēn gěi dàjiā chī. Yǒu yí cì tā gěi wǒ yí gè gāng zhāi xiàlái de huángguā, wǒ yǎo le yì kǒu, wèidao gēn chāoshì lǐ mǎi de wánquán bù yíyàng, tèbié xīnxiān. Yí gè xiǎoxiǎo de tiāntái huāyuán, búdàn ràng zhè dòng lóu biàn de gèng hǎokàn, hái ràng línjū zhījiān de guānxi biàn de gèng jìn le.
text_en: Our apartment rooftop originally had nothing — just gray cement and a few pipes. But Aunt Wang from the sixth floor turned it into a small garden. She made planters from old tires and wooden crates, growing tomatoes, cucumbers, and some flowers. She said she grew up in the countryside and always missed the feeling of growing things after moving to the city. At first the property management didn't agree, but seeing the rooftop becoming more and more beautiful, they stopped objecting. Now several neighbors go up to help water the plants. In summer when the tomatoes turn red, Aunt Wang shares them with everyone. Once she gave me a freshly picked cucumber — I took a bite and the taste was completely different from supermarket ones, especially fresh. A small rooftop garden not only made the building more beautiful, but also brought the neighbors closer together.

questions.q_zh: 王阿姨用什么做花盆？
questions.q_en: What did Aunt Wang use as planters?
questions.explanation: "She made planters from old tires and wooden crates, growing" gives you what you need.

questions.q_zh: 物业管理一开始的态度是什么？
questions.q_en: What was the property management's initial attitude?
questions.explanation: Go back to "At first the property management didn't agree, but seeing." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 天台花园带来了什么好处？
questions.q_en: What benefits did the rooftop garden bring?

options.text: 楼更好看，邻居关系更近
options.pinyin: lóu gèng hǎokàn, línjū guānxi gèng jìn

questions.explanation: Focus on "A small rooftop garden not only made the building more" — the rest follows.

title: The Rule About Eating Together
text_zh: 在我们家有一条规矩：晚饭必须全家人一起吃，吃饭的时候不能看手机。这条规矩是我妈妈定的。她说现在每个人都太忙了，如果连吃饭的时候都不能坐在一起好好聊天，那一家人跟住在同一栋楼里的陌生人有什么区别？一开始我和我姐姐都觉得这个规定太严了，特别是不能看手机这一条。但是时间长了，我发现这段时间真的很珍贵。我爸会讲他今天在公司遇到的有趣的事，我妈会说她学了什么新菜，我姐姐会分享她最近看的书。有时候我们也会吵架，但至少我们在交流。现在我自己有了家庭，我也把这条规矩带到了我自己的家里。我女儿有时候会抱怨，但是我相信她以后会理解的。
text_pinyin: Zài wǒmen jiā yǒu yì tiáo guījǔ: wǎnfàn bìxū quánjiā rén yìqǐ chī, chī fàn de shíhou bù néng kàn shǒujī. Zhè tiáo guījǔ shì wǒ māma dìng de. Tā shuō xiànzài měi gè rén dōu tài máng le, rúguǒ lián chī fàn de shíhou dōu bù néng zuò zài yìqǐ hǎohāo liáo tiān, nà yì jiā rén gēn zhù zài tóng yí dòng lóu lǐ de mòshēng rén yǒu shénme qūbié? Yì kāishǐ wǒ hé wǒ jiějie dōu juéde zhège guīdìng tài yán le, tèbié shì bù néng kàn shǒujī zhè yì tiáo. Dànshì shíjiān cháng le, wǒ fāxiàn zhè duàn shíjiān zhēn de hěn zhēnguì. Wǒ bà huì jiǎng tā jīntiān zài gōngsī yù dào de yǒuqù de shì, wǒ mā huì shuō tā xué le shénme xīn cài, wǒ jiějie huì fēnxiǎng tā zuìjìn kàn de shū. Yǒu shíhou wǒmen yě huì chǎojià, dàn zhìshǎo wǒmen zài jiāoliú. Xiànzài wǒ zìjǐ yǒu le jiātíng, wǒ yě bǎ zhè tiáo guījǔ dài dào le wǒ zìjǐ de jiā lǐ. Wǒ nǚ'ér yǒu shíhou huì bàoyuàn, dànshì wǒ xiāngxìn tā yǐhòu huì lǐjiě de.
text_en: Our family has a rule: dinner must be eaten together by the whole family, and no one can look at their phone during the meal. My mother made this rule. She said everyone is too busy now — if you can't even sit together and chat properly at dinner, what's the difference between a family and strangers living in the same building? At first my sister and I thought this rule was too strict, especially the no-phone part. But over time, I realized this time is truly precious. My dad would share funny things from work, my mom would talk about new dishes she learned, my sister would share books she'd been reading. Sometimes we argued, but at least we were communicating. Now that I have my own family, I've brought this rule to my own home. My daughter complains sometimes, but I believe she'll understand later.

questions.q_zh: 这条规矩是谁定的？
questions.q_en: Who made this rule?
questions.explanation: Go back to "My dad would share funny things from work, my mom." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: "Our family has a rule: dinner must be eaten together" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以看出什么样的关系？
questions.q_en: What kind of relationship does this passage reveal?
questions.explanation: Go back to "Our family has a rule: dinner must be eaten together." That's where the answer lives.

title: The Note in the Library Book
text_zh: 我从图书馆借了一本小说，翻开第一页的时候，发现里面夹着一张小纸条。纸条上用铅笔写着：「如果你也喜欢这本书，请在第 88 页留下你的想法。」我很好奇，就翻到了第 88 页。那一页的空白处写着一行小字：「这个故事让我想到了我的外婆。2023年春天。」下面还有另一行不同的笔迹：「我也是，读到这里的时候哭了。2024年秋天。」我看了看第 88 页的内容——是一段关于一个老人回忆年轻时候的文字。我读完以后，也拿起笔在空白处写了一句话：「2026年冬天。第三个被这一页感动的人。」然后我把纸条放回了第一页。我不知道下一个借这本书的人是谁，但是我希望这本书能继续传递这种安静的连接。
text_pinyin: Wǒ cóng túshūguǎn jiè le yì běn xiǎoshuō, fānkāi dì yī yè de shíhou, fāxiàn lǐmiàn jiā zhe yì zhāng xiǎo zhǐtiáo. Zhǐtiáo shàng yòng qiānbǐ xiě zhe:「Rúguǒ nǐ yě xǐhuan zhè běn shū, qǐng zài dì bāshíbā yè liú xià nǐ de xiǎngfǎ.」Wǒ hěn hàoqí, jiù fān dào le dì bāshíbā yè. Nà yí yè de kòngbái chù xiě zhe yì háng xiǎo zì:「Zhège gùshi ràng wǒ xiǎng dào le wǒ de wàipó. Èr líng èr sān nián chūntiān.」Xiàmiàn háiyǒu lìng yì háng bùtóng de bǐjì:「Wǒ yě shì, dú dào zhèlǐ de shíhou kū le. Èr líng èr sì nián qiūtiān.」Wǒ kàn le kàn dì bāshíbā yè de nèiróng——shì yí duàn guānyú yí gè lǎorén huíyì niánqīng shíhou de wénzì. Wǒ dú wán yǐhòu, yě ná qǐ bǐ zài kòngbái chù xiě le yí jù huà:「Èr líng èr liù nián dōngtiān. Dì sān gè bèi zhè yí yè gǎndòng de rén.」Ránhòu wǒ bǎ zhǐtiáo fàng huí le dì yī yè. Wǒ bù zhīdào xià yí gè jiè zhè běn shū de rén shì shuí, dànshì wǒ xīwàng zhè běn shū néng jìxù chuándì zhè zhǒng ānjìng de liánjiē.
text_en: I borrowed a novel from the library and found a small note tucked inside the first page. In pencil it read: 'If you also like this book, please leave your thoughts on page 88.' Curious, I turned to page 88. In the margin was a line of small writing: 'This story reminds me of my grandmother. Spring 2023.' Below it in different handwriting: 'Me too — I cried when I read this part. Autumn 2024.' I looked at what was on page 88 — a passage about an old person remembering their youth. After reading it, I picked up a pen and wrote in the margin: 'Winter 2026. The third person moved by this page.' Then I put the note back on the first page. I don't know who will borrow this book next, but I hope it continues passing along this quiet connection.

questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

title: The Wrong Number
text_zh: 有一天晚上十点多，我的手机突然响了。我接了以后，听到一个老太太的声音：「小明啊，你吃饭了吗？」我说：「阿姨，您打错了，我不是小明。」她说了声「对不起」就挂了。但是第二天晚上同一个时间，她又打来了：「小明啊……」我又告诉她打错了。第三天她又打来了。这一次我没有马上纠正她，而是说：「吃了，您呢？」她高兴地说了好多话，说她做了红烧肉，说天气变冷了让我多穿衣服。我听了十分钟。挂电话以前她说：「小明，常回来看看啊。」后来她没有再打来过。我不知道小明是谁，为什么不接他妈妈的电话。但是那天晚上，我也想起了很久没有给我自己妈妈打电话了。
text_pinyin: Yǒu yì tiān wǎnshàng shí diǎn duō, wǒ de shǒujī tūrán xiǎng le. Wǒ jiē le yǐhòu, tīng dào yí gè lǎo tàitai de shēngyīn:「Xiǎo Míng a, nǐ chī fàn le ma?」Wǒ shuō:「Āyí, nín dǎ cuò le, wǒ búshì Xiǎo Míng.」Tā shuō le shēng「duìbuqǐ」jiù guà le. Dànshì dì èr tiān wǎnshàng tóng yí gè shíjiān, tā yòu dǎ lái le:「Xiǎo Míng a……」Wǒ yòu gàosu tā dǎ cuò le. Dì sān tiān tā yòu dǎ lái le. Zhè yí cì wǒ méi yǒu mǎshàng jiūzhèng tā, érshì shuō:「Chī le, nín ne?」Tā gāoxìng de shuō le hǎo duō huà, shuō tā zuò le hóngshāo ròu, shuō tiānqì biàn lěng le ràng wǒ duō chuān yīfu. Wǒ tīng le shí fēnzhōng. Guà diànhuà yǐqián tā shuō:「Xiǎo Míng, cháng huílái kànkan a.」Hòulái tā méi yǒu zài dǎ lái guò. Wǒ bù zhīdào Xiǎo Míng shì shuí, wèishénme bù jiē tā māma de diànhuà. Dànshì nà tiān wǎnshàng, wǒ yě xiǎng qǐ le hěn jiǔ méi yǒu gěi wǒ zìjǐ māma dǎ diànhuà le.
text_en: One evening past ten, my phone suddenly rang. I answered and heard an old woman's voice: 'Xiao Ming, have you eaten?' I said: 'Ma'am, you have the wrong number. I'm not Xiao Ming.' She said sorry and hung up. But the next evening at the same time, she called again: 'Xiao Ming...' I told her again it was wrong. The third day she called again. This time I didn't correct her right away. Instead I said: 'I ate, how about you?' She happily said many things — she'd made braised pork, the weather was getting cold and I should wear more clothes. I listened for ten minutes. Before hanging up she said: 'Xiao Ming, come visit more often.' She never called again after that. I don't know who Xiao Ming is, or why he doesn't answer his mother's calls. But that evening, I also remembered I hadn't called my own mother in a long time.

questions.q_en: What time did the old woman first call?

title: The Language I Dream In
text_zh: 我来中国已经三年了。有一天早上醒来，我突然意识到一件奇怪的事情——我昨天晚上做梦的时候说的是中文。以前我做梦都是用英语的，不管梦里的场景在哪里。但是慢慢地，我的梦开始变了。先是梦里的人开始说中文，后来连我自己也开始用中文了。我跟我的中文老师说了这件事，她笑着说：「那说明你真的开始用中文思考了。」这让我想了很久。语言不只是一种工具，它会改变你看世界的方式。用中文的时候，我会注意到一些用英语的时候不会注意到的东西。比如中文里「家」这个字——它既是「家庭」也是「家乡」也是「国家」。一个字就把你跟很多东西连在了一起。也许当你用一种新的语言做梦的时候，你就有了一双新的眼睛。
text_pinyin: Wǒ lái Zhōngguó yǐjīng sān nián le. Yǒu yì tiān zǎoshang xǐng lái, wǒ tūrán yìshí dào yí jiàn qíguài de shìqing——wǒ zuótiān wǎnshàng zuò mèng de shíhou shuō de shì Zhōngwén. Yǐqián wǒ zuò mèng dōu shì yòng Yīngyǔ de, bùguǎn mèng lǐ de chǎngjǐng zài nǎlǐ. Dànshì mànmàn de, wǒ de mèng kāishǐ biàn le. Xiān shì mèng lǐ de rén kāishǐ shuō Zhōngwén, hòulái lián wǒ zìjǐ yě kāishǐ yòng Zhōngwén le. Wǒ gēn wǒ de Zhōngwén lǎoshī shuō le zhè jiàn shì, tā xiào zhe shuō:「Nà shuōmíng nǐ zhēn de kāishǐ yòng Zhōngwén sīkǎo le.」Zhè ràng wǒ xiǎng le hěn jiǔ. Yǔyán bù zhǐ shì yì zhǒng gōngjù, tā huì gǎibiàn nǐ kàn shìjiè de fāngshì. Yòng Zhōngwén de shíhou, wǒ huì zhùyì dào yìxiē yòng Yīngyǔ de shíhou bú huì zhùyì dào de dōngxi. Bǐrú Zhōngwén lǐ「jiā」zhège zì——tā jì shì「jiātíng」yě shì「jiāxiāng」yě shì「guójiā」. Yí gè zì jiù bǎ nǐ gēn hěn duō dōngxi lián zài le yìqǐ. Yěxǔ dāng nǐ yòng yì zhǒng xīn de yǔyán zuò mèng de shíhou, nǐ jiù yǒu le yì shuāng xīn de yǎnjing.
text_en: I've been in China for three years now. One morning I woke up and suddenly realized something strange — in last night's dream, I was speaking Chinese. Before, I always dreamed in English, no matter where the dream was set. But gradually my dreams began to change. First the people in my dreams started speaking Chinese, then I started using Chinese myself. I told my Chinese teacher, and she smiled: 'That means you've truly started thinking in Chinese.' This made me think for a long time. Language isn't just a tool — it changes how you see the world. When using Chinese, I notice things I wouldn't notice in English. For example, the character 'jia' in Chinese — it's 'family,' 'hometown,' and 'country' all at once. One character connects you to so many things. Maybe when you dream in a new language, you've gained a new pair of eyes.

title: The Delivery Driver's Revenge
text_zh: 我点了外卖，在备注里写了很多要求：「送快一点！」「不要按门铃，打我电话！」「如果迟到了就别来了！」「汤不能洒出来！」外卖员看到以后没有生气，他只是在送餐的时候多带了一样东西——一张纸条。纸条上写着：「亲爱的顾客，您好！感谢您今天的订单。汤没有洒，我也没有迟到。希望您吃得开心。另外，我帮您在外卖平台上写了一个好评，内容是：'外卖员很帅，服务很好，建议加薪。'」我看了以后，又生气又想笑。我打开外卖平台一看，他真的用我的账号写了这条评价。我犹豫了一下，最后没有删掉它。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài wǎng shàng kàn dào yí gè hěn yǒuqù de gùshi. Yǒu yí gè rén diǎn le wàimài, zài bèizhù lǐ xiě le hěn duō hěn guòfèn de yāoqiú:「Sòng kuài yìdiǎn!」「Búyào àn ménlíng, dǎ wǒ diànhuà!」「Rúguǒ chídào le jiù bié lái le!」「Tāng bù néng sǎ chūlái!」Wàimài yuán kàn dào yǐhòu méi yǒu shēngqì, tā zhǐ shì zài sòng cān de shíhou duō dài le yí yàng dōngxi——yì zhāng zhǐtiáo. Zhǐtiáo shàng xiě zhe:「Qīn'ài de gùkè, nín hǎo! Gǎnxiè nín jīntiān de dìngdān. Tāng méi yǒu sǎ, wǒ yě méi yǒu chídào. Xīwàng nín chī de kāixīn. Lìngwài, wǒ bāng nín zài wàimài píngtái shàng xiě le yí gè hǎopíng, nèiróng shì:「Wàimài yuán hěn shuài, fúwù hěn hǎo, jiànyì jiā xīn.」」Nàge rén kàn le yǐhòu, yòu shēngqì yòu xiǎng xiào. Tā dǎkāi wàimài píngtái yí kàn, fāxiàn wàimài yuán zhēn de yòng tā de zhànghào xiě le zhè tiáo píngjià. Tā yóuyù le yíxià, zuìhòu méi yǒu shān diào tā. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì zuì hǎo de bàofù——ràng nǐ shēngqì de rén xiào chūlái.
text_en: I ordered delivery and wrote a bunch of demands in the notes: 'Deliver faster!' 'Don't ring the doorbell, call me!' 'If you're late, don't bother coming!' 'Don't spill the soup!' The delivery driver wasn't angry. He just brought an extra item — a note. It read: 'Dear customer, hello! Thank you for today's order. The soup didn't spill, and I wasn't late. Hope you enjoy your meal. Also, I helped you write a review on the delivery platform: "The delivery driver is very handsome, great service, recommend a raise."' I was both angry and amused. I opened the app and found he had actually written that review using my account. I hesitated for a moment, then didn't delete it.

title: The Convenience Store at 3 AM
text_zh: 凌晨三点的便利店跟白天完全是两个世界。灯光还是那么亮，货架上的东西还是那么整齐，但是整个店只有我和收银员两个人。他是一个看起来二十出头的男孩，正在用笔记本写着什么。我拿了一个饭团和一瓶绿茶走过去结账。他抬起头，微微笑了一下。我注意到他写的是数学公式——大概是一个大学生在打工。我坐在便利店门口的台阶上吃饭团，街上一辆车都没有，只有路灯静静地亮着。远处偶尔传来一两声猫叫。我觉得凌晨三点的城市特别诚实，没有装饰，没有表演，只有最真实的安静。吃完饭团我回去的时候，那个男孩又低下头继续写他的数学题了。
text_pinyin: Língchén sān diǎn de biànlìdiàn gēn báitiān wánquán shì liǎng gè shìjiè. Dēngguāng háishì nàme liàng, huòjià shàng de dōngxi háishì nàme zhěngqí, dànshì zhěnggè diàn zhǐ yǒu wǒ hé shōuyín yuán liǎng gè rén. Tā shì yí gè kàn qǐlái èrshí chūtóu de nánhái, zhèngzài yòng bǐjìběn xiě zhe shénme. Wǒ ná le yí gè fàntuán hé yì píng lǜchá zǒu guòqù jiézhàng. Tā tái qǐ tóu, wēiwēi xiào le yíxià. Wǒ zhùyì dào tā xiě de shì shùxué gōngshì — dàgài shì yí gè dàxuéshēng zài dǎgōng. Wǒ zuò zài biànlìdiàn ménkǒu de táijiē shàng chī fàntuán, jiē shàng yí liàng chē dōu méiyǒu, zhǐ yǒu lùdēng jìngjìng de liàng zhe. Yuǎnchù ǒu'ěr chuán lái yì liǎng shēng māo jiào. Wǒ juéde língchén sān diǎn de chéngshì tèbié chéngshí, méiyǒu zhuāngshì, méiyǒu biǎoyǎn, zhǐ yǒu zuì zhēnshí de ānjìng. Chī wán fàntuán wǒ huíqù de shíhou, nàge nánhái yòu dī xià tóu jìxù xiě tā de shùxué tí le.
text_en: The convenience store at 3 AM is a completely different world from daytime. The lights are just as bright, the shelves just as tidy, but the entire store holds only me and the cashier. He's a young man who looks barely twenty, writing something in a notebook. I grabbed a rice ball and a bottle of green tea and went to check out. He looked up and gave a slight smile. I noticed he was writing math formulas — probably a college student working part-time. I sat on the steps outside the store eating my rice ball. Not a single car on the street, just streetlights shining quietly. An occasional cat call drifted from somewhere distant. I think the city at 3 AM is especially honest — no decoration, no performance, just the most authentic quiet. When I finished my rice ball and headed back, the young man had lowered his head again, continuing his math problems.

title: The Elevator That Gets Stuck
text_zh: 上个星期五，办公楼的电梯突然在七楼和八楼之间停了。里面只有我和一个不认识的女士。一开始我们都有点紧张，按了好几次紧急按钮。物业说十分钟就来修。那十分钟是我在这栋楼里跟陌生人说话最多的一次。她是十二楼一家设计公司的，来这栋楼上班三年了，之前从来没遇到过电梯坏。我说我在九楼，来了两年。我们聊了聊楼下那家咖啡店的咖啡越来越贵了，又聊了聊最近天气忽冷忽热。十分钟很快就过去了，电梯修好以后我们各自去了自己的楼层。但是从那以后，我们在大堂碰到的时候会互相笑一笑，说一声「你好」。原来，有时候需要一个小小的意外，才能让两个每天擦肩而过的人产生联系。
text_pinyin: Shàng gè xīngqīwǔ, bàngōnglóu de diàntī tūrán zài qī lóu hé bā lóu zhījiān tíng le. Lǐmiàn zhǐ yǒu wǒ hé yí gè bú rènshi de nǚshì. Yì kāishǐ wǒmen dōu yǒudiǎn jǐnzhāng, àn le hǎo jǐ cì jǐnjí ànniǔ. Wùyè shuō shí fēnzhōng jiù lái xiū. Nà shí fēnzhōng shì wǒ zài zhè dòng lóu lǐ gēn mòshēng rén shuō huà zuì duō de yí cì. Tā shì shí'èr lóu yì jiā shèjì gōngsī de, lái zhè dòng lóu shàng bān sān nián le, zhīqián cónglái méi yù dào guò diàntī huài. Wǒ shuō wǒ zài jiǔ lóu, lái le liǎng nián. Wǒmen liáo le liáo lóu xià nà jiā kāfēi diàn de kāfēi yuè lái yuè guì le, yòu liáo le liáo zuìjìn tiānqì hū lěng hū rè. Shí fēnzhōng hěn kuài jiù guòqù le, diàntī xiū hǎo yǐhòu wǒmen gèzì qù le zìjǐ de lóucéng. Dànshì cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒmen zài dàtáng pèng dào de shíhou huì hùxiāng xiào yí xiào, shuō yì shēng「nǐ hǎo」. Yuánlái, yǒu shíhou xūyào yí gè xiǎo xiǎo de yìwài, cái néng ràng liǎng gè měi tiān cā jiān ér guò de rén chǎnshēng liánxì.
text_en: Last Friday, the office elevator suddenly stopped between the seventh and eighth floors. Inside were me and a woman I didn't know. At first we were both a bit nervous, pressing the emergency button several times. The property management said they'd come fix it in ten minutes. Those ten minutes were the most I've ever talked to a stranger in this building. She works at a design company on the twelfth floor, has been coming to this building for three years, and had never encountered an elevator breakdown before. I said I'm on the ninth floor, been here two years. We chatted about how the coffee at the shop downstairs keeps getting more expensive, and about the recent weather going back and forth between cold and hot. The ten minutes passed quickly, and after the elevator was fixed we each went to our own floors.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: Moments like "Last Friday, the office elevator suddenly stopped between the seventh" reach beyond the page.

questions.q_zh: 电梯修好以后他们的关系怎么样了？
questions.q_en: How is their relationship after the elevator was fixed?

options.text: 碰到的时候会笑一笑说你好
options.pinyin: pèng dào de shíhou huì xiào yí xiào shuō nǐ hǎo
options.text_en: they smile and say hello when they meet

questions.explanation: Look at "The ten minutes passed quickly, and after the elevator" — it shows the mechanism.

title: The Cat Colony Under the Bridge
text_zh: 河边的那座旧桥下面住着一群猫。没有人知道它们是什么时候开始住在那里的，好像它们一直就在那里。每天傍晚，附近有两三个人会来喂它们。其中一个是开出租车的大叔，他下班以后总是先去桥下看一眼猫，再回家。他跟我说他一开始只是偶然看到了一只小猫，给它喂了一点鱼。后来那只猫每天都在同一个地方等他。再后来，更多的猫出现了。现在桥下大概有十几只猫，大的小的都有。它们好像已经习惯了城市的噪音，汽车开过去的时候也不怕。大叔说：「它们选择住在这里，这里就是它们的家。我只是一个邻居。」
text_pinyin: Hé biān de nà zuò jiù qiáo xiàmiàn zhù zhe yì qún māo. Méiyǒu rén zhīdào tāmen shì shénme shíhou kāishǐ zhù zài nàlǐ de, hǎoxiàng tāmen yìzhí jiù zài nàlǐ. Měi tiān bàngwǎn, fùjìn yǒu liǎng sān gè rén huì lái wèi tāmen. Qízhōng yí gè shì kāi chūzūchē de dàshū, tā xià bān yǐhòu zǒng shì xiān qù qiáo xià kàn yì yǎn māo, zài huí jiā. Tā gēn wǒ shuō tā yì kāishǐ zhǐ shì ǒurán kàn dào le yì zhī xiǎo māo, gěi tā wèi le yìdiǎn yú. Hòulái nà zhī māo měi tiān dōu zài tóng yí gè dìfang děng tā. Zài hòulái, gèng duō de māo chūxiàn le. Xiànzài qiáo xià dàgài yǒu shí jǐ zhī māo, dà de xiǎo de dōu yǒu. Tāmen hǎoxiàng yǐjīng xíguàn le chéngshì de zàoyīn, qìchē kāi guòqù de shíhou yě bú pà. Dàshū shuō: 「Tāmen xuǎnzé zhù zài zhèlǐ, zhèlǐ jiù shì tāmen de jiā. Wǒ zhǐ shì yí gè línjū.」
text_en: Under the old bridge by the river lives a colony of cats. No one knows when they first started living there — it's as if they've always been. Every evening, two or three people from the neighborhood come to feed them. One is a taxi driver who always stops by the bridge to check on the cats after work before going home. He told me that at first he just happened to see a small cat and fed it a bit of fish. Later that cat waited for him in the same spot every day. Then more cats appeared. Now there are about a dozen under the bridge, big and small. They seem to have gotten used to the city's noise, unfazed even when cars drive past. The driver said: 'They chose to live here, so this is their home. I'm just a neighbor.'

questions.q_zh: 出租车大叔一开始怎么认识那些猫的？
questions.q_en: How did the taxi driver first encounter the cats?

options.text: 偶然看到一只小猫，喂了一点鱼
options.pinyin: ǒurán kàn dào yì zhī xiǎo māo, wèi le yìdiǎn yú
options.text_en: happened to see a kitten and fed it fish

options.pinyin: tā zài qiáo xià jiǎn dào de
options.text_en: he found them under the bridge

questions.explanation: "He told me that at first he just happened" walks you through it.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: "Under the old bridge by the river lives a colony" gives you what you need.

title: Waiting in the Rain
text_zh: 我站在公交站等车，雨已经下了快一个小时了。旁边站着一个老爷爷，他撑着一把很旧的黑伞，伞上有几个小洞，雨水从洞里滴下来。他好像一点也不着急，安安静静地看着雨。我问他要不要站到棚子里面来，他摇摇头说：「在雨里站一会儿也不错。」他告诉我他已经退休了，平时没什么事情做，下雨的时候他就出来走走。他说他小时候住在农村，下雨的时候孩子们都在外面玩，现在的孩子下雨就待在家里看手机，不一样了。公交车终于来了，我让老爷爷先上车。他上车的时候收起伞，对我点了点头。雨水顺着他的伞流了一地。那个画面很简单，但是在我的记忆里留了很久。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhàn zài gōngjiāo zhàn děng chē, yǔ yǐjīng xià le kuài yí gè xiǎoshí le. Pángbiān zhàn zhe yí gè lǎo yéye, tā chēng zhe yì bǎ hěn jiù de hēi sǎn, sǎn shàng yǒu jǐ gè xiǎo dòng, yǔshuǐ cóng dòng lǐ dī xiàlái. Tā hǎoxiàng yìdiǎn yě bù zháojí, ān'ānjìngjìng de kàn zhe yǔ. Wǒ wèn tā yào bu yào zhàn dào péngzi lǐmiàn lái, tā yáoyao tóu shuō: 「Zài yǔ lǐ zhàn yíhuìr yě búcuò.」 Tā gàosu wǒ tā yǐjīng tuìxiū le, píngshí méi shénme shìqing zuò, xià yǔ de shíhou tā jiù chūlái zǒuzou. Tā shuō tā xiǎo shíhou zhù zài nóngcūn, xià yǔ de shíhou háizimen dōu zài wàimiàn wán, xiànzài de háizi xià yǔ jiù dāi zài jiā lǐ kàn shǒujī, bù yíyàng le. Gōngjiāo chē zhōngyú lái le, wǒ ràng lǎo yéye xiān shàng chē. Tā shàng chē de shíhou shōu qǐ sǎn, duì wǒ diǎn le diǎn tóu. Yǔshuǐ shùn zhe tā de sǎn liú le yí dì. Nàge huàmiàn hěn jiǎndān, dànshì zài wǒ de jìyì lǐ liú le hěn jiǔ.
text_en: I stood at the bus stop waiting, and it had been raining for nearly an hour. Beside me stood an old grandfather holding a old black umbrella with a few small holes — rainwater dripped through them. He didn't seem rushed at all, quietly watching the rain. I asked if he wanted to come stand under the shelter. He shook his head and said: 'Standing in the rain for a bit isn't bad.' He told me he's retired and doesn't have much to do — when it rains, he comes out for a walk. He said when he was young and lived in the countryside, children played outside in the rain, but nowadays children stay home and look at their phones when it rains — things are different now. The bus finally came, and I let the grandfather board first. As he got on, he folded his umbrella and nodded at me. Rainwater ran off his umbrella onto the ground.

questions.q_zh: 老爷爷为什么不站到棚子里？
questions.q_en: Why didn't the grandfather stand under the shelter?

options.text: 他觉得在雨里站一会儿也不错
options.pinyin: tā juéde zài yǔ lǐ zhàn yíhuìr yě búcuò
options.text_en: he felt standing in the rain was nice

questions.explanation: The reason hides in plain sight: "Standing in the rain for a bit isn't bad.'."

questions.q_zh: 老爷爷小时候下雨的时候做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the grandfather do in the rain as a child?
questions.explanation: "He said when he was young and lived" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

title: The Museum Guard's Favorite Painting
text_zh: 我在美术馆里遇到了一个很特别的保安。大部分保安只是站在角落里看手机，但是他不一样。他会站在不同的画前面看，好像他也是一个参观者。有一次我问他：「你最喜欢哪一幅画？」他想了想，带我走到了二楼最里面的一个小展厅。那里挂着一幅不太大的画，画的是一个黄昏的海边，沙滩上只有一个人的影子。他说：「我在这里工作了五年，每天都会来看这幅画。它让我想起我老家的海。」他老家在南方的一个海边小城，他年轻的时候来了这个城市打工，已经十几年没回去了。他说有时候站在这幅画前面，就好像回到了小时候在海边玩的日子。我觉得，对于他来说，这幅画不只是一幅画，而是一扇窗户，能看到他最想念的风景。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài měishùguǎn lǐ yù dào le yí gè hěn tèbié de bǎo'ān. Dà bùfen bǎo'ān zhǐ shì zhàn zài jiǎoluò lǐ kàn shǒujī, dànshì tā bù yíyàng. Tā huì zhàn zài bùtóng de huà qiánmiàn kàn, hǎoxiàng tā yě shì yí gè cānguān zhě. Yǒu yí cì wǒ wèn tā: 「Nǐ zuì xǐhuan nǎ yì fú huà?」 Tā xiǎng le xiǎng, dài wǒ zǒu dào le èr lóu zuì lǐmiàn de yí gè xiǎo zhǎntīng. Nàlǐ guà zhe yì fú bú tài dà de huà, huà de shì yí gè huánghūn de hǎibiān, shātān shàng zhǐ yǒu yí gè rén de yǐngzi. Tā shuō: 「Wǒ zài zhèlǐ gōngzuò le wǔ nián, měi tiān dōu huì lái kàn zhè fú huà. Tā ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ wǒ lǎojiā de hǎi.」 Tā lǎojiā zài nánfāng de yí gè hǎibiān xiǎo chéng, tā niánqīng de shíhou lái le zhège chéngshì dǎgōng, yǐjīng shí jǐ nián méi huíqù le. Tā shuō yǒu shíhou zhàn zài zhè fú huà qiánmiàn, jiù hǎoxiàng huí dào le xiǎo shíhou zài hǎibiān wán de rìzi. Wǒ juéde, duìyú tā lái shuō, zhè fú huà bù zhǐ shì yì fú huà, ér shì yí shàn chuānghu, néng kàn dào tā zuì xiǎngniàn de fēngjǐng.
text_en: I met a special security guard at the art museum. Most guards stand in corners looking at their phones, but he's different. He stands in front of different paintings and looks at them, as if he's also a visitor. Once I asked him: 'Which painting is your favorite?' He thought about it, then led me to a small exhibition room at the far end of the second floor. There hung a modest painting of a seaside at dusk, with only one person's shadow on the beach. He said: 'I've worked here for five years, and I come to see this painting every day. It reminds me of the sea back home.' His hometown is a small coastal city in the south. He came to this city to work when he was young and hasn't been back in over ten years. He says sometimes standing before this painting feels like returning to his childhood days playing by the sea.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: The mood lives in specifics like "I met a special security guard at the art museum."

questions.q_zh: 保安多长时间没回老家了？
questions.q_en: How long has the guard been away from his hometown?
questions.explanation: Follow the action in "He came to this city to work when" — that's how.

title: The Barista's Latte Art
text_zh: 我发现公司附近新开了一家咖啡店，里面的咖啡师特别喜欢做拉花。每一杯拿铁上面都有不同的图案——有时候是一片叶子，有时候是一颗心，有时候是一只猫。我问他是怎么学会的，他说他每天下班以后都会练习两个小时。一开始总是失败，牛奶倒下去就变成一团白色的东西，什么也看不出来。但是他没有放弃，练了半年以后终于可以画出漂亮的图案了。他说最开心的时刻不是客人夸他，而是看到客人拿到咖啡以后，先拿出手机拍一张照片，然后才舍得喝。他笑着说：「那一秒钟的犹豫，就是对我最大的称赞。」我觉得他说得很有道理。有些美好的东西，就是因为短暂才让人珍惜。
text_pinyin: Wǒ fāxiàn gōngsī fùjìn xīn kāi le yì jiā kāfēi diàn, lǐmiàn de kāfēi shī tèbié xǐhuan zuò lāhuā. Měi yì bēi ná tiě shàngmiàn dōu yǒu bùtóng de tú'àn — yǒu shíhou shì yí piàn yèzi, yǒu shíhou shì yì kē xīn, yǒu shíhou shì yì zhī māo. Wǒ wèn tā shì zěnme xué huì de, tā shuō tā měi tiān xià bān yǐhòu dōu huì liànxí liǎng gè xiǎoshí. Yì kāishǐ zǒng shì shībài, niúnǎi dào xiàqù jiù biàn chéng yì tuán bái sè de dōngxi, shénme yě kàn bu chūlái. Dànshì tā méiyǒu fàngqì, liàn le bàn nián yǐhòu zhōngyú kěyǐ huà chū piàoliang de tú'àn le. Tā shuō zuì kāixīn de shíkè bú shì kèrén kuā tā, ér shì kàn dào kèrén ná dào kāfēi yǐhòu, xiān ná chū shǒujī pāi yì zhāng zhàopiàn, ránhòu cái shěde hē. Tā xiào zhe shuō: 「Nà yì miǎozhōng de yóuyù, jiù shì duì wǒ zuì dà de chēngzàn.」 Wǒ juéde tā shuō de hěn yǒu dàolǐ. Yǒuxiē měihǎo de dōngxi, jiù shì yīnwèi duǎnzàn cái ràng rén zhēnxī.
text_en: I discovered a new coffee shop near the office where the barista especially loves making latte art. Each latte has a different design — sometimes a leaf, sometimes a heart, sometimes a cat. I asked how he learned. He said he practices for two hours after work every day. At first he always failed — the milk poured in would just become a white blob with no recognizable shape. But he didn't give up, and after six months of practice he could finally create beautiful patterns. He said his happiest moment isn't when customers compliment him, but when he sees a customer receive their coffee, pull out their phone to take a photo first, and only then bear to drink it. He smiled and said: 'That one second of hesitation is the greatest compliment to me.' I think he makes a good point. Some beautiful things are precious precisely because they're brief.

questions.q_zh: 咖啡师练了多长时间才学会拉花？
questions.q_en: How long did the barista practice before learning latte art?
questions.explanation: "I discovered a new coffee shop near the office where" reveals the how.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: "I discovered a new coffee shop near the office where" is designed to make you pause and reconsider.

title: The Apartment Next Door
text_zh: 住在我隔壁的是一对年轻夫妻。我们的墙不太隔音，所以有时候能听到他们的生活。每天早上六点半，闹钟响了以后，他们会小声地讨论今天谁先用洗手间。做早饭的时候，厨房里传来锅碗的声音和炒蛋的香味。晚上的时候，有时候能听到他们一起看电影，偶尔传来笑声。周末的早上他们不设闹钟，我能感觉到整面墙都安静下来了。有一次在电梯里碰到他们，女孩有点不好意思地说：「我们是不是太吵了？」我笑着摇摇头说不会。其实，听到隔壁有人好好地生活着，会让我觉得自己也没有那么孤单。
text_pinyin: Zhù zài wǒ gébì de shì yí duì niánqīng fūqī. Wǒmen de qiáng bú tài géyīn, suǒyǐ yǒu shíhou néng tīng dào tāmen de shēnghuó. Měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, nàozhōng xiǎng le yǐhòu, tāmen huì xiǎo shēng de tǎolùn jīntiān shéi xiān yòng xǐshǒujiān. Zuò zǎofàn de shíhou, chúfáng lǐ chuán lái guō wǎn de shēngyīn hé chǎo dàn de xiāngwèi. Wǎnshang de shíhou, yǒu shíhou néng tīng dào tāmen yìqǐ kàn diànyǐng, ǒu'ěr chuán lái xiào shēng. Zhōumò de zǎoshang tāmen bú shè nàozhōng, wǒ néng gǎnjué dào zhěng miàn qiáng dōu ānjìng xiàlái le. Yǒu yí cì zài diàntī lǐ pèng dào tāmen, nǚhái yǒudiǎn bù hǎoyìsi de shuō: 「Wǒmen shì bu shì tài chǎo le?」 Wǒ xiào zhe yáoyao tóu shuō bú huì. Qíshí, tīng dào gébì yǒu rén hǎohǎo de shēnghuó zhe, huì ràng wǒ juéde zìjǐ yě méiyǒu nàme gūdān.
text_en: A young couple lives next door to me. Our walls aren't very soundproof, so sometimes I can hear their daily life. Every morning at six-thirty, after the alarm goes off, they quietly discuss who gets the bathroom first. When they make breakfast, the sounds of pots and pans and the smell of scrambled eggs drift from the kitchen. In the evenings, I can sometimes hear them watching a movie together, with occasional laughter. On weekend mornings they don't set an alarm, and I can feel the entire wall go quiet. Once I ran into them in the elevator, and the girl said a bit sheepishly: 'Are we too noisy?' I smiled, shook my head, and said no. Hearing people living well next door makes me feel less alone myself.

questions.q_zh: 每天早上闹钟响了以后他们讨论什么？
questions.q_en: What do they discuss after the alarm goes off?
questions.explanation: Go back to "Every morning at six-thirty, after the alarm goes off." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么不觉得隔壁吵？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't 'I' find the neighbors noisy?

options.text: 听到有人好好生活让自己不那么孤单
options.pinyin: tīng dào yǒu rén hǎohǎo shēnghuó ràng zìjǐ bú nàme gūdān
options.text_en: hearing people living well makes one less lonely

questions.explanation: "Hearing people living well next door makes me feel less" gives away the reason without spelling it out.

title: The Rooftop at Night
text_zh: 夏天的晚上，我喜欢一个人上楼顶坐一会儿。楼顶上什么都没有，只有水泥地和一些旧管道。但是从这里可以看到很远的地方——对面的楼、远处的马路、更远处闪着灯光的高楼。晚上的风吹过来很凉快，城市的声音到了这个高度变得很模糊，就像一首很远的歌。有时候我会带一罐啤酒上来，有时候什么都不带，就这样坐着看城市的灯光。我想，城市里的每一盏灯后面都有一个人，他们各自在过着自己的生活。有的人在加班，有的人在做饭，有的人在哄孩子睡觉。这些灯光让我觉得，虽然我不认识他们，但是我们在同一个夜晚里一起存在着。
text_pinyin: Xiàtiān de wǎnshang, wǒ xǐhuan yí gè rén shàng lóudǐng zuò yíhuìr. Lóudǐng shàng shénme dōu méiyǒu, zhǐ yǒu shuǐní dì hé yìxiē jiù guǎndào. Dànshì cóng zhèlǐ kěyǐ kàn dào hěn yuǎn de dìfang — duìmiàn de lóu, yuǎnchù de mǎlù, gèng yuǎnchù shǎn zhe dēngguāng de gāolóu. Wǎnshang de fēng chuī guòlái hěn liángkuai, chéngshì de shēngyīn dào le zhège gāodù biàn de hěn móhu, jiù xiàng yì shǒu hěn yuǎn de gē. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì dài yì guàn píjiǔ shànglái, yǒu shíhou shénme dōu bú dài, jiù zhèyàng zuòzhe kàn chéngshì de dēngguāng. Wǒ xiǎng, chéngshì lǐ de měi yì zhǎn dēng hòumiàn dōu yǒu yí gè rén, tāmen gèzì zài guò zhe zìjǐ de shēnghuó. Yǒu de rén zài jiābān, yǒu de rén zài zuò fàn, yǒu de rén zài hǒng háizi shuìjiào. Zhèxiē dēngguāng ràng wǒ juéde, suīrán wǒ bú rènshi tāmen, dànshì wǒmen zài tóng yí gè yèwǎn lǐ yìqǐ cúnzài zhe.
text_en: On summer evenings, I like to go up to the rooftop alone and sit for a while. There's nothing up there — just concrete and some old pipes. But from here you can see very far — the buildings across the way, the distant roads, and even farther, skyscrapers twinkling with lights. The night breeze is cool, and the city's sounds at this height become muffled, like a distant song. Sometimes I bring a can of beer up, sometimes nothing at all — I just sit and watch the city lights. I think behind every light in the city is a person, each living their own life. Some are working overtime, some are cooking, some are coaxing children to sleep. These lights make me feel that although I don't know them, we exist together in the same night.

questions.q_zh: 城市的声音到了楼顶变成了什么样？
questions.q_en: What do city sounds become at rooftop height?

options.text: 很模糊，像一首很远的歌
options.pinyin: hěn móhu, xiàng yì shǒu hěn yuǎn de gē

questions.explanation: "The night breeze is cool, and the city's sounds" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 城市的灯光让作者有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How do the city lights make the author feel?

options.text: 跟不认识的人在同一个夜晚一起存在
options.pinyin: gēn bú rènshi de rén zài tóng yí gè yèwǎn yìqǐ cúnzài
options.text_en: existing together with strangers in the same night

questions.explanation: The mood lives in specifics like "These lights make me feel that although I don't know."

title: The Saturday Farmers Market Musician
text_zh: 每个周六的农贸市场上，除了卖菜的和买菜的，还有一个拉二胡的老人。他总是坐在市场入口旁边的一把塑料椅子上，面前放着一个旧帽子。他拉的曲子都很老，是那种我小时候在电视里听过的旋律。来来往往的人大部分不会停下来听，但是他好像并不在意。他闭着眼睛拉琴，沉浸在自己的音乐里。有一次我买完菜以后在他旁边站了一会儿，听他拉了两首曲子。他睁开眼睛看到我，笑着点了点头，然后继续拉下一首。我在帽子里放了一些零钱。他说了声谢谢，然后又闭上了眼睛。市场的嘈杂声和他的音乐混在一起，变成了一种奇怪的和谐。
text_pinyin: Měi gè zhōuliù de nóngmào shìchǎng shàng, chúle mài cài de hé mǎi cài de, hái yǒu yí gè lā èrhú de lǎorén. Tā zǒng shì zuò zài shìchǎng rùkǒu pángbiān de yì bǎ sùliào yǐzi shàng, miànqián fàng zhe yí gè jiù màozi. Tā lā de qǔzi dōu hěn lǎo, shì nà zhǒng wǒ xiǎo shíhou zài diànshì lǐ tīng guò de xuánlǜ. Láilái wǎngwǎng de rén dà bùfen bú huì tíng xiàlái tīng, dànshì tā hǎoxiàng bìng bú zàiyì. Tā bì zhe yǎnjing lā qín, chénjìn zài zìjǐ de yīnyuè lǐ. Yǒu yí cì wǒ mǎi wán cài yǐhòu zài tā pángbiān zhàn le yíhuìr, tīng tā lā le liǎng shǒu qǔzi. Tā zhēng kāi yǎnjing kàn dào wǒ, xiào zhe diǎn le diǎn tóu, ránhòu jìxù lā xià yì shǒu. Wǒ zài màozi lǐ fàng le yìxiē língqián. Tā shuō le shēng xièxie, ránhòu yòu bì shàng le yǎnjing. Shìchǎng de cáozá shēng hé tā de yīnyuè hùn zài yìqǐ, biàn chéng le yì zhǒng qíguài de héxié.
text_en: At the Saturday farmers market, besides the vendors and shoppers, there's an old man who plays the erhu. He always sits on a plastic chair near the market entrance, with an old hat placed in front of him. The tunes he plays are all old ones — the kind of melodies I heard on TV as a child. Most passersby don't stop to listen, but he doesn't seem to mind. He plays with his eyes closed, immersed in his own music. Once after buying groceries, I stood beside him for a while and listened to two pieces. He opened his eyes, saw me, smiled and nodded, then continued with the next piece. I put some change in his hat. He said thank you, then closed his eyes again. The noise of the market and his music blended together into a strange kind of harmony.

questions.q_zh: 老人用什么乐器演奏？
questions.q_en: What instrument does the old man play?
questions.explanation: Go back to "At the Saturday farmers market, besides the vendors and shoppers." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 大部分路过的人会停下来听吗？
questions.q_en: Do most passersby stop to listen?

options.pinyin: dà bùfen rén huì tíng xiàlái
dà bùfen rén bú huì tíng xiàlái
suǒyǒu rén dōu huì tíng xiàlái

questions.explanation: Focus on "Most passersby don't stop to listen, but he doesn't seem" — the rest follows.

title: The Bookshop Cat
text_zh: 巷子里有一家很小的独立书店，门口总是坐着一只橘色的猫。它不怕人，谁走过来它都不动，好像觉得自己才是这条街的主人。进了书店你会发现，这只猫在里面也有一个固定的位置——靠窗的书架旁边有一个小垫子，那是它下午睡觉的地方。老板说这只猫是几年前自己跑过来的，从那以后就再也没离开过。有些客人来这家书店不是为了买书，而是为了看猫。它有时候会跳到收银台上面，用爪子轻轻碰你的手，好像在提醒你该买一本书了。老板笑着说：「这只猫比我更会做生意。」我觉得，一家有猫的书店，总是会让人多待一会儿。
text_pinyin: Xiàngzi lǐ yǒu yì jiā hěn xiǎo de dúlì shūdiàn, ménkǒu zǒng shì zuò zhe yì zhī jú sè de māo. Tā bú pà rén, shéi zǒu guòlái tā dōu bú dòng, hǎoxiàng juéde zìjǐ cái shì zhè tiáo jiē de zhǔrén. Jìn le shūdiàn nǐ huì fāxiàn, zhè zhī māo zài lǐmiàn yě yǒu yí gè gùdìng de wèizhì — kào chuāng de shūjià pángbiān yǒu yí gè xiǎo diànzi, nà shì tā xiàwǔ shuìjiào de dìfang. Lǎobǎn shuō zhè zhī māo shì jǐ nián qián zìjǐ pǎo guòlái de, cóng nà yǐhòu jiù zài yě méi líkāi guò. Yǒuxiē kèrén lái zhè jiā shūdiàn bú shì wèile mǎi shū, ér shì wèile kàn māo. Tā yǒu shíhou huì tiào dào shōuyíntái shàngmiàn, yòng zhuǎzi qīngqīng pèng nǐ de shǒu, hǎoxiàng zài tíxǐng nǐ gāi mǎi yì běn shū le. Lǎobǎn xiào zhe shuō: 「Zhè zhī māo bǐ wǒ gèng huì zuò shēngyì.」 Wǒ juéde, yì jiā yǒu māo de shūdiàn, zǒng shì huì ràng rén duō dāi yíhuìr.
text_en: In the alley there's a tiny independent bookshop with an orange cat always sitting at the entrance. It's not afraid of people — it doesn't move no matter who walks by, as if it considers itself the owner of this street. Inside the shop, you'll find the cat also has a designated spot — a small cushion beside the window bookshelf, where it naps in the afternoon. The owner says the cat showed up on its own a few years ago and has never left since. Some customers come to this bookshop not for books, but for the cat. It sometimes jumps onto the counter and gently taps your hand with its paw, as if reminding you it's time to buy a book. The owner laughs and says: 'This cat is better at business than I am.' I think a bookshop with a cat always makes people want to stay a little longer.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: "In the alley there's a tiny independent bookshop" — the emotional temperature is right there.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: "In the alley there's a tiny independent bookshop" — the emotional temperature is right there.

title: The Night Market Tofu Stall
text_zh: 夜市最里面有一个不太起眼的豆腐摊，只卖两样东西：臭豆腐和豆腐脑。摊主是一个五十多岁的阿姨，她每天下午两点就开始准备，磨豆子、做豆腐、调酱料，一直忙到傍晚五点夜市开始。她的臭豆腐炸得外面脆里面嫩，配上她自己做的辣酱，味道特别好。我问她为什么不多卖几样东西，她说：「做好两样就够了。什么都卖的话，什么都做不好。」她说这句话的时候很认真。排队的人总是很多，但是她从来不着急，每一份都做得很仔细。吃她的豆腐就像一种仪式，要慢慢地品尝。有些食物不需要很多选择，简单的、认真做的，就是最好的。
text_pinyin: Yèshì zuì lǐmiàn yǒu yí gè bú tài qǐyǎn de dòufu tān, zhǐ mài liǎng yàng dōngxi: chòu dòufu hé dòufu nǎo. Tānzhǔ shì yí gè wǔshí duō suì de āyí, tā měi tiān xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn jiù kāishǐ zhǔnbèi, mò dòuzi, zuò dòufu, tiáo jiàngliào, yìzhí máng dào bàngwǎn wǔ diǎn yèshì kāishǐ. Tā de chòu dòufu zhá de wàimiàn cuì lǐmiàn nèn, pèi shàng tā zìjǐ zuò de làjiàng, wèidào tèbié hǎo. Wǒ wèn tā wèi shénme bù duō mài jǐ yàng dōngxi, tā shuō: 「Zuò hǎo liǎng yàng jiù gòu le. Shénme dōu mài dehuà, shénme dōu zuò bu hǎo.」 Tā shuō zhè jù huà de shíhou hěn rènzhēn. Páiduì de rén zǒng shì hěn duō, dànshì tā cónglái bù zháojí, měi yí fèn dōu zuò de hěn zǐxì. Chī tā de dòufu jiù xiàng yì zhǒng yíshì, yào mànmàn de pǐncháng. Yǒuxiē shíwù bù xūyào hěn duō xuǎnzé, jiǎndān de, rènzhēn zuò de, jiù shì zuì hǎo de.
text_en: At the far end of the night market there's an unassuming tofu stall that sells only two things: stinky tofu and tofu pudding. The owner is a woman in her fifties who starts preparing at two every afternoon — grinding soybeans, making tofu, mixing sauces — busy until the market opens at five. Her stinky tofu is fried crispy on the outside and tender inside, and paired with her homemade chili sauce, the flavor is exceptional. I asked why she doesn't sell more items. She said: 'Doing two things well is enough. If you sell everything, you can't do anything well.' She was very serious when she said this. The line is always long, but she's never in a rush, preparing each portion carefully. Eating her tofu is like a ritual — you have to savor it slowly. Some foods don't need many choices; simple, made with care, is the best there is.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: Go back to "At the far end of the night market there's." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 阿姨为什么不多卖几样东西？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the woman sell more items?

options.text: 因为她没有钱买更多食材
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā méiyǒu qián mǎi gèng duō shícái

options.text: 做好两样就够了，什么都卖就做不好
options.pinyin: zuò hǎo liǎng yàng jiù gòu le, shénme dōu mài jiù zuò bu hǎo
options.text_en: doing two well is enough; selling everything means nothing is done well

options.pinyin: kèrén zhǐ xǐhuan zhè liǎng yàng

questions.explanation: It comes down to "She said: 'Doing two things well is enough" — cause and effect.

title: The Shared Dryer
text_zh: 梅雨季节的时候，公寓楼的公用烘干机变成了最受欢迎的东西。平时没什么人用，但是连续下了一个星期的雨以后，大家都需要烘干衣服。洗衣房里贴了一张纸，上面写着每家可以用四十分钟。有一天我去洗衣房的时候，发现一个女孩正坐在地上等烘干机。她告诉我已经等了快一个小时了，前面那个人用了很久。我们一起等的时候聊了起来。她刚搬来两个月，还不太认识这栋楼的人。我告诉她二楼的便利店周三打折，六楼的陈阿姨有时候会分享她种的菜。她听了觉得很开心，说：「原来这栋楼还挺有意思的。」后来我们偶尔在洗衣房碰到，都会聊上几句。一台烘干机让两个陌生人变成了可以说话的邻居。
text_pinyin: Méiyǔ jìjié de shíhou, gōngyù lóu de gōngyòng hōnggānjī biàn chéng le zuì shòu huānyíng de dōngxi. Píngshí méi shénme rén yòng, dànshì liánxù xià le yí gè xīngqī de yǔ yǐhòu, dàjiā dōu xūyào hōnggān yīfu. Xǐyīfáng lǐ tiē le yì zhāng zhǐ, shàngmiàn xiě zhe měi jiā kěyǐ yòng sìshí fēnzhōng. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ qù xǐyīfáng de shíhou, fāxiàn yí gè nǚhái zhèng zuò zài dìshang děng hōnggānjī. Tā gàosu wǒ yǐjīng děng le kuài yí gè xiǎoshí le, qiánmiàn nàge rén yòng le hěn jiǔ. Wǒmen yìqǐ děng de shíhou liáo le qǐlái. Tā gāng bān lái liǎng gè yuè, hái bú tài rènshi zhè dòng lóu de rén. Wǒ gàosu tā èr lóu de biànlìdiàn zhōusān dǎzhé, liù lóu de Chén āyí yǒu shíhou huì fēnxiǎng tā zhòng de cài. Tā tīng le juéde hěn kāixīn, shuō: 「Yuánlái zhè dòng lóu hái tǐng yǒu yìsi de.」 Hòulái wǒmen ǒu'ěr zài xǐyīfáng pèng dào, dōu huì liáo shàng jǐ jù. Yì tái hōnggānjī ràng liǎng gè mòshēng rén biàn chéng le kěyǐ shuō huà de línjū.
text_en: During the plum rain season, the apartment building's shared dryer becomes the most popular item. Normally few people use it, but after a week of continuous rain, everyone needs to dry their clothes. A notice in the laundry room says each household can use it for forty minutes. One day when I went to the laundry room, I found a girl sitting on the floor waiting for the dryer. She told me she'd been waiting almost an hour — the person before her had used it a long time. We started chatting while waiting together. She'd moved in two months ago and didn't know many people in the building yet. I told her the convenience store on the second floor has discounts on Wednesdays, and Aunt Chen on the sixth floor sometimes shares vegetables she grows. She seemed happy to hear this and said: 'So this building is pretty interesting.' After that we'd occasionally run into each other in the laundry room and chat a bit.

questions.q_zh: 为什么梅雨季节烘干机特别受欢迎？
questions.q_en: Why is the dryer especially popular during plum rain season?

options.text: 因为连续下雨，大家都需要烘干衣服
options.pinyin: yīnwèi liánxù xià yǔ, dàjiā dōu xūyào hōnggān yīfu
options.text_en: continuous rain means everyone needs to dry clothes

questions.explanation: Pay attention to "Normally few people use it, but after a week" — it answers the why.

questions.q_zh: 那个女孩搬来多久了？
questions.q_en: How long has the girl been living there?
questions.explanation: Follow the action in "She'd moved in two months ago and didn't know many" — that's how.

title: The Park Bench in Autumn
text_zh: 秋天的时候，公园里的长椅变得更加安静了。夏天的时候到处都是人，但是天气一凉，坐在外面的人就少了。我喜欢秋天的长椅，因为你可以一个人坐很久也不会有人来打扰。有一天下午我坐在椅子上看书，一片黄叶飘下来落在了我的书页上。我把它夹在书里当作书签。旁边的椅子上坐着一个头发白了一半的女士，她在织一条围巾。织了一会儿她抬起头来，看着树上的叶子说：「今年的叶子比去年红得更早。」我说是的。我们没有继续说话，但是那一刻的安静让人觉得很舒服。秋天的公园不需要太多的话，树叶的颜色、凉爽的风、还有长椅上各自安静的人，这些就已经足够了。
text_pinyin: Qiūtiān de shíhou, gōngyuán lǐ de chángyǐ biàn de gèngjiā ānjìng le. Xiàtiān de shíhou dàochù dōu shì rén, dànshì tiānqì yí liáng, zuò zài wàimiàn de rén jiù shǎo le. Wǒ xǐhuan qiūtiān de chángyǐ, yīnwèi nǐ kěyǐ yí gè rén zuò hěn jiǔ yě bú huì yǒu rén lái dǎrǎo. Yǒu yì tiān xiàwǔ wǒ zuò zài yǐzi shàng kàn shū, yí piàn huáng yè piāo xiàlái luò zài le wǒ de shūyè shàng. Wǒ bǎ tā jiā zài shū lǐ dāng zuò shūqiān. Pángbiān de yǐzi shàng zuòzhe yí gè tóufa bái le yí bàn de nǚshì, tā zài zhī yì tiáo wéijīn. Zhī le yíhuìr tā tái qǐ tóu lái, kàn zhe shù shàng de yèzi shuō: 「Jīnnián de yèzi bǐ qùnián hóng de gèng zǎo.」 Wǒ shuō shì de. Wǒmen méiyǒu jìxù shuō huà, dànshì nà yí kè de ānjìng ràng rén juéde hěn shūfu. Qiūtiān de gōngyuán bù xūyào tài duō de huà, shùyè de yánsè, liángshuǎng de fēng, hái yǒu chángyǐ shàng gèzì ānjìng de rén, zhèxiē jiù yǐjīng zúgòu le.
text_en: In autumn, the park benches become even quieter. In summer there are people everywhere, but once the weather turns cool, fewer people sit outside. I like autumn benches because you can sit alone for a long time without anyone disturbing you. One afternoon I was sitting and reading when a yellow leaf drifted down and landed on my page. I tucked it inside the book as a bookmark. On the bench beside me sat a woman with half-gray hair, knitting a scarf. After knitting for a while she looked up at the leaves on the tree and said: 'This year's leaves turned red earlier than last year.' I said yes. We didn't continue talking, but that moment of quiet felt very comfortable. An autumn park doesn't need many words — the colors of the leaves, the cool breeze, and people sitting quietly on their benches — that's already enough.

questions.q_zh: 「我」用什么当书签？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' use as a bookmark?
questions.explanation: The key is "One afternoon I was sitting and reading when a yellow" — read it with the answer in mind.

questions.q_zh: 旁边的女士在做什么？
questions.q_en: What was the woman beside doing?
questions.explanation: Focus on "On the bench beside me sat a woman with half-gray" — the rest follows.

title: The Restaurant That Changes with Spring
text_zh: 春天一到，楼下的那家小饭馆就会在门口摆出一块小黑板，上面用粉笔写着这个月的新菜。三月是荠菜馄饨和香椿炒蛋，四月是春笋烧肉。老板说他最喜欢春天，因为春天的食材最丰富也最新鲜。他每个星期都会自己去郊外的农场买菜，回来以后根据买到了什么来决定做什么。所以你永远不知道下次来的时候菜单上会多出什么新菜。有一次他买到了很好的野菜，高兴得不得了，当天晚上做了一道从来没有出现在菜单上的凉拌野菜。他送了一小盘给我，味道清淡但是很香。他说：「好的食材不需要太多调料，简单地做就是对它最大的尊重。」
text_pinyin: Chūntiān yí dào, lóu xià de nà jiā xiǎo fànguǎn jiù huì zài ménkǒu bǎi chū yí kuài xiǎo hēibǎn, shàngmiàn yòng fěnbǐ xiě zhe zhège yuè de xīn cài. Sān yuè shì jìcài húntun hé xiāngchūn chǎo dàn, sì yuè shì chūnsǔn shāo ròu. Lǎobǎn shuō tā zuì xǐhuan chūntiān, yīnwèi chūntiān de shícái zuì fēngfù yě zuì xīnxiān. Tā měi gè xīngqī dōu huì zìjǐ qù jiāowài de nóngchǎng mǎi cài, huílái yǐhòu gēnjù mǎi dào le shénme lái juédìng zuò shénme. Suǒyǐ nǐ yǒngyuǎn bù zhīdào xià cì lái de shíhou càidān shàng huì duō chū shénme xīn cài. Yǒu yí cì tā mǎi dào le hěn hǎo de yěcài, gāoxìng de bùdéliǎo, dāng tiān wǎnshang zuò le yí dào cónglái méiyǒu chūxiàn zài càidān shàng de liángbàn yěcài. Tā sòng le yì xiǎo pán gěi wǒ, wèidào qīngdàn dànshì hěn xiāng. Tā shuō: 「Hǎo de shícái bù xūyào tài duō tiáoliào, jiǎndān de zuò jiù shì duì tā zuì dà de zūnzhòng.」
text_en: As soon as spring arrives, the small restaurant downstairs puts out a little blackboard by the entrance with this month's new dishes written in chalk. March brings shepherd's purse wontons and scrambled eggs with Chinese toon, April has braised pork with spring bamboo shoots. The owner says he loves spring best because the ingredients are richest and freshest. Every week he drives to a farm outside the city to buy produce himself, then decides what to cook based on what he found. So you never know what new dish will appear on the menu next time. Once he found some excellent wild greens and was absolutely delighted — that evening he made a cold wild greens dish that had never appeared on the menu before. He gave me a small plate; the taste was light but fragrant. He said: 'Good ingredients don't need much seasoning. Keeping it simple is the greatest respect you can show them.'

questions.q_zh: 老板怎么决定做什么菜？
questions.q_en: How does the owner decide what to cook?

options.text: 根据从农场买到了什么来决定
options.pinyin: gēnjù cóng nóngchǎng mǎi dào le shénme lái juédìng
options.text_en: based on what he buys from the farm

questions.explanation: "Every week he drives to a farm outside the city" walks you through it.

questions.q_zh: 老板觉得好食材应该怎么做？
questions.q_en: How does the owner think good ingredients should be prepared?
questions.explanation: The method is right there: "Keeping it simple is the greatest respect you can show."

title: The Bicycle Repair Woman
text_zh: 地铁站外面有一个修自行车的阿姨。她每天早上八点准时到，晚上六点收摊。她的摊位很简单，一辆三轮车上放着工具箱和打气筒，旁边撑着一把大伞。我的自行车链条断了，别的地方修要五十块，她只收了十五块。修车的时候她跟我说她以前在工厂上班，工厂关了以后她就开始修自行车。她丈夫教她的——他以前就是干这行的。丈夫去世以后她接过了这个摊位。她说修车不难，难的是一年三百六十五天风吹日晒都在外面。但是她已经习惯了，每天能见到各种各样的人，听到各种各样的故事，日子也不无聊。她把修好的车还给我的时候说：「骑慢一点，链条新换的，要磨合几天。」
text_pinyin: Dìtiě zhàn wàimiàn yǒu yí gè xiū zìxíngchē de āyí. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang bā diǎn zhǔnshí dào, wǎnshang liù diǎn shōu tān. Tā de tānwèi hěn jiǎndān, yí liàng sānlún chē shàng fàng zhe gōngjùxiāng hé dǎqìtǒng, pángbiān chēng zhe yì bǎ dà sǎn. Wǒ de zìxíngchē liàntiáo duàn le, bié de dìfang xiū yào wǔshí kuài, tā zhǐ shōu le shíwǔ kuài. Xiū chē de shíhou tā gēn wǒ shuō tā yǐqián zài gōngchǎng shàng bān, gōngchǎng guān le yǐhòu tā jiù kāishǐ xiū zìxíngchē. Tā zhàngfu jiāo tā de — tā yǐqián jiù shì gàn zhè háng de. Zhàngfu qùshì yǐhòu tā jiē guò le zhège tānwèi. Tā shuō xiū chē bù nán, nán de shì yì nián sān bǎi liùshíwǔ tiān fēngchuī rìshài dōu zài wàimiàn. Dànshì tā yǐjīng xíguàn le, měi tiān néng jiàn dào gè zhǒng gè yàng de rén, tīng dào gè zhǒng gè yàng de gùshi, rìzi yě bù wúliáo. Tā bǎ xiū hǎo de chē huán gěi wǒ de shíhou shuō: 「Qí màn yìdiǎn, liàntiáo xīn huàn de, yào móhé jǐ tiān.」
text_en: Outside the subway station there's a woman who repairs bicycles. She arrives promptly at eight every morning and packs up at six in the evening. Her setup is simple — a tricycle carrying a toolbox and an air pump, with a large umbrella propped up beside it. My bicycle chain broke; other places wanted fifty yuan, but she only charged fifteen. While repairing it, she told me she used to work in a factory. After the factory closed, she started repairing bicycles. Her husband taught her — he used to be in the trade. After he passed away, she took over the stall. She says repairing bikes isn't hard; what's hard is being outside in wind and sun 365 days a year. But she's used to it — meeting all sorts of people and hearing all sorts of stories every day keeps life from being boring. When she returned my repaired bike she said: 'Ride slowly — the chain is newly replaced, it needs a few days to break in.'

questions.q_zh: 阿姨是怎么学会修自行车的？
questions.q_en: How did the woman learn to repair bicycles?

options.pinyin: zìjǐ zài wǎng shàng xué de

questions.explanation: The method is right there: "Her husband taught."

questions.q_zh: 文中的描写方式有什么效果？
questions.q_en: What effect does the descriptive style have?

options.text: 让场景变得立体和生动
options.pinyin: ràng chǎngjǐng biàn de lìtǐ hé shēngdòng
options.text_en: makes the scene vivid and three-dimensional

questions.explanation: "Outside the subway station there's a woman who repairs bicycles" gives you what you need.

title: The Lost Umbrella
text_zh: 我有一把蓝色的折叠伞，用了三年了，伞柄上刻着我的名字。上个星期我在咖啡店忘了拿，回去找的时候已经不见了。我有点难过，虽然只是一把伞，但是用了很久就会有感情。过了几天，我去地铁站的失物招领处问了一下，没想到那把伞真的在那里。工作人员说有人在咖啡店捡到了，看到上面有名字，就送到了最近的失物招领处。伞上还挂着一张小纸条：「希望你的伞能找到回家的路。」我不知道是谁写的，但是看到这句话的时候鼻子有点酸。在一个大城市里，有人愿意为了一把别人的伞多走几步路，这件事本身就很了不起。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu yì bǎ lán sè de zhédié sǎn, yòng le sān nián le, sǎn bǐng shàng kè zhe wǒ de míngzi. Shàng gè xīngqī wǒ zài kāfēi diàn wàng le ná, huíqù zhǎo de shíhou yǐjīng bújiàn le. Wǒ yǒudiǎn nánguò, suīrán zhǐ shì yì bǎ sǎn, dànshì yòng le hěn jiǔ jiù huì yǒu gǎnqíng. Guò le jǐ tiān, wǒ qù dìtiě zhàn de shīwù zhāolǐng chù wèn le yíxià, méi xiǎng dào nà bǎ sǎn zhēn de zài nàlǐ. Gōngzuò rényuán shuō yǒu rén zài kāfēi diàn jiǎn dào le, kàn dào shàngmiàn yǒu míngzi, jiù sòng dào le zuìjìn de shīwù zhāolǐng chù. Sǎn shàng hái guà zhe yì zhāng xiǎo zhǐtiáo: 「Xīwàng nǐ de sǎn néng zhǎo dào huí jiā de lù.」 Wǒ bù zhīdào shì shéi xiě de, dànshì kàn dào zhè jù huà de shíhou bízi yǒudiǎn suān. Zài yí gè dà chéngshì lǐ, yǒu rén yuànyì wèile yì bǎ biéren de sǎn duō zǒu jǐ bù lù, zhè jiàn shì běnshēn jiù hěn liǎobuqǐ.
text_en: I have a blue folding umbrella that I've used for three years, with my name engraved on the handle. Last week I forgot it at a coffee shop, and when I went back to look, it was gone. I was a little sad — even though it's just an umbrella, you develop feelings for something you've used so long. A few days later, I asked at the subway station's lost and found, and to my surprise, the umbrella was actually there. The staff said someone found it at the coffee shop, saw the name on it, and brought it to the nearest lost and found. There was even a small note hanging from it: 'I hope your umbrella finds its way home.' I don't know who wrote it, but when I read those words my nose tingled a bit. In a big city, someone willing to walk a few extra steps for another person's umbrella — that in itself is quite remarkable.

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: The emotion comes through in choices like "I have a blue folding umbrella that I've used."

questions.q_zh: 纸条上写了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the note say?

options.text: 希望你的伞能找到回家的路
options.pinyin: xīwàng nǐ de sǎn néng zhǎo dào huí jiā de lù
options.text_en: I hope your umbrella finds its way home

options.pinyin: qǐng dào kāfēi diàn lái qǔ
options.text_en: please come pick it up at the coffee shop

questions.explanation: "I hope your umbrella finds its way home.' I don" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

title: The Notice on the Bulletin Board
text_zh: 今天经过小区公告栏的时候，我看到了一张特别的通知。上面用大字写着：「本周六下午三点，楼下空地，社区电影放映。欢迎大家带椅子来看。」下面还画了一个笑脸。我想起小时候在农村老家，夏天的晚上大家会在院子里看露天电影。那时候的感觉是那么开心——天上有星星，旁边有邻居，银幕上是好看的故事。周六我真的搬了一把椅子下去了。来的人比我想象的多，有老人有孩子，有人还带了瓜子和水果。放映的是一部很老的电影，画面有点模糊，但是大家看得很高兴。那天晚上回家的时候，我觉得自己好像真的住在一个「社区」里了，而不只是住在一栋楼里。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān jīngguò xiǎoqū gōnggào lán de shíhou, wǒ kàn dào le yì zhāng tèbié de tōngzhī. Shàngmiàn yòng dà zì xiě zhe: 「Běn zhōuliù xiàwǔ sān diǎn, lóu xià kòngdì, shèqū diànyǐng fàngyìng. Huānyíng dàjiā dài yǐzi lái kàn.」 Xiàmiàn hái huà le yí gè xiàoliǎn. Wǒ xiǎng qǐ xiǎo shíhou zài nóngcūn lǎojiā, xiàtiān de wǎnshang dàjiā huì zài yuànzi lǐ kàn lùtiān diànyǐng. Nà shíhou de gǎnjué shì nàme kāixīn — tiān shàng yǒu xīngxing, pángbiān yǒu línjū, yínmù shàng shì hǎokàn de gùshi. Zhōuliù wǒ zhēn de bān le yì bǎ yǐzi xiàqù le. Lái de rén bǐ wǒ xiǎngxiàng de duō, yǒu lǎorén yǒu háizi, yǒu rén hái dài le guāzi hé shuǐguǒ. Fàngyìng de shì yí bù hěn lǎo de diànyǐng, huàmiàn yǒudiǎn móhu, dànshì dàjiā kàn de hěn gāoxìng. Nà tiān wǎnshang huí jiā de shíhou, wǒ juéde zìjǐ hǎoxiàng zhēn de zhù zài yí gè「shèqū」lǐ le, ér bù zhǐ shì zhù zài yí dòng lóu lǐ.
text_en: Today when I passed the compound's bulletin board, I noticed a special announcement. In big characters it read: 'This Saturday at 3 PM, open space downstairs, community movie screening. Everyone welcome — bring a chair.' Below it was a smiley face. I remembered my childhood in the countryside, when on summer evenings everyone would watch outdoor movies in the courtyard. The feeling then was so joyful — stars overhead, neighbors beside you, a good story on the screen. On Saturday I actually carried a chair downstairs. More people came than I'd imagined — elderly, children, some even bringing sunflower seeds and fruit. They screened a old movie with slightly blurry images, but everyone enjoyed it. Walking home that evening, I felt like I truly lived in a 'community,' not just in a building.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: "Today when I passed the compound's bulletin board" — the emotional temperature is right there.

questions.q_zh: 看完电影以后「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel after watching the movie?

options.text_en: thought the movie was too old

options.text: 觉得自己真的住在一个社区里了
options.pinyin: juéde zìjǐ zhēn de zhù zài yí gè shèqū lǐ le
options.text_en: felt like truly living in a community
wanted to move to the countryside

questions.explanation: Read "I felt like I truly lived in a 'community,' not" slowly — the feeling is in the phrasing.

title: The Last Train Stranger
text_zh: 末班地铁上，坐在我对面的是一个穿着护士服的年轻女孩。她看起来非常疲倦，眼圈有点黑，但是手里拿着一个三明治在认真地吃。我猜她大概是刚下夜班。吃完三明治以后她从包里拿出一本很厚的书开始看。我注意到那是一本考试用的参考书——也许她在准备什么考试。地铁到了一个站，上来了一个抱着孩子的妈妈。孩子一直在哭，妈妈看起来也很累。那个护士把自己的座位让给了那位妈妈，然后站到了车门旁边继续看书。孩子慢慢不哭了，在妈妈怀里睡着了。我看着这个安静的画面想，这座城市的深夜，处处都有温柔的人在安静地努力着。
text_pinyin: Mòbān dìtiě shàng, zuò zài wǒ duìmiàn de shì yí gè chuān zhe hùshi fú de niánqīng nǚhái. Tā kàn qǐlái fēicháng píjuàn, yǎnquān yǒudiǎn hēi, dànshì shǒu lǐ ná zhe yí gè sānmíngzhì zài rènzhēn de chī. Wǒ cāi tā dàgài shì gāng xià yèbān. Chī wán sānmíngzhì yǐhòu tā cóng bāo lǐ ná chū yì běn hěn hòu de shū kāishǐ kàn. Wǒ zhùyì dào nà shì yì běn kǎoshì yòng de cānkǎo shū — yěxǔ tā zài zhǔnbèi shénme kǎoshì. Dìtiě dào le yí gè zhàn, shàng lái le yí gè bào zhe háizi de māma. Háizi yìzhí zài kū, māma kàn qǐlái yě hěn lèi. Nàge hùshi bǎ zìjǐ de zuòwèi ràng gěi le nà wèi māma, ránhòu zhàn dào le chē mén pángbiān jìxù kàn shū. Háizi mànmàn bù kū le, zài māma huái lǐ shuì zháo le. Wǒ kàn zhe zhège ānjìng de huàmiàn xiǎng, zhè zuò chéngshì de shēnyè, chùchù dōu yǒu wēnróu de rén zài ānjìng de nǔlì zhe.
text_en: On the last subway train, sitting across from me was a young woman in a nurse's uniform. She looked extremely tired, with slight dark circles under her eyes, but she was earnestly eating a sandwich. I guessed she'd just come off a night shift. After finishing the sandwich, she pulled a thick book from her bag and started reading. I noticed it was a test preparation reference book — perhaps she's studying for an exam. The train reached a station, and a mother holding a crying child got on. The child kept crying, and the mother looked exhausted too. The nurse gave her seat to the mother, then stood by the door and continued reading. The child gradually stopped crying and fell asleep in the mother's arms. Looking at this quiet scene, I thought: in the depths of this city's night, gentle people everywhere are quietly working hard.

questions.q_zh: 这个选择反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: "On the last subway train, sitting across from me" gives you what you need.

questions.q_zh: 这个选择反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: "On the last subway train, sitting across from me" gives you what you need.

title: The Dawn Bakery Queue
text_zh: 街角面包店早上五点半开门，但是五点就已经有人在排队了。最早来的总是那个遛狗的大爷，他每天带着他的小狗来买一个全麦面包。小狗很乖，安安静静地坐在他脚边等。第二个到的一般是对面花店的老板娘，她每天要早起开店进货，顺便买两个牛角面包当早餐。我是第三个常客，六点左右到。我们三个人每天早上都会见面，但是说的话很少，最多就是「早」和「今天天气不错」。面包师傅有时候会多做几个小饼干，免费送给排队的人。大爷总是把他那份饼干掰一半给小狗吃。清晨的面包店门口，这些简单的、重复的小事情，组成了一种让人安心的日常。
text_pinyin: Jiējiǎo miànbāo diàn zǎoshang wǔ diǎn bàn kāi mén, dànshì wǔ diǎn jiù yǐjīng yǒu rén zài páiduì le. Zuì zǎo lái de zǒng shì nàge liú gǒu de dàye, tā měi tiān dài zhe tā de xiǎo gǒu lái mǎi yí gè quánmài miànbāo. Xiǎo gǒu hěn guāi, ān'ānjìngjìng de zuò zài tā jiǎo biān děng. Dì èr gè dào de yìbān shì duìmiàn huādiàn de lǎobǎnniáng, tā měi tiān yào zǎo qǐ kāi diàn jìn huò, shùnbiàn mǎi liǎng gè niújiǎo miànbāo dāng zǎocān. Wǒ shì dì sān gè chángkè, liù diǎn zuǒyòu dào. Wǒmen sān gè rén měi tiān zǎoshang dōu huì jiànmiàn, dànshì shuō de huà hěn shǎo, zuìduō jiù shì「zǎo」hé「jīntiān tiānqì búcuò」. Miànbāo shīfu yǒu shíhou huì duō zuò jǐ gè xiǎo bǐnggān, miǎnfèi sòng gěi páiduì de rén. Dàye zǒng shì bǎ tā nà fèn bǐnggān bāi yí bàn gěi xiǎo gǒu chī. Qīngchén de miànbāo diàn ménkǒu, zhèxiē jiǎndān de, chóngfù de xiǎo shìqing, zǔchéng le yì zhǒng ràng rén ānxīn de rìcháng.
text_en: The corner bakery opens at five-thirty, but people are already queuing by five. The first to arrive is always the old man who walks his dog — he comes every day with his small dog to buy a whole wheat loaf. The dog is well-behaved, sitting quietly at his feet. The second to arrive is usually the flower shop owner from across the street, who gets up early to open shop and stock inventory, picking up two croissants for breakfast along the way. I'm the third regular, arriving around six. The three of us see each other every morning but rarely say much — at most 'morning' and 'nice weather today.' The baker sometimes makes a few extra cookies and gives them free to people in line. The old man always breaks his cookie in half for the dog. In front of the bakery at dawn, these simple, repeated little things form a kind of reassuring daily rhythm.

questions.q_zh: 环境描写和人物心理之间有什么关系？
questions.q_en: What is the relationship between the setting and the character's psychology?

options.text_en: the environment mirrors the inner state

questions.explanation: Focus on "The corner bakery opens at five-thirty, but people are already" — the rest follows.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以推断人物是什么样的人？
questions.q_en: What kind of person can we infer the character is?
questions.explanation: Details like "In front of the bakery at dawn, these simple, repeated" accumulate into this larger meaning.

title: The Sound of Rain on the Window
text_zh: 今天晚上下了一场小雨。我关了灯，躺在床上听窗户外面的雨声。雨滴打在玻璃上的声音很轻，像有人在用手指轻轻敲门。楼下偶尔传来一两辆车经过水坑的声音，哗的一下，然后又安静了。远处好像有人在关窗户，发出咔嗒一声。我想起小时候，下雨天妈妈总是说「下雨了，今天早点睡」。那时候我不喜欢下雨，因为下雨就不能出去玩。但是现在我很喜欢雨天的晚上——世界变得安静下来，所有的声音都被雨包裹住了，变得柔和、模糊。雨声是最好的催眠曲，它不需要旋律，只需要一种均匀的、不断的、温柔的节奏。听着听着，我慢慢地睡着了。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān wǎnshang xià le yì chǎng xiǎo yǔ. Wǒ guān le dēng, tǎng zài chuáng shàng tīng chuānghu wàimiàn de yǔ shēng. Yǔdī dǎ zài bōlí shàng de shēngyīn hěn qīng, xiàng yǒu rén zài yòng shǒuzhǐ qīngqīng qiāo mén. Lóu xià ǒu'ěr chuán lái yì liǎng liàng chē jīngguò shuǐkēng de shēngyīn, huá de yíxià, ránhòu yòu ānjìng le. Yuǎnchù hǎoxiàng yǒu rén zài guān chuānghu, fā chū kādā yì shēng. Wǒ xiǎng qǐ xiǎo shíhou, xià yǔ tiān māma zǒng shì shuō「xià yǔ le, jīntiān zǎo diǎn shuì」. Nà shíhou wǒ bù xǐhuan xià yǔ, yīnwèi xià yǔ jiù bù néng chūqù wán. Dànshì xiànzài wǒ hěn xǐhuan yǔ tiān de wǎnshang — shìjiè biàn de ānjìng xiàlái, suǒyǒu de shēngyīn dōu bèi yǔ bāoguǒ zhù le, biàn de róuhé, móhu. Yǔ shēng shì zuì hǎo de cuīmián qǔ, tā bù xūyào xuánlǜ, zhǐ xūyào yì zhǒng jūnyún de, búduàn de, wēnróu de jiézòu. Tīng zhe tīng zhe, wǒ mànmàn de shuì zháo le.
text_en: Tonight a light rain fell. I turned off the lights and lay in bed listening to the rain outside the window. The sound of raindrops hitting the glass was very soft, like someone gently tapping on a door with their fingers. Occasionally from downstairs came the sound of a car or two splashing through a puddle — a quick swoosh, then quiet again. In the distance, someone seemed to be closing a window with a click. I remembered how as a child, on rainy days my mother would always say, 'It's raining — go to sleep early tonight.' Back then I didn't like rain because it meant I couldn't go out to play. But now I love rainy evenings — the world grows quiet, all sounds wrapped in rain, becoming soft and blurred. The sound of rain is the best lullaby. It doesn't need a melody, just an even, continuous, gentle rhythm. Listening and listening, I slowly fell asleep.

questions.q_zh: 这段话会让不同的人有不同的理解吗？
questions.q_en: Would different people interpret this passage differently?

options.text: 是的，因为涉及个人经历
options.pinyin: shì de, yīnwèi shèjí gèrén jīnglì
options.text_en: yes, because it involves personal experience

options.pinyin: zhǐ yǒu zhuānjiā cái néng lǐjiě

questions.explanation: Go back to "I remembered how as a child, on rainy days." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: "I remembered how as a child, on rainy days" pulls double duty — scene-setting and meaning.

title: The Night Market Lanterns
text_zh: 夜市入口挂着两排红色的灯笼，从街头一直延伸到街尾。到了晚上，灯笼亮起来的时候，整条街都被染成了暖暖的橙色。我最喜欢在灯笼下面慢慢地走，看两边的小摊一个一个地亮起来。有一个卖灯笼的老人，他的摊位上挂满了各种各样的手工灯笼——有圆的、方的、兔子形状的、鱼形状的。他说这些灯笼都是他自己做的，用竹子和纸，方法是他爷爷教他的。现在会做这种灯笼的人越来越少了。我买了一个小鱼灯笼带回家，挂在阳台上。晚上开灯的时候，橙色的光透过薄薄的纸照出来，像夜市的那种温暖又安静的光。
text_pinyin: Yèshì rùkǒu guà zhe liǎng pái hóng sè de dēnglóng, cóng jiētóu yìzhí yánshēn dào jiēwěi. Dào le wǎnshang, dēnglóng liàng qǐlái de shíhou, zhěng tiáo jiē dōu bèi rǎn chéng le nuǎnnuǎn de chéng sè. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan zài dēnglóng xiàmiàn mànmàn de zǒu, kàn liǎng biān de xiǎo tān yí gè yí gè de liàng qǐlái. Yǒu yí gè mài dēnglóng de lǎorén, tā de tānwèi shàng guà mǎn le gè zhǒng gè yàng de shǒugōng dēnglóng — yǒu yuán de, fāng de, tùzi xíngzhuàng de, yú xíngzhuàng de. Tā shuō zhèxiē dēnglóng dōu shì tā zìjǐ zuò de, yòng zhúzi hé zhǐ, fāngfǎ shì tā yéye jiāo tā de. Xiànzài huì zuò zhè zhǒng dēnglóng de rén yuè lái yuè shǎo le. Wǒ mǎi le yí gè xiǎo yú dēnglóng dài huí jiā, guà zài yángtái shàng. Wǎnshang kāi dēng de shíhou, chéng sè de guāng tòu guò báobáo de zhǐ zhào chūlái, xiàng yèshì de nà zhǒng wēnnuǎn yòu ānjìng de guāng.
text_en: Two rows of red lanterns hang at the night market entrance, stretching from one end of the street to the other. When they light up at night, the whole street is bathed in a warm orange glow. I love walking slowly beneath the lanterns, watching the stalls on both sides light up one by one. There's an old man who sells lanterns — his stall is covered with all sorts of handmade lanterns: round ones, square ones, rabbit-shaped, fish-shaped. He says he makes them all himself using bamboo and paper, a method his grandfather taught him. Fewer and fewer people know how to make these lanterns now. I bought a small fish lantern to take home and hung it on the balcony. When I turn on the light at night, the orange glow shines through the thin paper, like the warm and quiet light of the night market.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以看出什么样的关系？
questions.q_en: What kind of relationship does this passage reveal?
questions.explanation: "Two rows of red lanterns hang at the night market" — once you spot this, the answer clicks.

questions.q_zh: 「我」买了什么形状的灯笼？
questions.q_en: What shape lantern did 'I' buy?
questions.explanation: Go back to "I bought a small fish lantern to take home." That's where the answer lives.

title: The Laundry Room at Midnight
text_zh: 加班到半夜回家以后，我才想起来衣服还在洗衣机里。我只好穿着拖鞋去地下室的洗衣房。走廊里的灯有一半是坏的，忽明忽暗的。推开洗衣房的门，里面只有洗衣机运转的嗡嗡声。我把衣服放进烘干机，然后坐在旁边的塑料椅子上等。洗衣房里有一股温暖的、混合着洗衣液和热空气的味道。我不知道为什么，在这个安静的、嗡嗡响的小房间里，我突然觉得很放松。白天的压力和疲倦好像被烘干机的热风慢慢地吹走了。四十分钟以后，衣服烘好了，热热的、软软的。我抱着一堆暖和的衣服上楼，觉得今天最好的时刻不是什么了不起的事情，而是午夜洗衣房里那四十分钟的安静。
text_pinyin: Jiābān dào bànyè huí jiā yǐhòu, wǒ cái xiǎng qǐlái yīfu hái zài xǐyījī lǐ. Wǒ zhǐhǎo chuān zhe tuōxié qù dìxiàshì de xǐyīfáng. Zǒuláng lǐ de dēng yǒu yí bàn shì huài de, hū míng hū àn de. Tuī kāi xǐyīfáng de mén, lǐmiàn zhǐ yǒu xǐyījī yùnzhuǎn de wēngwēng shēng. Wǒ bǎ yīfu fàng jìn hōnggānjī, ránhòu zuò zài pángbiān de sùliào yǐzi shàng děng. Xǐyīfáng lǐ yǒu yì gǔ wēnnuǎn de, hùnhé zhe xǐyīyè hé rè kōngqì de wèidào. Wǒ bù zhīdào wèi shénme, zài zhège ānjìng de, wēngwēng xiǎng de xiǎo fángjiān lǐ, wǒ tūrán juéde hěn fàngsōng. Báitiān de yālì hé píjuàn hǎoxiàng bèi hōnggānjī de rè fēng mànmàn de chuī zǒu le. Sìshí fēnzhōng yǐhòu, yīfu hōng hǎo le, rèrè de, ruǎnruǎn de. Wǒ bào zhe yì duī nuǎnhuo de yīfu shàng lóu, juéde jīntiān zuì hǎo de shíkè búshì shénme liǎobuqǐ de shìqing, ér shì wǔyè xǐyīfáng lǐ nà sìshí fēnzhōng de ānjìng.
text_en: After working overtime until midnight and coming home, I suddenly remembered my clothes were still in the washing machine. I had no choice but to go down to the basement laundry room in my slippers. Half the corridor lights were broken, flickering on and off. Pushing open the laundry room door, the only sound was the humming of the washing machine. I put the clothes in the dryer and sat in a plastic chair to wait. The laundry room had a warm smell of detergent mixed with hot air. I don't know why, but in this quiet, humming little room, I suddenly felt very relaxed. The stress and fatigue of the day seemed to be slowly blown away by the dryer's warm air. Forty minutes later, the clothes were done — warm and soft. I carried a pile of warm clothes upstairs and felt that the best moment of my day wasn't anything remarkable, but those forty minutes of quiet in the midnight laundry room.

questions.q_zh: 走廊里的灯怎么了？
questions.q_en: What was wrong with the corridor lights?

options.text: 有一半是坏的，忽明忽暗
options.pinyin: yǒu yí bàn shì huài de, hū míng hū àn

questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "Half the corridor lights were broken, flickering on and off."

questions.q_en: What view of work attitude does this passage present?

options.text_en: work is less important than life

questions.explanation: Focus on "After working overtime until midnight and coming home" — the rest follows.

title: The Park Bench Letter
text_zh: 今天在公园的长椅上，我发现了一封用信封装着的信。信封上没有写收件人的名字，只写了一句话：「给坐在这把椅子上的你。」我打开信，里面是一张手写的纸条。上面写着：「你好，陌生人。如果你正在看这封信，说明今天的天气不错，你也许在这里坐了一会儿。我希望你今天过得开心。如果不太开心也没关系，明天会好的。——一个跟你一样喜欢这把椅子的人。」我读完以后笑了。我不知道是谁写的，也许是一个很温柔的人，也许是一个心情好的人随手写的。但是不管怎样，一个陌生人的善意确实让我这个普通的下午变得特别了。我把信放回了原来的地方，希望下一个坐在这里的人也能看到。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān zài gōngyuán de chángyǐ shàng, wǒ fāxiàn le yì fēng yòng xìnfēng zhuāng zhe de xìn. Xìnfēng shàng méiyǒu xiě shōujiàn rén de míngzi, zhǐ xiě le yí jù huà: 「Gěi zuò zài zhè bǎ yǐzi shàng de nǐ.」 Wǒ dǎkāi xìn, lǐmiàn shì yì zhāng shǒuxiě de zhǐtiáo. Shàngmiàn xiě zhe: 「Nǐ hǎo, mòshēng rén. Rúguǒ nǐ zhèngzài kàn zhè fēng xìn, shuōmíng jīntiān de tiānqì búcuò, nǐ yěxǔ zài zhèlǐ zuò le yíhuìr. Wǒ xīwàng nǐ jīntiān guò de kāixīn. Rúguǒ bú tài kāixīn yě méi guānxi, míngtiān huì hǎo de. —— Yí gè gēn nǐ yíyàng xǐhuan zhè bǎ yǐzi de rén.」 Wǒ dú wán yǐhòu xiào le. Wǒ bù zhīdào shì shéi xiě de, yěxǔ shì yí gè hěn wēnróu de rén, yěxǔ shì yí gè xīnqíng hǎo de rén suíshǒu xiě de. Dànshì bùguǎn zěnyàng, yí gè mòshēng rén de shànyì quèshí ràng wǒ zhège pǔtōng de xiàwǔ biàn de tèbié le. Wǒ bǎ xìn fàng huí le yuánlái de dìfang, xīwàng xià yí gè zuò zài zhèlǐ de rén yě néng kàn dào.
text_en: Today on a park bench, I found a letter in an envelope. There was no recipient's name on it, just one line: 'For you, sitting on this bench.' I opened it and inside was a handwritten note. It read: 'Hello, stranger. If you're reading this letter, the weather must be nice today, and perhaps you've been sitting here for a while. I hope you're having a good day. If it's not so great, that's okay too — tomorrow will be better. — Someone who likes this bench just as much as you do.' I smiled after reading it. I don't know who wrote it — perhaps a very gentle person, or someone in a good mood writing on a whim. But regardless, a stranger's kindness really did make my ordinary afternoon special. I put the letter back where I found it, hoping the next person who sits here will see it too.

questions.q_zh: 信封上写了什么？

options.text_en: for you, sitting on this bench

questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "For you, sitting on this bench."

questions.q_en: What did 'I' do after reading the letter?

options.pinyin: bǎ xìn dài huí jiā le
bǎ xìn fàng huí yuánlái de dìfang
options.text_en: put the letter back where it was
