title: The Autumn Coffee Menu
text_zh: 九月一到，我常去的那家咖啡店就换上了秋天的菜单。夏天的冰咖啡和水果茶不见了，换成了热的南瓜拿铁、肉桂拿铁和桂花乌龙茶。咖啡师还在门口放了一块小黑板，上面用粉笔画了几片红叶。我点了一杯南瓜拿铁，坐在靠窗的位置。杯子是暖暖的，拿在手里特别舒服。窗外的银杏树叶子已经开始变黄了，偶尔有一片飘下来。我想起去年秋天也是在这个位置，喝的也是南瓜拿铁。一年过得真快。但是有些东西不会变——每年秋天，南瓜拿铁会回来，银杏叶会变黄，我会坐在同一个位置，感受同一种安静的幸福。
text_pinyin: Jiǔ yuè yí dào, wǒ cháng qù de nà jiā kāfēi diàn jiù huàn shàng le qiūtiān de càidān. Xiàtiān de bīng kāfēi hé shuǐguǒ chá bújiàn le, huàn chéng le rè de nánguā ná tiě, ròuguì ná tiě hé guìhuā wūlóng chá. Kāfēi shī hái zài ménkǒu fàng le yí kuài xiǎo hēibǎn, shàngmiàn yòng fěnbǐ huà le jǐ piàn hóng yè. Wǒ diǎn le yì bēi nánguā ná tiě, zuò zài kào chuāng de wèizhì. Bēizi shì nuǎnnuǎn de, ná zài shǒu lǐ tèbié shūfu. Chuāng wài de yínxìng shù yèzi yǐjīng kāishǐ biàn huáng le, ǒu'ěr yǒu yí piàn piāo xiàlái. Wǒ xiǎng qǐ qùnián qiūtiān yě shì zài zhège wèizhì, hē de yě shì nánguā ná tiě. Yì nián guò de zhēn kuài. Dànshì yǒuxiē dōngxi bú huì biàn — měi nián qiūtiān, nánguā ná tiě huì huílái, yínxìng yè huì biàn huáng, wǒ huì zuò zài tóng yí gè wèizhì, gǎnshòu tóng yì zhǒng ānjìng de xìngfú. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì jìjié de yìyì: tā tíxǐng nǐ, hǎo de dōngxi huì xúnhuán huílái.
text_en: As soon as September arrives, the coffee shop I frequent switches to its autumn menu. The summer iced coffee and fruit tea disappear, replaced by hot pumpkin latte, cinnamon latte, and osmanthus oolong tea. The barista also placed a small blackboard at the entrance with a few red leaves drawn in chalk. I ordered a pumpkin latte and sat by the window. The cup was warm, comfortable to hold. Outside the window, the ginkgo tree leaves had already started turning yellow, one occasionally drifting down. I remembered that last autumn I sat in this same spot, also drinking a pumpkin latte. A year goes by so fast. But some things don't change — every autumn, the pumpkin latte returns, the ginkgo leaves turn yellow, and I sit in the same spot, feeling the same quiet happiness.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: "As soon as September arrives, the coffee shop I frequent" quietly shapes how you read everything else.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: "As soon as September arrives, the coffee shop I frequent" quietly shapes how you read everything else.

title: The Bicycle Doctor
text_zh: 我骑着自行车上班的路上，后轮突然没气了。我推着车走了好一会儿，终于在一个小巷子里找到了一家修车店。店很小，门口贴着一张纸：「修车·补胎·打气——老赵」。老赵是一个头发花白的老人，正坐在一把小凳子上喝茶。他看了看我的车说：「扎了一个钉子，五分钟就好。」他动作很快，先把轮胎拆下来，找到了那个小洞，用胶水和橡胶片补好了，然后又打满气。他一边干活一边说：「现在的年轻人都不会修车了。以前谁家的自行车坏了，自己就能修。」我说现在大家都骑共享单车了。他叹了口气说：「共享单车坏了，没人心疼。自己的车坏了，才会想办法修好它。」他说得对，我们越来越不习惯修理东西了，总觉得换一个新的更方便。
text_pinyin: Wǒ qí zhe zìxíngchē shàng bān de lù shàng, hòu lún tūrán méi qì le. Wǒ tuī zhe chē zǒu le hǎo yíhuìr, zhōngyú zài yí gè xiǎo xiàngzi lǐ zhǎo dào le yì jiā xiū chē diàn. Diàn hěn xiǎo, ménkǒu tiē zhe yì zhāng zhǐ: 「Xiū chē · bǔ tāi · dǎ qì — Lǎo Zhào」. Lǎo Zhào shì yí gè tóufa huābái de lǎorén, zhèng zuò zài yì bǎ xiǎo dèngzi shàng hē chá. Tā kàn le kàn wǒ de chē shuō: 「Zhā le yí gè dīngzi, wǔ fēnzhōng jiù hǎo.」 Tā dòngzuò hěn kuài, xiān bǎ lúntāi chāi xiàlái, zhǎo dào le nàge xiǎo dòng, yòng jiāoshuǐ hé xiàngjiāo piàn bǔ hǎo le, ránhòu yòu dǎ mǎn qì. Tā yìbiān gàn huó yìbiān shuō: 「Xiànzài de niánqīng rén dōu bú huì xiū chē le. Yǐqián shéi jiā de zìxíngchē huài le, zìjǐ jiù néng xiū.」 Wǒ shuō xiànzài dàjiā dōu qí gòngxiǎng dānchē le. Tā tàn le kǒu qì shuō: 「Gòngxiǎng dānchē huài le, méi rén xīnténg. Zìjǐ de chē huài le, cái huì xiǎng bànfǎ xiū hǎo tā.」 Tā shuō de duì, wǒmen yuè lái yuè bù xíguàn xiūlǐ dōngxi le, zǒng juéde huàn yí gè xīn de gèng fāngbiàn.
text_en: On my way to work by bicycle, my back tire suddenly went flat. I pushed the bike for a while before finally finding a repair shop in a small alley. The shop was tiny, with a paper notice at the door: 'Repairs, patch tires, pump air — Old Zhao.' Old Zhao was a gray-haired man sitting on a small stool drinking tea. He looked at my bike and said: 'Ran over a nail. Five minutes.' He worked quickly — removed the tire, found the small hole, patched it with glue and a rubber strip, then pumped it full of air. While working he said: 'Young people these days can't repair bikes anymore. In the old days, if your bike broke, you'd fix it yourself.' I said people mostly ride bike-shares now. He sighed and said: 'When a shared bike breaks, nobody cares.

questions.q_zh: 车胎为什么没气了？
questions.q_en: Why did the tire go flat?
questions.explanation: Once you read "He looked at my bike and said: 'Ran over," the cause clicks.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: The mood lives in specifics like "On my way to work by bicycle, my back tire."

title: The Lost Glove
text_zh: 冬天的地铁站里，经常能看到一只孤零零的手套被放在座位上或者栏杆上。有人捡到以后会把它放在一个显眼的地方，希望主人回来找的时候能看到。我今天在站台的长椅上看到了一只深红色的毛线手套。它看起来是手织的，织得不太均匀，但是很暖和的样子。我把它拿起来放在了椅背上更容易看到的地方。我想，织这只手套的人一定花了不少时间。也许是一个奶奶为孙子织的，也许是一个女朋友为男朋友织的。丢手套的人回来找了吗？我不知道。但是那只手套在椅背上等了一个下午，从我到站的时候一直到我离开的时候。也许它还在那里等着。有些东西虽然小，但是里面装着很多看不见的心意。
text_pinyin: Dōngtiān de dìtiě zhàn lǐ, jīngcháng néng kàn dào yì zhī gūlínglíng de shǒutào bèi fàng zài zuòwèi shàng huòzhě lángān shàng. Yǒu rén jiǎn dào yǐhòu huì bǎ tā fàng zài yí gè xiǎnyǎn de dìfang, xīwàng zhǔrén huílái zhǎo de shíhou néng kàn dào. Wǒ jīntiān zài zhàntái de chángyǐ shàng kàn dào le yì zhī shēn hóng sè de máoxiàn shǒutào. Tā kàn qǐlái shì shǒu zhī de, zhī de bú tài jūnyún, dànshì hěn nuǎnhuo de yàngzi. Wǒ bǎ tā ná qǐlái fàng zài le yǐ bèi shàng gèng róngyì kàn dào de dìfang. Wǒ xiǎng, zhī zhè zhī shǒutào de rén yídìng huā le bù shǎo shíjiān. Yěxǔ shì yí gè nǎinai wèi sūnzi zhī de, yěxǔ shì yí gè nǚ péngyou wèi nán péngyou zhī de. Diū shǒutào de rén huílái zhǎo le ma? Wǒ bù zhīdào. Dànshì nà zhī shǒutào zài yǐ bèi shàng děng le yí gè xiàwǔ, cóng wǒ dào zhàn de shíhou yìzhí dào wǒ líkāi de shíhou. Yěxǔ tā hái zài nàlǐ děng zhe. Yǒuxiē dōngxi suīrán xiǎo, dànshì lǐmiàn zhuāng zhe hěn duō kàn bu jiàn de xīnyì.
text_en: In subway stations during winter, you often see a lone glove placed on a seat or a railing. Whoever picks it up puts it somewhere visible, hoping the owner will spot it when they come back. Today on the platform bench I saw a dark red knitted glove. It looked handmade — the stitching wasn't perfectly even, but it seemed very warm. I picked it up and placed it on the back of the chair where it would be easier to see. I thought whoever knit this glove must have spent quite some time. Maybe a grandmother knit it for her grandchild, or a girlfriend for her boyfriend. Did the person who lost it come back to look? I don't know. But the glove waited on the chair back for an entire afternoon, from when I arrived until I left. Perhaps it's still waiting there. Some things are small, but they hold a lot of invisible care inside.

questions.q_zh: 那只手套是什么样的？
questions.q_en: What was the glove like?

options.text: 手织的毛线手套，织得不太均匀
options.pinyin: shǒu zhī de máoxiàn shǒutào, zhī de bú tài jūnyún

questions.explanation: Go back to "In subway stations during winter, you often see a lone." That's where the answer lives.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: "Did the person who lost it come back to look?" gives you what you need.

title: The Bulletin Board in Winter
text_zh: 冬天的公告栏跟别的季节不太一样。夏天的时候上面贴的大多是活动通知和广告，但是一到冬天，多了很多暖心的小纸条。有人写：「楼下的流浪猫怕冷，我在花园里放了几个纸箱子，如果你有不穿的旧衣服，可以放在箱子里给猫当被子。」还有人写：「家里做了太多饺子，吃不完，放在一楼走廊的桌子上了，欢迎邻居们拿。」最让我感动的是一张用彩色笔写的纸条：「王奶奶，天冷了，出门记得戴帽子。——你的四楼邻居。」我不认识王奶奶，也不认识四楼的邻居，但是看到这种小纸条会觉得这个冬天没那么冷。公告栏上的纸条会被风吹走，但是这些温暖的心意不会。
text_pinyin: Dōngtiān de gōnggào lán gēn bié de jìjié bú tài yíyàng. Xiàtiān de shíhou shàngmiàn tiē de dà duō shì huódòng tōngzhī hé guǎnggào, dànshì yí dào dōngtiān, duō le hěn duō nuǎn xīn de xiǎo zhǐtiáo. Yǒu rén xiě: 「Lóu xià de liúlàng māo pà lěng, wǒ zài huāyuán lǐ fàng le jǐ gè zhǐ xiāngzi, rúguǒ nǐ yǒu bù chuān de jiù yīfu, kěyǐ fàng zài xiāngzi lǐ gěi māo dāng bèizi.」 Hái yǒu rén xiě: 「Jiā lǐ zuò le tài duō jiǎozi, chī bu wán, fàng zài yī lóu zǒuláng de zhuōzi shàng le, huānyíng línjūmen ná.」 Zuì ràng wǒ gǎndòng de shì yì zhāng yòng cǎisè bǐ xiě de zhǐtiáo: 「Wáng nǎinai, tiān lěng le, chū mén jìde dài màozi. —— Nǐ de sì lóu línjū.」 Wǒ bú rènshi Wáng nǎinai, yě bú rènshi sì lóu de línjū, dànshì kàn dào zhè zhǒng xiǎo zhǐtiáo huì juéde zhège dōngtiān méi nàme lěng. Gōnggào lán shàng de zhǐtiáo huì bèi fēng chuī zǒu, dànshì zhèxiē wēnnuǎn de xīnyì bú huì.
text_en: The bulletin board in winter is different from other seasons. In summer it's mostly activity notices and advertisements, but once winter comes, there are many more heartwarming little notes. Someone wrote: 'The stray cats downstairs are cold. I put some cardboard boxes in the garden — if you have old clothes you don't wear, you can put them in the boxes as blankets for the cats.' Another wrote: 'Made too many dumplings at home, can't finish them. Left them on the table in the first-floor hallway — neighbors welcome to take some.' The most moving was a note written in colored pen: 'Grandma Wang, it's gotten cold — remember to wear a hat when you go out. — Your 4th floor neighbor.' I don't know Grandma Wang, and I don't know the fourth-floor neighbor, but seeing notes like these makes this winter feel less cold. Notes on the bulletin board may blow away in the wind, but the warmth behind them won't.

questions.q_zh: 有人在花园里放纸箱子是为了什么？
questions.q_en: Why did someone put cardboard boxes in the garden?
questions.explanation: Pay attention to "Made too many dumplings at home, can" — it answers the why.

questions.q_zh: 四楼邻居提醒王奶奶做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the 4th floor neighbor remind Grandma Wang to do?

options.text_en: remember to wear a hat when going out

questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "Grandma Wang."

title: The Last Train Musician
text_zh: 末班地铁的车厢里，一个年轻人坐在角落里轻轻地弹着吉他。声音很小，只有离他很近的人才能听到。他弹的是一首我不认识的曲子，旋律很简单，但是很好听——有一种让人安静下来的力量。车厢里只有五六个人，大家都很累，有的在打瞌睡，有的在看手机。但是我注意到，几个人慢慢地放下了手机，开始安静地听。弹吉他的年轻人戴着一顶毛线帽子，眼睛看着窗外的黑夜。他好像不是在为谁弹，而是为他自己弹。一首曲子弹完以后，他安静了一会儿，又开始弹下一首。到站的时候他背起吉他走了，消失在站台的人群里。我想，有些音乐不需要舞台和观众，一节深夜的车厢就够了。
text_pinyin: Mòbān dìtiě de chēxiāng lǐ, yí gè niánqīng rén zuò zài jiǎoluò lǐ qīngqīng de tán zhe jítā. Shēngyīn hěn xiǎo, zhǐ yǒu lí tā hěn jìn de rén cái néng tīng dào. Tā tán de shì yì shǒu wǒ bú rènshi de qǔzi, xuánlǜ hěn jiǎndān, dànshì hěn hǎotīng — yǒu yì zhǒng ràng rén ānjìng xiàlái de lìliang. Chēxiāng lǐ zhǐ yǒu wǔ liù gè rén, dàjiā dōu hěn lèi, yǒu de zài dǎ kēshuì, yǒu de zài kàn shǒujī. Dànshì wǒ zhùyì dào, jǐ gè rén mànmàn de fàng xià le shǒujī, kāishǐ ānjìng de tīng. Tán jítā de niánqīng rén dài zhe yì dǐng máoxiàn màozi, yǎnjing kàn zhe chuāng wài de hēiyè. Tā hǎoxiàng búshì zài wèi shéi tán, ér shì wèi tā zìjǐ tán. Yì shǒu qǔzi tán wán yǐhòu, tā ānjìng le yíhuìr, yòu kāishǐ tán xià yì shǒu. Dào zhàn de shíhou tā bēi qǐ jítā zǒu le, xiāoshī zài zhàntái de rénqún lǐ. Wǒ xiǎng, yǒuxiē yīnyuè bù xūyào wǔtái hé guānzhòng, yì jié shēnyè de chēxiāng jiù gòu le.
text_en: In the last subway car, a young man sat in the corner softly playing a guitar. The sound was so quiet that only those very close could hear. He played a song I didn't recognize — a simple melody, but beautiful, with a power to quiet people down. There were only five or six people in the car, all tired — some dozing, some on their phones. But I noticed a few people gradually put down their phones and began to listen quietly. The guitarist wore a knit hat, eyes watching the dark night outside the window. He seemed not to be playing for anyone, but for himself. After finishing one piece, he paused for a moment, then began another. When he reached his stop, he slung his guitar over his back and disappeared into the platform crowd. I think some music doesn't need a stage or an audience — a late-night subway car is enough.

questions.q_zh: 吉他声音大吗？
questions.q_en: Was the guitar loud?

options.text: 很大声，整个车厢都能听到
options.pinyin: hěn dà shēng, zhěnggè chēxiāng dōu néng tīng dào
options.text_en: very loud, the whole car could hear

options.text: 很小，只有离他近的人才能听到
options.pinyin: hěn xiǎo, zhǐ yǒu lí tā jìn de rén cái néng tīng dào
options.text_en: very quiet, only those close could hear

questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "The sound was so quiet that only those very close."

questions.q_zh: 听到音乐以后，有些乘客做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did some passengers do after hearing the music?

options.pinyin: fàng xià shǒujī ānjìng de tīng
options.text_en: put down phones and listened quietly

questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "I noticed a few people gradually put down their phones."

title: The Bakery's First Customer
text_zh: 面包店的师傅告诉我，他最喜欢早上第一个客人。不是因为那个人买得最多，而是因为那是他一天里跟外面世界的第一次接触。他说凌晨三点到五点之间，整个店里只有他一个人，和面、发面、烤面包，安安静静的。这段时间他跟世界是分开的，像在一个温暖的泡泡里面。然后五点半开门的时候，第一个客人推开门走进来，带着外面冷冷的空气和早晨的味道，泡泡就破了。他说这个时刻让他觉得很特别——一天真正开始了。我说：「那我努力做你的第一个客人吧。」他笑了笑说：「不用努力，你来的时候来就好。面包不会跑的。」
text_pinyin: Miànbāo diàn de shīfu gàosu wǒ, tā zuì xǐhuan zǎoshang dì yī gè kèrén. Búshì yīnwèi nàge rén mǎi de zuì duō, ér shì yīnwèi nà shì tā yì tiān lǐ gēn wàimiàn shìjiè de dì yī cì jiēchù. Tā shuō língchén sān diǎn dào wǔ diǎn zhījiān, zhěnggè diàn lǐ zhǐ yǒu tā yí gè rén, hé miàn, fā miàn, kǎo miànbāo, ān'ānjìngjìng de. Zhè duàn shíjiān tā gēn shìjiè shì fēnkāi de, xiàng zài yí gè wēnnuǎn de pàopao lǐmiàn. Ránhòu wǔ diǎn bàn kāi mén de shíhou, dì yī gè kèrén tuī kāi mén zǒu jìnlái, dài zhe wàimiàn lěnglěng de kōngqì hé zǎochen de wèidào, pàopao jiù pò le. Tā shuō zhège shíkè ràng tā juéde hěn tèbié — yì tiān zhēnzhèng kāishǐ le. Wǒ shuō: 「Nà wǒ nǔlì zuò nǐ de dì yī gè kèrén ba.」 Tā xiào le xiào shuō: 「Búyòng nǔlì, nǐ lái de shíhou lái jiù hǎo. Miànbāo bú huì pǎo de.」
text_en: The baker told me his favorite person is the first customer of the morning. Not because they buy the most, but because that's his first contact with the outside world each day. He says between 3 and 5 AM, he's alone in the shop — mixing dough, letting it rise, baking bread, all in quiet. During this time he's separated from the world, as if inside a warm bubble. Then at 5:30 when the door opens, the first customer pushes in, bringing the cold outside air and the smell of morning, and the bubble pops. He says that moment feels very special — the day has truly begun. I said: 'Then I'll try to be your first customer.' He smiled and said: 'No need to try. Come when you come. The bread won't run away.'

questions.q_zh: 师傅为什么最喜欢第一个客人？
questions.q_en: Why does the baker like the first customer most?

options.text: 因为那是他跟外面世界的第一次接触
options.pinyin: yīnwèi nà shì tā gēn wàimiàn shìjiè de dì yī cì jiēchù
options.text_en: because it's his first contact with the outside world

options.text: 因为那个人总是很友好
options.pinyin: yīnwèi nàge rén zǒng shì hěn yǒuhǎo
options.text_en: because that person is always friendly

questions.explanation: "Not because they buy the most, but because that's" gives away the reason without spelling it out.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: Moments like "The baker told me his favorite person is the first" reach beyond the page.

title: The Courtyard Between Buildings
text_zh: 我们两栋楼之间有一个小院子，面积不大，但是种了一棵很老的梧桐树。春天的时候，梧桐树长出新叶子，嫩绿色的，阳光透过叶子照下来，地上全是一块一块的光斑。夏天的时候，树荫特别大，楼下的老人喜欢搬椅子到树下面打牌。秋天的时候，落叶把整个院子铺满了，踩上去沙沙地响。冬天树叶掉光了，可以从楼上看到天空和远处的山。我很喜欢这棵树，因为它让我记住了时间的变化。在城市里，很多东西都是不变的——马路还是那条马路，楼还是那栋楼。但是这棵树不一样，它一年四季都在变，提醒我日子在一天一天地过。有些最安静的东西，反而最能让你感受到生活的流动。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen liǎng dòng lóu zhījiān yǒu yí gè xiǎo yuànzi, miànjī bú dà, dànshì zhòng le yì kē hěn lǎo de wútóng shù. Chūntiān de shíhou, wútóng shù zhǎng chū xīn yèzi, nèn lǜ sè de, yángguāng tòu guò yèzi zhào xiàlái, dì shàng quán shì yí kuài yí kuài de guāng bān. Xiàtiān de shíhou, shùyīn tèbié dà, lóu xià de lǎorén xǐhuan bān yǐzi dào shù xiàmiàn dǎ pái. Qiūtiān de shíhou, luòyè bǎ zhěnggè yuànzi pū mǎn le, cǎi shàngqù shāshā de xiǎng. Dōngtiān shùyè diào guāng le, kěyǐ cóng lóu shàng kàn dào tiānkōng hé yuǎnchù de shān. Wǒ hěn xǐhuan zhè kē shù, yīnwèi tā ràng wǒ jì zhù le shíjiān de biànhuà. Zài chéngshì lǐ, hěn duō dōngxi dōu shì bú biàn de — mǎlù háishì nà tiáo mǎlù, lóu háishì nà dòng lóu. Dànshì zhè kē shù bù yíyàng, tā yì nián sì jì dōu zài biàn, tíxǐng wǒ rìzi zài yì tiān yì tiān de guò. Yǒuxiē zuì ānjìng de dōngxi, fǎn'ér zuì néng ràng nǐ gǎnshòu dào shēnghuó de liúdòng.
text_en: Between our two buildings there's a small courtyard, not big, but with a old sycamore tree planted in it. In spring, the tree sprouts new leaves — a tender green — and sunlight filtering through creates dappled patterns on the ground. In summer, the shade is enormous, and the elderly downstairs like to bring chairs under the tree to play cards. In autumn, fallen leaves blanket the entire courtyard, rustling when you step on them. In winter, the leaves are all gone, and from upstairs you can see the sky and distant mountains. I love this tree because it helps me remember the passage of time. In a city, many things don't change — the road is still that road, the building still that building. But this tree is different. It changes through all four seasons, reminding me that the days are passing one by one. Some of the quietest things are actually what best let you feel life flowing.

questions.q_zh: 夏天的时候老人在树下做什么？
questions.q_en: What do elderly people do under the tree in summer?
questions.explanation: The answer sits right in "In summer, the shade is enormous, and the elderly downstairs."

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么喜欢这棵树？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' like this tree?

options.text: 因为它让我记住了时间的变化
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā ràng wǒ jì zhù le shíjiān de biànhuà
options.text_en: because it helps me remember the passage of time

options.pinyin: yīnwèi kěyǐ zài shù xià chéng liáng
options.text_en: because you can cool off under it

questions.explanation: It comes down to "I love this tree because it helps me remember" — cause and effect.
